title: Task 5: Implement Orim's Chant card definition
design:
## Goal
Create the Orim's Chant card definition file that uses the kicker and restriction systems built in previous tasks.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Context
Foundation complete:
- Task 1: Player grants system (:player/grants attribute, add/get/expire)
- Task 2: :add-restriction effect type (creates restriction grants on players)
- Task 3: cannot-cast-spells enforcement in can-cast?
- Task 4: Kicker casting modes (get-casting-modes returns :primary and :kicked)

Now we need the actual card that ties it all together.

## Implementation

### Reference: Existing Patterns
- Targeting format: See src/fizzle/cards/recoup.cljs:20-25 for :card/targeting structure
- Effects: See src/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:531-570 for :add-restriction effect
- Kicker: See src/test/fizzle/engine/rules_test.cljs for kicker test card pattern

### Card Definition
Create src/fizzle/cards/orims_chant.cljs:

```clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.orims-chant
  "Orim's Chant card definition.

   Orim's Chant: W - Instant
   Kicker {W}
   Target player can't cast spells this turn.
   If this spell was kicked, creatures can't attack this turn.")


(def orims-chant
  {:card/id :orims-chant
   :card/name "Orim's Chant"
   :card/cmc 1
   :card/mana-cost {:white 1}
   :card/colors #{:white}
   :card/types #{:instant}
   :card/text "Kicker {W}. Target player can't cast spells this turn. If this spell was kicked, creatures can't attack this turn."
   
   ;; Kicker cost - enables kicked mode via get-casting-modes
   :card/kicker {:white 1}
   
   ;; Cast-time targeting: target any player
   ;; IMPORTANT: Must use :card/targeting (vector format), NOT :card/targets
   ;; See recoup.cljs:20-25 for pattern
   :card/targeting [{:target/id :player
                     :target/type :player
                     :target/options #{:any-player}
                     :target/required true}]
   
   ;; Base effects (when not kicked) - :targeted-player resolves via :object/targets
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :add-restriction
                   :effect/target :targeted-player
                   :restriction/type :cannot-cast-spells}]
   
   ;; Kicked effects (replaces base effects when kicked)
   ;; Must include BOTH restrictions (kicked effects replace, not add to base)
   :card/kicked-effects [{:effect/type :add-restriction
                          :effect/target :targeted-player
                          :restriction/type :cannot-cast-spells}
                         {:effect/type :add-restriction
                          :effect/target :targeted-player
                          :restriction/type :cannot-attack}]})
```

### Test File
Create src/test/fizzle/cards/orims_chant_test.cljs:

```clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.orims-chant-test
  (:require
    [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest is testing]]
    [fizzle.cards.orims-chant :as orims-chant]
    [fizzle.engine.rules :as rules]
    [fizzle.engine.grants :as grants]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
    [fizzle.test-helpers :as h]))

;; Test 1: Card properties are oracle-accurate
(deftest card-properties-test
  (testing "card has correct oracle properties"
    (let [card orims-chant/orims-chant]
      (is (= :orims-chant (:card/id card)))
      (is (= "Orim's Chant" (:card/name card)))
      (is (= 1 (:card/cmc card)))
      (is (= {:white 1} (:card/mana-cost card)))
      (is (= #{:white} (:card/colors card)))
      (is (= #{:instant} (:card/types card))))))

;; Test 2: Kicker cost defined correctly
;; Bug caught: Missing kicker breaks kicked mode generation
(deftest kicker-cost-test
  (testing "card has kicker cost defined"
    (let [card orims-chant/orims-chant]
      (is (= {:white 1} (:card/kicker card))
          "Kicker cost should be {W}"))))

;; Test 3: Targeting system integration
;; Bug caught: Wrong attribute (:card/targets vs :card/targeting)
(deftest targeting-format-test
  (testing "card uses correct targeting format for targeting system"
    (let [card orims-chant/orims-chant
          targeting (:card/targeting card)]
      (is (vector? targeting)
          "Targeting must be a vector for targeting system")
      (is (= 1 (count targeting))
          "Should have exactly one target requirement")
      (let [req (first targeting)]
        (is (= :player (:target/id req))
            "Target ID should be :player")
        (is (= :player (:target/type req))
            "Target type should be :player")
        (is (contains? (:target/options req) :any-player)
            "Should allow targeting any player")
        (is (:target/required req)
            "Target should be required")))))

;; Test 4: Base effects have cannot-cast-spells
;; Bug caught: Missing restriction type in base effects
(deftest base-effects-test
  (testing "base effects add cannot-cast-spells restriction"
    (let [card orims-chant/orims-chant
          effects (:card/effects card)]
      (is (= 1 (count effects))
          "Should have exactly one base effect")
      (let [effect (first effects)]
        (is (= :add-restriction (:effect/type effect)))
        (is (= :targeted-player (:effect/target effect)))
        (is (= :cannot-cast-spells (:restriction/type effect)))))))

;; Test 5: Kicked effects have both restrictions
;; Bug caught: Kicked effects missing one restriction
(deftest kicked-effects-test
  (testing "kicked effects add both restrictions"
    (let [card orims-chant/orims-chant
          effects (:card/kicked-effects card)]
      (is (= 2 (count effects))
          "Should have two kicked effects")
      (is (= #{:cannot-cast-spells :cannot-attack}
             (set (map :restriction/type effects)))
          "Should include both restriction types"))))

;; Test 6: get-casting-modes returns 2 modes in hand
;; Bug caught: Kicker mode not generated
(deftest casting-modes-test
  (testing "get-casting-modes returns primary and kicked modes"
    (let [db (h/init-game-with-card orims-chant/orims-chant :hand)
          player-id :player-1
          hand (q/get-hand db player-id)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))
          modes (rules/get-casting-modes db player-id object-id)]
      (is (= 2 (count modes))
          "Should return 2 casting modes")
      (is (some #(= :primary (:mode/id %)) modes)
          "Should include primary mode")
      (is (some #(= :kicked (:mode/id %)) modes)
          "Should include kicked mode"))))

;; Test 7: Primary mode costs {W}
;; Bug caught: Primary mode has wrong cost
(deftest primary-mode-cost-test
  (testing "primary mode costs {W}"
    (let [db (h/init-game-with-card orims-chant/orims-chant :hand)
          modes (rules/get-casting-modes db :player-1 (:object/id (first (q/get-hand db :player-1))))
          primary (first (filter #(= :primary (:mode/id %)) modes))]
      (is (= {:white 1} (:mode/mana-cost primary))
          "Primary mode should cost {W}"))))

;; Test 8: Kicked mode costs {WW}
;; Bug caught: Kicked mode cost not merged correctly
(deftest kicked-mode-cost-test
  (testing "kicked mode costs {WW}"
    (let [db (h/init-game-with-card orims-chant/orims-chant :hand)
          modes (rules/get-casting-modes db :player-1 (:object/id (first (q/get-hand db :player-1))))
          kicked (first (filter #(= :kicked (:mode/id %)) modes))]
      (is (= {:white 2} (:mode/mana-cost kicked))
          "Kicked mode should cost {WW} (base + kicker)"))))

;; Test 9: Kicked mode has effects in mode
;; Bug caught: Kicked effects not attached to mode
(deftest kicked-mode-effects-test
  (testing "kicked mode carries kicked effects"
    (let [db (h/init-game-with-card orims-chant/orims-chant :hand)
          modes (rules/get-casting-modes db :player-1 (:object/id (first (q/get-hand db :player-1))))
          kicked (first (filter #(= :kicked (:mode/id %)) modes))]
      (is (seq (:mode/effects kicked))
          "Kicked mode should have effects")
      (is (= 2 (count (:mode/effects kicked)))
          "Kicked mode should have 2 effects"))))
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Card file exists at src/fizzle/cards/orims_chant.cljs
- [ ] Card text matches Scryfall oracle: "Kicker {W}. Target player can't cast spells this turn. If this spell was kicked, creatures can't attack this turn."
- [ ] :card/kicker is {:white 1}
- [ ] :card/targeting is a VECTOR with {:target/id :player, :target/type :player, :target/options #{:any-player}, :target/required true}
- [ ] :card/effects has one :add-restriction with :cannot-cast-spells
- [ ] :card/kicked-effects has two :add-restriction effects (both restriction types)
- [ ] 9+ unit tests pass in orims_chant_test.cljs
- [ ] make test passes (all existing tests still work)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations (Edge Cases)

**Edge Case: Wrong targeting attribute**
- MUST use :card/targeting (vector format), NOT :card/targets
- :card/targets does not exist in the targeting system
- Incorrect attribute will cause targeting system to not find targets

**Edge Case: Kicked effects replace, not extend**
- :card/kicked-effects completely replaces :card/effects when kicked
- Kicked effects MUST include the base effect (cannot-cast-spells)
- Forgetting this means kicked spell only prevents attacks, not casting

**Edge Case: Target options format**
- :target/options must be a set (#{:any-player}), not a vector
- Targeting system uses (contains? options ...) which requires set

**Edge Case: No valid targets**
- If no opponent exists (goldfish mode), still need a target
- :any-player allows targeting self, so spell is castable
- targeting/has-valid-targets? returns [player-id] when :any-player set

**Reference: Effect target resolution**
- :targeted-player in effect resolves via (get-in source-obj [:object/targets :player])
- This works because targeting stores targets keyed by :target/id
- Since :target/id is :player, stored at [:object/targets :player]

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO using :card/targets (wrong attribute - must use :card/targeting)
- ❌ NO hardcoded logic in rules.cljs (card uses generic systems)
- ❌ NO custom effect types (use existing :add-restriction)
- ❌ NO skipping player targeting (oracle says "target player")
- ❌ NO forgetting cannot-cast-spells in kicked effects (replace semantics)
- ❌ NO using vector for :target/options (must be set for contains? check)
close_reason: Implemented Orim's Chant card definition with kicker and player targeting. All 12 tests pass, make validate passes.

title: Task 4: Remove legacy trigger system and unify remaining duplicate code
design:
## Goal
Remove all legacy trigger entity code paths and unify remaining duplicate code. After this task, the codebase has ONE stack system (stack-items only), ONE query for the full stack, and ZERO -for-trigger builder functions.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Context
- Tasks 1-3 complete: most trigger creation sites now produce stack-items
- **EXCEPTION**: maybe-create-storm-trigger in rules.cljs still calls triggers/create-trigger + add-trigger-to-stack (missed in Task 3)
- Legacy trigger branches remain in resolve-top, resolve-top-of-stack, confirmation handlers
- 2 -for-trigger builder functions remain
- get-next-stack-order is duplicated (queries.cljs + stack.cljs)
- resolve-stack-item-ability-with-selection already uses spell builders with dissoc/assoc patching (no migration needed there)

## Implementation

### Step 1: Migrate maybe-create-storm-trigger to stack-items (rules.cljs)

**CRITICAL**: This is the last production caller of triggers/create-trigger + add-trigger-to-stack.

File: src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs, function maybe-create-storm-trigger (~lines 309-330)

Currently creates a :storm trigger entity that later resolves via the :storm defmethod in triggers.cljs, which calls create-spell-copy to create N stack-item copies.

Replace with direct stack-item creation that stores the storm copy count:

```clojure
;; OLD:
(let [trigger (triggers/create-trigger :storm object-id player-id {:count copy-count})]
  (triggers/add-trigger-to-stack db trigger))

;; NEW:
(stack/create-stack-item db
  {:stack-item/type :storm
   :stack-item/controller player-id
   :stack-item/source object-id
   :stack-item/effects []
   :stack-item/description (str "Storm - create " copy-count " copies")
   :stack-item/storm-count copy-count})
```

**Schema addition needed**: Add :stack-item/storm-count to schema.cljs (or reuse :stack-item/effects to encode count — but a dedicated attribute is clearer).

Wait — simpler approach: encode storm count in the existing :stack-item/effects as data:
```clojure
:stack-item/effects [{:effect/type :storm-copies :effect/count copy-count}]
```
This avoids schema additions and follows the existing pattern where effects are data.

**Then update resolve-stack-item-effects in game.cljs** to handle :storm type stack-items by calling triggers/create-spell-copy N times (the same logic currently in the :storm resolve-trigger defmethod).

Add require for stack in rules.cljs: [fizzle.engine.stack :as stack]
Remove require for triggers in rules.cljs IF no other trigger functions are called there. Check first — rules.cljs may still call resolve-trigger for immediate actions.

### Step 2: Update ::resolve-top to handle :storm stack-items

File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

The resolve-stack-item-effects function (or a new branch in ::resolve-top) needs to handle :storm type stack-items. When the top stack item is type :storm:
1. Get source-id and copy-count from stack-item
2. Call triggers/create-spell-copy N times (this function already creates stack-items for copies)
3. Remove the storm stack-item

This is similar to the existing Branch 3 (other stack-items) but with storm-specific logic.

### Step 3: Remove legacy trigger branches from ::resolve-top (game.cljs)

Lines ~442-455 have 2 legacy branches:
- Branch 4: activated-ability trigger with selection
- Branch 5: other legacy triggers

Remove these branches and the legacy trigger variables:
- Remove: stack-triggers, top-trigger, trigger-order, resolve-trigger? variables
- Remove: Branch 4 and Branch 5 cond clauses
- Keep: Branch 1 (spell stack-item), Branch 2 (activated-ability stack-item), Branch 3 (other stack-items including storm), Branch 6 (empty)

Also remove the queries/get-stack-items require if no longer used.

### Step 4: Remove legacy trigger branch from resolve-top-of-stack (game.cljs)

Lines ~261-308, remove:
- queries/get-stack-items call
- top-trigger, trigger-order, resolve-trigger? variables
- Legacy trigger cond branch (lines ~302-305)
- Keep: stack-item branches and empty stack branch

### Step 5: Remove resolve-top-trigger helper (game.cljs)

Line ~237: Dead code — defined but never called. Delete entirely.

### Step 6: Remove resolve-activated-ability-with-selection (selection.cljs)

Lines ~702-834: Legacy function that takes trigger maps. Only caller was Branch 4 in ::resolve-top (removed in Step 3). Delete entirely (~132 lines).

### Step 7: Remove -for-trigger builder functions (selection.cljs)

After Step 6, these have zero callers:
- build-tutor-selection-for-trigger (lines ~665-685, ~20 lines)
- build-player-target-selection-for-trigger (lines ~688-699, ~12 lines)

Note: resolve-stack-item-ability-with-selection already uses the spell builders (build-tutor-selection, build-player-target-selection) with dissoc/assoc patching. No migration needed — just delete the unused trigger versions.

### Step 8: Remove :trigger source-type from cleanup-selection-source (selection.cljs)

Lines ~841-866: Remove the (= source-type :trigger) branch (lines ~854-855). Only :stack-item and spell (nil) remain.

Also remove all occurrences of :selection/trigger-id setting/reading across selection.cljs (found at lines ~677-678, ~696-697, ~770-771, ~827-828, ~1502-1503, ~1537-1538). These are all in functions being deleted (Steps 6-7) or dead paths.

### Step 9: Simplify stack-empty? (queries.cljs)

Lines ~142-149: Currently checks legacy triggers + objects + stack-items. Remove the get-stack-items check. Check only:
- stack-items: (d/q '[:find ?e . :where [?e :stack-item/position _]] db)
- objects on stack: (get-objects-in-zone db player-id :stack)

### Step 10: Remove get-stack-items from queries.cljs

Lines ~130-139: No callers after Steps 3-4 and 9. Delete entirely.

### Step 11: Consolidate get-next-stack-order

queries.cljs has get-next-stack-order (~lines 113-127) checking triggers + objects.
stack.cljs has get-next-stack-order (~lines 12-31) checking stack-items + triggers + objects.

Simplify stack.cljs version to check only stack-items + objects (remove trigger query). Remove the queries.cljs version — its only remaining caller was add-trigger-to-stack (removed in Step 13).

Verify no other callers of queries/get-next-stack-order exist first.

### Step 12: Remove trigger creation functions from triggers.cljs

- create-trigger (lines ~16-37): 0 production callers after Step 1
- add-trigger-to-stack (lines ~40-57): 0 production callers after Step 1

Delete both. Do NOT remove:
- resolve-trigger multimethod and its defmethods (still used by trigger_dispatch for immediate triggers: :draw-step, :untap-step, :expire-grants, :permanent-tapped, :land-entered)
- remove-trigger (may still be needed — verify no callers remain first)
- create-spell-copy (called during storm resolution)

### Step 13: Remove :trigger/* schema from schema.cljs

Lines ~82-91: Remove all 7 trigger attributes:
- :trigger/id, :trigger/type, :trigger/source, :trigger/controller
- :trigger/data, :trigger/description, :trigger/stack-order

**Edge case**: After schema removal, any existing trigger entities in a running game become invisible to Datascript queries. This is acceptable — new games won't create them, and page refresh starts a new game.

**Important**: resolve-trigger defmethods still accept trigger MAPS as parameters (plain Clojure maps, not Datascript entities). The :trigger/* schema only affects Datascript storage. Immediate triggers (draw-step, untap-step, expire-grants) are created as maps and passed directly to resolve-trigger without ever being stored in Datascript. So schema removal is safe.

Wait — verify this: do :permanent-tapped and :land-entered triggers get stored in Datascript? After Task 3, trigger_dispatch creates stack-items for stacked triggers. But the immediate triggers (uses-stack? false) are resolved directly via resolve-trigger. They never use add-trigger-to-stack. So they're plain maps, not Datascript entities. Schema removal is safe.

### Step 14: Extract split-effects-around-selection helper (selection.cljs)

The subvec splitting pattern appears ~8 times:
```clojure
(subvec (vec effects-list) 0 idx)
(nth effects-list idx)
(subvec (vec effects-list) (inc idx))
```

Extract into:
```clojure
(defn- split-effects-around-index
  "Split effects list around the effect at idx.
   Returns [effects-before target-effect effects-after]."
  [effects idx]
  (let [v (vec effects)]
    [(subvec v 0 idx)
     (nth v idx)
     (subvec v (inc idx))]))
```

Replace all ~8 inline occurrences with destructuring:
```clojure
(let [[effects-before selection-effect effects-after]
      (split-effects-around-index effects-list selection-idx)]
  ...)
```

### Step 15: Update tests

Specific changes needed:

**src/test/fizzle/engine/triggers_test.cljs** (21 calls to create-trigger):
- KEEP tests for resolve-trigger defmethods (:permanent-tapped, :land-entered, :activated-ability, :draw-step, :untap-step, :expire-grants) — these test still-active production code
- These tests create trigger maps directly and pass to resolve-trigger — this is fine, they don't need Datascript storage
- REMOVE tests for create-trigger and add-trigger-to-stack functions (being deleted)
- REMOVE tests that assert trigger entities exist in Datascript after add-trigger-to-stack

**src/test/fizzle/engine/stack_test.cljs** (4 calls):
- Check what these tests do — if they test stack-item interaction with legacy triggers, update to only use stack-items

**src/test/fizzle/engine/storm_test.cljs** (2 calls):
- Update storm tests to verify storm creates stack-items (not trigger entities)
- Test: cast spell with :storm keyword → stack-item of type :storm created → resolves into N copy stack-items

**Add new test**: test-storm-creates-stack-item-not-trigger — verify maybe-create-storm-trigger creates a :storm stack-item, not a trigger entity.

### Step 16: Verify and commit

- make validate must pass
- Count LOC reduction (target: 300+ net)
- Verify single Datalog query for entire stack (stack/get-all-stack-items)
- Verify 0 :trigger/* attributes in schema
- Verify 0 -for-trigger suffixed functions
- grep -r 'trigger/id\|trigger/type\|trigger/stack-order' src/main/ should return 0 hits in schema and entity-creating code (resolve-trigger defmethods still use trigger map keys, which is fine)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] 0 branches in ::resolve-top for legacy triggers (was 2)
- [ ] 0 branches in resolve-top-of-stack for legacy triggers (was 1)
- [ ] maybe-create-storm-trigger creates stack-item, not trigger entity
- [ ] ::resolve-top handles :storm stack-items correctly (creates N copies)
- [ ] resolve-activated-ability-with-selection deleted (~132 lines)
- [ ] build-tutor-selection-for-trigger deleted (~20 lines)
- [ ] build-player-target-selection-for-trigger deleted (~12 lines)
- [ ] :trigger/* schema attributes removed (7 attributes)
- [ ] create-trigger and add-trigger-to-stack deleted from triggers.cljs
- [ ] resolve-trigger defmethods PRESERVED (still used for immediate triggers)
- [ ] queries/get-stack-items deleted
- [ ] get-next-stack-order exists in exactly one place (stack.cljs), checks only stack-items + objects
- [ ] split-effects-around-index helper extracts duplicated splitting logic (~8 sites)
- [ ] stack-empty? simplified (no trigger check)
- [ ] No -for-trigger suffixed functions exist
- [ ] 0 occurrences of :selection/trigger-id in codebase
- [ ] All tests pass (856+)
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] Net LOC reduction documented

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO leaving dead code commented out (delete completely)
- NO keeping trigger schema 'just in case'
- NO breaking resolve-trigger defmethods (:draw-step, :untap-step, :expire-grants, :permanent-tapped, :land-entered — still called by trigger_dispatch for immediate resolution)
- NO touching resolve-spell-with-selection (spell resolution path is separate and working)
- NO removing create-spell-copy from triggers.cljs (still actively used by storm resolution)
- NO removing remove-trigger before verifying zero callers remain
- NO adding :stack-item/storm-count to schema — use :stack-item/effects with {:effect/type :storm-copies :effect/count N} instead

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Storm trigger migration**
maybe-create-storm-trigger is the LAST production caller of triggers/create-trigger. Must be migrated (Step 1) BEFORE removing trigger creation functions (Step 12). The :storm resolve-trigger defmethod becomes dead code after Step 2 wires storm resolution through stack-items.

**Edge Case: Immediate vs stacked triggers**
After all changes, trigger_dispatch still calls resolve-trigger for immediate triggers (uses-stack? false). These pass plain Clojure maps to resolve-trigger — they never stored entities in Datascript. Schema removal (Step 13) does NOT affect them.

**Edge Case: remove-trigger after cleanup**
After Steps 3-4 remove legacy branches from resolve-top, and Step 8 removes :trigger source from cleanup-selection-source, verify remove-trigger has zero callers. If so, it can be deleted alongside create-trigger in Step 12.

**Edge Case: Storm count encoding**
Storm count encoded as {:effect/type :storm-copies :effect/count N} in :stack-item/effects. Resolution code must extract this — not use the standard effect execution path (it's not an effect the engine knows about).

**Edge Case: triggers_test.cljs test preservation**
Tests for resolve-trigger defmethods create trigger maps inline (not via create-trigger necessarily). Verify which tests actually call create-trigger vs which construct maps manually. Only remove tests that test deleted functions.

**Ordering dependency**: Steps must execute in this order:
1 → 2 (storm migration before removal)
3-5 (remove legacy branches, dead code)
6-8 (remove legacy selection code)
9-11 (remove legacy queries and consolidate)
12-13 (remove trigger creation and schema — LAST)
14 (refactoring — independent of above)
15-16 (tests and verify — final)
close_reason: All 16 steps complete. Legacy trigger system removed, stack unified. 831 tests pass, make validate clean. ~330 LOC net reduction.

title: Implement flashback system and Deep Analysis
design:
## Goal
Implement the flashback mechanic and Deep Analysis card to complete Phase 5 of the epic.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Context
Completed: Tutor system (Merchant Scroll), Land-entered triggers (City of Traitors), Fetchlands (Polluted Delta), Event system migration (City of Brass). Missing: Flashback system and Deep Analysis card.

## Implementation Checklist

### Step 1: Add flashback cost property schema
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs
- Add :card/flashback-cost property to card definitions
- Structure: {:mana {:blue 1 :colorless 1} :life 3} for Deep Analysis
- Reference: Study existing cost structures in lion's-eye-diamond, polluted-delta

### Step 2: Extend can-cast? for graveyard zone
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs
- Current code (line 28): `(= :hand (:object/zone obj))` - hardcoded hand check
- Extend to: Check hand OR (graveyard AND has flashback-cost)
- When in graveyard with flashback: use :card/flashback-cost for mana check
- When in graveyard with flashback: check life cost if present (player life >= cost)
```clojure
;; Pseudocode for can-cast? extension:
(let [zone (:object/zone obj)
      has-flashback (:card/flashback-cost card)]
  (cond
    (= :hand zone) (mana/can-pay? db player-id (:card/mana-cost card))
    (and (= :graveyard zone) has-flashback)
    (let [fb-cost (:card/flashback-cost card)]
      (and (mana/can-pay? db player-id (:mana fb-cost))
           (or (nil? (:life fb-cost))
               (>= (q/get-player-life db player-id) (:life fb-cost)))))
    :else false))
```

### Step 3: Extend cast-spell for flashback
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs
- Detect if casting from graveyard (check :object/zone before cast)
- If graveyard + has flashback: pay flashback cost (mana + life), not normal cost
- If flashback has life cost: deduct life using existing drain/life-loss pattern
- Mark object with :object/cast-via-flashback true for resolution tracking
```clojure
;; Add to object before moving to stack:
[:db/add obj-eid :object/cast-via-flashback true]
```

### Step 4: Handle exile-on-resolution in resolve-spell
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs (resolve-spell, lines 127-149)
- Current code sends instants/sorceries to :graveyard (line 144)
- Add check: if :object/cast-via-flashback true, destination = :exile
- This is a replacement effect - applies even if spell would otherwise go to graveyard
```clojure
;; Modified destination logic:
(let [cast-via-flashback (:object/cast-via-flashback obj)
      destination (cond
                    cast-via-flashback :exile
                    (permanent-type? card-types) :battlefield
                    :else :graveyard)]
  ...)
```

### Step 5: Implement Deep Analysis card
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs
- Card ID: :deep-analysis
- Name: "Deep Analysis"
- Mana cost: {:colorless 3 :blue 1} (normal cast)
- Flashback cost: {:mana {:colorless 1 :blue 1} :life 3}
- Type: #{:sorcery}
- Colors: #{:blue}
- Effect: Draw 2 cards (targeting caster only for simplicity)
  - Use existing :draw effect type
  - Note: Original card says "Target player draws two cards and loses 2 life"
  - For Iggy Pop practice, self-target only is acceptable (always draws for caster)
- Life loss is PART OF THE EFFECT (2 life on normal cast), not the cost
- Flashback life cost (3 life) is ADDITIONAL to the effect's 2 life loss
```clojure
(def deep-analysis
  {:card/id :deep-analysis
   :card/name "Deep Analysis"
   :card/cmc 4
   :card/mana-cost {:colorless 3 :blue 1}
   :card/flashback-cost {:mana {:colorless 1 :blue 1} :life 3}
   :card/colors #{:blue}
   :card/types #{:sorcery}
   :card/text "Target player draws two cards and loses 2 life. Flashback {1}{U}, Pay 3 life."
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :draw :effect/amount 2}
                  {:effect/type :drain :effect/amount 2}]}) ;; drain targets self = lose life
```

### Step 6: Add flashback tests
File: src/test/fizzle/cards/deep_analysis_test.cljs (NEW FILE)
Reference: src/test/fizzle/cards/merchant_scroll_test.cljs for test patterns

Tests to implement:
1. test-deep-analysis-in-hand-castable-with-mana
   - Card in hand, player has 3UU mana pool
   - can-cast? returns true
   - Bug caught: Ensures normal casting path still works

2. test-deep-analysis-in-graveyard-castable-with-flashback-mana-and-life
   - Card in graveyard, player has 1U mana and 3+ life
   - can-cast? returns true
   - Bug caught: Verifies flashback path enables casting from graveyard

3. test-deep-analysis-in-graveyard-not-castable-insufficient-life
   - Card in graveyard, player has 1U mana but only 2 life
   - can-cast? returns false
   - Bug caught: Ensures life cost is checked, prevents death-before-resolution

4. test-deep-analysis-in-graveyard-not-castable-insufficient-mana
   - Card in graveyard, player has 3 life but 0 mana
   - can-cast? returns false
   - Bug caught: Ensures mana cost still required

5. test-flashback-cast-pays-correct-costs
   - Cast from graveyard, verify 1U mana deducted, 3 life deducted
   - Bug caught: Ensures flashback cost (not normal cost) is paid

6. test-flashback-spell-exiles-on-resolution
   - Cast from graveyard, resolve
   - Object ends up in :exile zone, not :graveyard
   - Bug caught: Core flashback behavior - exile replacement effect

7. test-normal-cast-spell-goes-to-graveyard
   - Cast from hand, resolve
   - Object ends up in :graveyard zone (normal behavior preserved)
   - Bug caught: Regression - flashback changes don't break normal path

8. test-deep-analysis-draws-two-loses-two
   - Cast and resolve
   - Player hand +2 cards, life -2
   - Bug caught: Effect implementation correct

9. test-flashback-not-castable-from-exile
   - Card in exile zone with flashback
   - can-cast? returns false
   - Bug caught: Flashback only works from graveyard

### Step 7: Update all-cards in iggy_pop.cljs
- Add deep-analysis to all-cards vector

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Deep Analysis card defined with :card/flashback-cost {:mana {:colorless 1 :blue 1} :life 3}
- [ ] can-cast? returns true for flashback card in graveyard when player has mana AND life
- [ ] can-cast? returns false for flashback card in graveyard when player lacks life (< 3)
- [ ] cast-spell deducts flashback cost (not normal cost) when casting from graveyard
- [ ] cast-spell deducts life when flashback cost includes life
- [ ] Flashback cast marks object with :object/cast-via-flashback true
- [ ] resolve-spell sends :object/cast-via-flashback cards to :exile (not :graveyard)
- [ ] Normal cast (from hand) still sends to :graveyard (no regression)
- [ ] Deep Analysis effect: draw 2, lose 2 life
- [ ] All 9 flashback tests pass
- [ ] All existing tests pass (make test)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Life Cost Payment Timing**
- Flashback life cost is paid as part of casting (like mana)
- Must check life >= cost BEFORE allowing cast
- If player at 3 life casts flashback costing 3 life: allowed (goes to 0)
- If player at 2 life: cannot cast (insufficient resources)

**Deep Analysis Has Two Life Costs**
- Effect causes 2 life loss (target player loses 2 life)
- Flashback cost includes 3 life payment
- Total life loss when flashback casting: 5 life (3 cost + 2 effect)
- This is correct per Oracle text

**Flashback Only From Graveyard**
- Cards in exile with flashback: NOT castable
- Cards in hand with flashback: use normal cost
- Cards in library: not castable (can't cast from library)
- Only graveyard zone enables flashback alternate cost

**Replacement Effect for Destination**
- "If this spell would go to graveyard, exile it instead"
- This applies regardless of HOW it would go to graveyard:
  - Normal resolution: effect happens, then exile
  - Countered: counter tries to send to GY, replacement exiles
- Current code: resolve-spell handles resolution destination
- Counter implementation (if exists): may need separate handling
- Note: Counter system not implemented yet, so only resolution path needed

**No Flashback Without Cost**
- Card without :card/flashback-cost cannot be cast from graveyard
- Ensure can-cast? returns false for non-flashback cards in graveyard

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoding Deep Analysis specifically - flashback system must be generic
- ❌ NO checking flashback in resolve-spell by card name - use :object/cast-via-flashback flag
- ❌ NO allowing cast from graveyard without checking life cost (if present)
- ❌ NO breaking normal cast path for flashback cards (must still cast from hand normally)
- ❌ NO sending flashback spells to graveyard on resolution
close_reason: Deferred to future epic - scope too large for current batch. Epic delivered tutor system, land-entered triggers, fetchlands, and event system migration. Flashback system well-documented for future implementation.

title: Bug: Next Phase button allows advancing with non-empty stack
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Next Phase button MUST be disabled when the stack is non-empty
- New Turn button MUST be disabled when the stack is non-empty
- advance-phase event handler MUST no-op when stack is non-empty
- start-turn event handler MUST no-op when stack is non-empty
- Mana pool still empties on valid phase advance (existing behavior preserved)
- Turn-based triggers still fire on phase entry (existing behavior preserved)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Next Phase button visually disabled (greyed out) when stack has items
- [ ] New Turn button visually disabled when stack has items
- [ ] Clicking disabled button does nothing (no phase change)
- [ ] advance-phase event returns db unchanged when stack non-empty
- [ ] start-turn event returns db unchanged when stack non-empty
- [ ] Buttons re-enable immediately when stack empties
- [ ] All existing phase advancement behavior preserved when stack is empty
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] make validate passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO removing items from the stack on phase advance (bug fix: stack items must persist until explicitly resolved)
- ❌ NO auto-resolving the stack before advancing (simplicity: player decides when to resolve)
- ❌ NO new subscriptions when existing ::subs/stack works (YAGNI: reuse what exists)
- ❌ NO priority system implementation in this fix (scope: this is a bug fix, not a feature)

## Approach
Disable the Next Phase / New Turn button in the UI when the stack subscription returns non-empty items. Add defensive guards in both advance-phase and start-turn re-frame event handlers to no-op when the stack is non-empty. Two files changed: core.cljs (UI) and events/game.cljs (event guards).

## Architecture
- core.cljs turn-bar component: subscribe to ::subs/stack, use (seq stack) to conditionally disable button
- events/game.cljs ::advance-phase handler: check stack via queries, return db unchanged if non-empty
- events/game.cljs ::start-turn handler: same guard

## Design Rationale
### Problem
The Next Phase button currently allows advancing phases while items remain on the stack. This violates MTG rules where phase transitions only occur with an empty stack. Stack items persist across phases and even across turns, creating invalid game states.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- core.cljs:346-376 - turn-bar component, dispatches ::advance-phase with no stack check
- events/game.cljs:428-439 - advance-phase and start-turn handlers, no stack guards
- subs/game.cljs:36-53 - ::stack subscription already exists, returns all stack items
- queries.cljs:130-139 - get-stack-items query available for event handler guards

**External:**
- MTG Comprehensive Rules: Phase transitions require empty stack and both players passing priority

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Both UI disable + event guard ✓
**What it is:** Disable button in UI when stack non-empty AND add no-op guards in event handlers.

**Pros:**
- Defense-in-depth: UI prevents clicks, event handler catches programmatic dispatch
- Clear visual feedback to player

**Cons:**
- Slightly more code than UI-only

**Chosen because:** User preference for belt-and-suspenders safety, minimal additional complexity.

#### 2. UI-only disable ❌
**What it is:** Only disable the button in the UI, no event handler changes.

**Why we looked at this:** Simplest possible fix for a single-player tool.

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** User preferred defense-in-depth with both UI and event guard.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Explicitly asked to simplify.

#### 3. Event guard only ❌
**What it is:** Silent no-op in event handlers, button stays visually enabled.

**Why we looked at this:** Backend-first approach.

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Poor UX - button looks clickable but does nothing. No visual feedback.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** UI constraints prevent button disabling.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Disabling Next Phase / New Turn buttons when stack non-empty
- Event handler guards for advance-phase and start-turn

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Priority passing system - deferred to future feature
- Stack auto-resolution - never (player controls resolution)
- Resolve button changes - not needed for this fix

### Open Questions
- None - straightforward bug fix

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| What happens to stack items on phase advance? | Button should not work until stack empty | Disable button, don't touch stack items |
| Mana pool behavior? | Always empty on phase advance | No change to existing mana pool logic |
| New button or fix existing? | Fix existing - MTG rules say phase advances with empty stack | Modify existing turn-bar component |
| Scope: simple disable or priority system? | Just disable when stack non-empty | No priority system, simple guard |
| Which approach? | Both UI + event guard | Defense-in-depth |

### Dead-End Paths
None - straightforward bug fix with clear approach.

### Open Concerns Raised
None - user confirmed design without modifications.
close_reason: All success criteria verified: guards in pure functions, button disabled in UI, 729/729 tests pass, make validate clean

title: Create engine/costs.cljs with multimethod for :tap and :remove-counter
design:
## Goal
Implement engine/costs.cljs with multimethod dispatch for cost payment. Support :tap and :remove-counter cost types.

## Context
Completed fizzle-isp: Schema fields and effect types in place. Now need cost payment system that abilities.cljs will use.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Implementation Steps

### 1. RED: Write tests for pay-cost multimethod
File: src/test/fizzle/engine/costs_test.cljs (new file)

Follow pattern from effects_test.cljs - use add-permanent helper.

**:tap cost tests (6 tests):**
```clojure
(deftest test-pay-tap-cost-untapped-permanent
  (testing "pay-cost with :tap on untapped permanent taps it and returns db"
    ;; Setup: untapped permanent on battlefield
    ;; Action: (costs/pay-cost db object-id {:tap true})
    ;; Assert: returned db has permanent tapped, not nil
    ))

(deftest test-pay-tap-cost-already-tapped
  (testing "pay-cost with :tap on already tapped permanent returns nil"
    ;; Setup: tapped permanent on battlefield
    ;; Action: (costs/pay-cost db object-id {:tap true})
    ;; Assert: returns nil (cost cannot be paid)
    ))

(deftest test-can-pay-tap-untapped
  (testing "can-pay? :tap returns true for untapped permanent"
    ;; Catches: missing can-pay? implementation
    ))

(deftest test-can-pay-tap-already-tapped
  (testing "can-pay? :tap returns false for tapped permanent"
    ;; Catches: can-pay? not checking tapped state
    ))

(deftest test-pay-tap-invalid-object-id
  (testing "pay-cost with invalid object-id returns nil, no crash"
    ;; Catches: missing nil guard on object lookup
    ))

(deftest test-can-pay-tap-invalid-object-id
  (testing "can-pay? with invalid object-id returns false, no crash"
    ;; Catches: missing nil guard
    ))
```

**:remove-counter cost tests (6 tests):**
```clojure
(deftest test-pay-remove-counter-has-counters
  (testing "pay-cost removes counter from permanent with sufficient counters"
    ;; Setup: permanent with {:mining 3}
    ;; Action: (costs/pay-cost db object-id {:remove-counter {:mining 1}})
    ;; Assert: permanent now has {:mining 2}
    ;; Catches: counter not decremented
    ))

(deftest test-pay-remove-counter-exact-amount
  (testing "pay-cost removes last counter (edge case: counter goes to 0)"
    ;; Setup: permanent with {:mining 1}
    ;; Action: (costs/pay-cost db object-id {:remove-counter {:mining 1}})
    ;; Assert: permanent has {:mining 0} (counter exists with 0 value)
    ;; Catches: off-by-one error
    ))

(deftest test-pay-remove-counter-insufficient
  (testing "pay-cost returns nil when insufficient counters"
    ;; Setup: permanent with {:mining 1}
    ;; Action: (costs/pay-cost db object-id {:remove-counter {:mining 2}})
    ;; Assert: returns nil, counters unchanged
    ;; Catches: paying cost even when can't afford
    ))

(deftest test-pay-remove-counter-no-counters-at-all
  (testing "pay-cost returns nil when permanent has no counters (nil :object/counters)"
    ;; Setup: permanent with nil :object/counters
    ;; Action: (costs/pay-cost db object-id {:remove-counter {:mining 1}})
    ;; Assert: returns nil
    ;; Catches: nil pointer when counters map missing
    ))

(deftest test-can-pay-remove-counter-has-counters
  (testing "can-pay? returns true when counters sufficient"
    ;; Catches: can-pay? not checking counter count
    ))

(deftest test-can-pay-remove-counter-insufficient
  (testing "can-pay? returns false when counters insufficient"
    ;; Catches: can-pay? returning true when can't pay
    ))
```

### 2. GREEN: Implement engine/costs.cljs

Create new file: src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.costs
  "Cost payment system for abilities.
   
   Dispatches on cost type to pay costs.
   All functions are pure: (db, object-id, cost) -> db or nil."
  (:require
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as q]))


(defmulti pay-cost
  "Pay a single cost on an object.
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
     object-id - UUID of the permanent paying the cost
     cost - Map with single cost type key, e.g. {:tap true} or {:remove-counter {:mining 1}}
   
   Returns:
     New db with cost paid, or nil if cost cannot be paid."
  (fn [_db _object-id cost] (first (keys cost))))


(defmulti can-pay?
  "Check if a cost can be paid.
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
     object-id - UUID of the permanent
     cost - Map with single cost type key
   
   Returns:
     Boolean - true if cost can be paid, false otherwise."
  (fn [_db _object-id cost] (first (keys cost))))


;; Default: unknown cost types cannot be paid
(defmethod pay-cost :default [_db _object-id _cost] nil)
(defmethod can-pay? :default [_db _object-id _cost] false)
```

### 3. Implement :tap cost type

```clojure
(defmethod can-pay? :tap [db object-id _cost]
  ;; Can pay tap cost if:
  ;; 1. Object exists
  ;; 2. Object is not already tapped
  (when-let [obj-eid (q/get-object-eid db object-id)]
    (not (d/q '[:find ?tapped .
                :in $ ?e
                :where [?e :object/tapped ?tapped]]
              db obj-eid))))


(defmethod pay-cost :tap [db object-id cost]
  ;; Guard: check can pay first
  (when (can-pay? db object-id cost)
    (let [obj-eid (q/get-object-eid db object-id)]
      (d/db-with db [[:db/add obj-eid :object/tapped true]]))))
```

Note: May need to add q/get-object-eid to queries.cljs if it doesn't exist.

### 4. Implement :remove-counter cost type

```clojure
(defmethod can-pay? :remove-counter [db object-id cost]
  ;; Can pay remove-counter if:
  ;; 1. Object exists
  ;; 2. Object has sufficient counters of required type(s)
  (when-let [obj-eid (q/get-object-eid db object-id)]
    (let [required (:remove-counter cost)
          current (or (d/q '[:find ?c .
                             :in $ ?e
                             :where [?e :object/counters ?c]]
                           db obj-eid)
                      {})]
      (every? (fn [[counter-type amount]]
                (>= (get current counter-type 0) amount))
              required))))


(defmethod pay-cost :remove-counter [db object-id cost]
  ;; Guard: check can pay first
  (when (can-pay? db object-id cost)
    (let [obj-eid (q/get-object-eid db object-id)
          required (:remove-counter cost)
          current (or (d/q '[:find ?c .
                             :in $ ?e
                             :where [?e :object/counters ?c]]
                           db obj-eid)
                      {})
          new-counters (merge-with - current required)]
      (d/db-with db [[:db/add obj-eid :object/counters new-counters]]))))
```

### 5. Add helper query if needed

Check if q/get-object-eid exists. If not, add to db/queries.cljs:

```clojure
(defn get-object-eid
  "Get the entity ID for an object by its UUID."
  [db object-id]
  (d/q '[:find ?e .
         :in $ ?oid
         :where [?e :object/id ?oid]]
       db object-id))
```

### 6. VERIFY
- Run: make validate
- Confirm all 12 new tests pass
- Confirm existing tests still pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] engine/costs.cljs exists at src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs
- [ ] pay-cost multimethod dispatches on (first (keys cost))
- [ ] can-pay? multimethod dispatches on (first (keys cost))
- [ ] :tap cost: taps untapped permanent, returns nil if already tapped
- [ ] :remove-counter cost: removes counters, returns nil if insufficient
- [ ] 12 tests pass in costs_test.cljs (6 tap + 6 counter)
- [ ] All 161+ existing tests still pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO cond-> blocks for cost types (use multimethod dispatch)
- ❌ NO checking specific card IDs (data over code)
- ❌ NO mutating permanent state outside transaction
- ❌ NO paying cost when can-pay? returns false
- ❌ NO crashing on nil object-id (return nil/false gracefully)

## Key Considerations (Edge Cases)

**Nil Object ID**:
- Both pay-cost and can-pay? must handle nil object-id
- Return nil/false gracefully, no exceptions
- Test: test-pay-tap-invalid-object-id

**Nil Counters Map**:
- Permanent may have nil :object/counters (never had counters)
- Treat as empty map {} for comparison
- Test: test-pay-remove-counter-no-counters-at-all

**Counter Goes to Zero**:
- Removing last counter should leave {:mining 0}, not delete key
- This is important for state-based actions that check counter count
- Test: test-pay-remove-counter-exact-amount

**Default Multimethod**:
- Unknown cost types should return nil/false (graceful degradation)
- Matches pattern in effects.cljs

## Reference Implementation
- Study engine/effects.cljs for multimethod pattern
- Study test helpers in effects_test.cljs for add-permanent
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement Recoup card with grant, targeting, and cleanup systems
description:
Implement the Recoup card (1R Sorcery) which requires three new infrastructure systems:

**Oracle Text:** Target sorcery card in your graveyard gains flashback until end of turn. The flashback cost is equal to the card's mana cost. Flashback {3}{R}

## Systems Required

1. **Grant System** - Temporarily grant abilities/alternate costs to card instances
   - New :object/grants schema attribute
   - Explicit expiration: {:expires/turn N :expires/phase :cleanup}
   - Source tracking for removal effects

2. **Cast-Time Targeting System** - Declare and validate targets when casting
   - New :card/targeting and :ability/targeting attributes
   - Targets stored as map: target-ref → target-id
   - Fizzle if targets invalid on resolution

3. **Cleanup System** - Remove expired grants at end of turn
   - Registered as turn-based trigger on :cleanup phase

## Implementation Phases

1. Grant System (schema + core functions)
2. Cleanup System (expiration + trigger)
3. Targeting Foundation (core functions)
4. Deep Analysis Migration (stepping stone)
5. Cephalid Coliseum Migration (stepping stone)
6. Grant Flashback Effect
7. Recoup Card

**Design Doc:** docs/design/recoup-design.md
close_reason: All features implemented and tested: grant system, cast-time targeting, cleanup expiration, Recoup card with flashback granting. Multiple bug fixes for targeting UI and flashback exile behavior.

title: Implement land-entered trigger and City of Traitors
design:
## Goal
Implement :land-entered event type and City of Traitors card which produces {C}{C} and sacrifices itself when another land enters the battlefield.

## Context
Completed:
- fizzle-h4l: Event system migration done, old scanning code removed
- fizzle-pk2: Tutor infrastructure built, Merchant Scroll working

This is Phase 3 of epic fizzle-wjl. Uses the event-based trigger system established in Phase 1.

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Add land-entered-event helper
- File: src/main/fizzle/engine/events.cljs
- Add after zone-change-event (line ~54)
- Pattern similar to permanent-tapped-event:
  \`\`\`clojure
  (defn land-entered-event
    \"Create a land-entered event.
     Arguments:
       object-id - ID of the land that entered
       controller-id - ID of the land's controller
     Returns:
       Event map with :event/type :land-entered\"
    [object-id controller-id]
    {:event/type :land-entered
     :event/object-id object-id
     :event/controller controller-id})
  \`\`\`

### Step 2: Dispatch land-entered event in play-land
- File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs
- Location: play-land function around line 451-462
- After ETB effects execute, dispatch land-entered event:
  \`\`\`clojure
  ;; After ETB effects processing, before returning:
  (dispatch/dispatch-event db-after-etb 
    (game-events/land-entered-event object-id player-id))
  \`\`\`

### Step 3: Extend matches-filter? for :exclude-self
- File: src/main/fizzle/engine/trigger_dispatch.cljs
- Location: matches-filter? function (lines 11-31)
- Add :exclude-self handling in the cond:
  \`\`\`clojure
  (defn matches-filter?
    [trigger event]
    (let [f (:trigger/filter trigger)]
      (if (and f (seq f))
        (every? (fn [[k v]]
                  (cond
                    ;; Existing: :self matches trigger's source
                    (= v :self) (= (get event k) (:trigger/source trigger))
                    ;; NEW: :exclude-self means trigger source must NOT match event object
                    (= k :exclude-self) (not= (:event/object-id event) (:trigger/source trigger))
                    :else (= (get event k) v)))
                f)
        true)))
  \`\`\`

### Step 4: Implement City of Traitors card definition
- File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs
- Add after city-of-brass definition (around line 77)
- Add to all-cards vector at end
  \`\`\`clojure
  (def city-of-traitors
    {:card/id :city-of-traitors
     :card/name "City of Traitors"
     :card/cmc 0
     :card/mana-cost {}
     :card/colors #{}
     :card/types #{:land}
     :card/text \"When you play another land, sacrifice City of Traitors. {T}: Add {C}{C}.\"
     ;; Trigger: When ANOTHER land enters (not self)
     :card/triggers [{:trigger/type :land-entered
                      :trigger/description \"sacrifice City of Traitors\"
                      :trigger/filter {:exclude-self true}
                      :trigger/effects [{:effect/type :sacrifice
                                         :effect/target :self}]}]
     ;; Mana ability: T: Add {C}{C}
     :card/abilities [{:ability/type :mana
                       :ability/cost {:tap true}
                       :ability/produces {:colorless 2}}]})
  \`\`\`

### Step 5: Update register-card-triggers! for land-entered
- File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs
- Location: trigger-type->event-type function (lines 333-340)
- Add mapping for :land-entered:
  \`\`\`clojure
  (defn- trigger-type->event-type
    [trigger-type]
    (case trigger-type
      :becomes-tapped :permanent-tapped
      :land-entered :land-entered  ;; NEW
      trigger-type))
  \`\`\`

### Step 6: Write tests
- File: src/test/fizzle/cards/city_of_traitors_test.cljs (NEW)
- Tests to implement (see Success Criteria)

### Step 7: Run validation
- make validate passes
- All tests green

## Success Criteria
- [ ] land-entered-event helper exists in events.cljs
- [ ] play-land dispatches :land-entered event after land ETBs
- [ ] matches-filter? supports :exclude-self filter key
- [ ] City of Traitors produces {:colorless 2} via mana ability
- [ ] City of Traitors trigger registered on ETB with :exclude-self filter
- [ ] City of Traitors sacrifices when ANOTHER land enters battlefield
- [ ] City of Traitors does NOT sacrifice when it enters (exclude-self works)
- [ ] Test: test-cot-produces-colorless-mana - verifies {C}{C} production
- [ ] Test: test-cot-does-not-sacrifice-on-self-entry - verifies exclude-self
- [ ] Test: test-cot-sacrifices-when-another-land-enters - core trigger behavior
- [ ] Test: test-two-cots-both-sacrifice-when-land-enters - edge case
- [ ] Test: test-cot-trigger-uses-stack - trigger goes on stack, can respond
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: City of Traitors as First Land**
- When CoT enters as first land, trigger should NOT fire (no other lands entered)
- Verify :exclude-self filter is working correctly
- Test explicitly: play CoT, verify no trigger on stack

**Edge Case: Two City of Traitors on Battlefield**
- When a third land enters, BOTH CoT triggers should fire
- Both should sacrifice (or both triggers on stack)
- Test explicitly: play CoT #1, play CoT #2 (CoT #1 sacrifices), play Island (remaining CoT sacrifices)

**Edge Case: City of Traitors Sacrificed in Response**
- If player sacrifices CoT to another effect (e.g., mana ability that sacs)
- Trigger already on stack should still try to resolve
- But :self target won't find permanent - should handle gracefully
- Reference: cob-event-trigger-test.cljs:273-292 shows similar pattern

**Test Isolation**
- Use reset-registry fixture pattern from cob_event_trigger_test.cljs
- Clear registry before and after each test
- Re-register turn-based actions after clearing

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow City of Brass pattern in cob_event_trigger_test.cljs
- Use same test helpers (create-test-db, add-land-to-hand, add-land-to-battlefield)
- Similar register function for test setup

## Anti-Patterns (from epic)
- NO scanning-based triggers (use event system)
- NO check-trigger multimethod (use trigger-registry + dispatch-event)
- NO leaving dead code (clean up if anything obsolete)
close_reason: Implemented land-entered event type and City of Traitors card. All tests pass, make validate passes.

title: Task 3: Migrate card-selection, pile-choice, graveyard, exile-cards, and peek-selection modals
description:
## Goal
Migrate 5 modals that share the selection-card-view helper from core.cljs to modals.cljs. Also move the selection-card-view helper itself.

## Implementation

### Move to modals.cljs:
1. selection-card-view (core.cljs:421-442) - shared helper used by 5 modals
2. card-selection-modal (core.cljs:445-569) - discard/tutor with multi-select
3. pile-choice-modal (core.cljs:661-729) - Intuition-style pile choice
4. graveyard-selection-modal (core.cljs:732-804) - Ill-Gotten Gains style
5. exile-cards-selection-modal (core.cljs:807-876) - Flash of Insight flashback
6. peek-selection-modal (core.cljs:879-950) - Flash of Insight peek

### Key details:
- selection-card-view uses ::selection-events/toggle-selection as default event — already required in modals.cljs
- card-selection-modal also dispatches ::selection-events/confirm-tutor-selection, ::selection-events/confirm-selection, ::selection-events/cancel-selection — all in selection-events namespace
- graveyard-selection-modal has purple border (#6a4a8a) — use container-style with border-color param
- exile-cards, peek, graveyard modals also have purple border (#6a4a8a)
- card-selection-modal has blue border (#4A9BD9)
- pile-choice-modal has blue border (#4A9BD9)
- All modals use header margin "0 0 8px 0" (NOT 16px like header-style) — render custom h2, don't use modal-wrapper
- Use overlay-style and container-style from shared constants but render h2 with "0 0 8px 0" margin

### Changes to core.cljs:
- Delete selection-card-view (lines 421-442)
- Delete card-selection-modal (lines 445-569)
- Delete pile-choice-modal (lines 661-729)
- Delete graveyard-selection-modal (lines 732-804)
- Delete exile-cards-selection-modal (lines 807-876)
- Delete peek-selection-modal (lines 879-950)
- Update selection-modal router to use modals/ prefix for these 5 modals

### Verification:
- make fmt then make validate
- core.cljs should drop from 1278 to ~750 lines

## Success Criteria
- [ ] selection-card-view helper in modals.cljs
- [ ] 5 modals moved to modals.cljs, pixel-identical behavior
- [ ] core.cljs router updated to call modals/* for these 5
- [ ] Purple borders preserved on graveyard/exile/peek modals
- [ ] Blue borders preserved on card-selection/pile-choice modals
- [ ] make validate passes
close_reason: Migrated selection-card-view, card-selection-modal, pile-choice-modal, graveyard-selection-modal, exile-cards-selection-modal, and peek-selection-modal to modals.cljs. core.cljs reduced from 1278 to 836 lines (-442). All 741 tests pass, lint + format clean.
