title: Strengthen YELLOW tests with weak assertions
design:
## Goal
Replace weak assertions with meaningful ones that catch real bugs.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Tests to Strengthen

### 1. move-to-zone-same-zone-no-op-test (zones_test.cljs:48-55)
**Current:** Verifies zone and count only
**Issue:** Doesn't verify `db = db'` - could modify other state silently
**Upgrade:** Add `(is (= db db') "Same-zone move should be exact no-op")`
**File:** src/test/fizzle/engine/zones_test.cljs

### 2. test-add-counters-to-permanent (effects_test.cljs)
**Current:** Only tests adding new counters to object with no counters
**Issue:** No edge case for adding to object that already has counters
**Upgrade:** Add test case where object already has 2 counters, add 3 more, verify 5 total
**File:** src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs

### 3. Threshold boundary regression (conditions_test.cljs)
**Current:** Tests 0, 6, 7, 10+ cards in graveyard
**Issue:** No test for crossing the boundary (7 -> 6 via removal)
**Upgrade:** Add test: start with 7 cards (threshold=true), remove 1, verify threshold=false
**File:** src/test/fizzle/engine/conditions_test.cljs

### 4. Helper function validation
**Issue:** Many tests use `add-cards-to-library`, `add-card-to-zone` helpers. If helper buggy, all tests using it have false confidence.
**Upgrade:** Add sanity check at top of test files that helpers work:
```clojure
(deftest test-helper-add-card-to-zone-works
  (testing "Sanity check: helper adds card to correct zone"
    (let [db (create-test-db)
          [db' obj-id] (add-card-to-zone db :dark-ritual :hand :player-1)]
      (is (= :hand (get-object-zone db' obj-id))))))
```

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Read zones_test.cljs:48-55 and add db equality assertion
- [ ] Find add-counters test in effects_test.cljs and add cumulative counter case
- [ ] Add threshold boundary regression test to conditions_test.cljs
- [ ] Add helper validation test to merchant_scroll_test.cljs (uses most helpers)
- [ ] Run `make test` after each change
- [ ] Run `make validate` at the end

## Success Criteria
- [ ] zones_test.cljs: move-to-zone-same-zone-no-op-test verifies `(= db db')`
- [ ] effects_test.cljs: counter test includes cumulative add case
- [ ] conditions_test.cljs: new test `test-threshold-boundary-regression` exists and passes
- [ ] At least one test file has helper sanity check
- [ ] `make test` passes
- [ ] `make validate` passes

## Key Considerations

**db Equality in ClojureScript:**
Datascript databases are immutable and support `=` comparison. Adding `(is (= db db'))` works directly.

**Threshold Regression Test Structure:**
```clojure
(deftest test-threshold-boundary-regression
  (testing "threshold? changes from true to false when card removed"
    (let [db-7 (-> (init-game-state)
                   (add-cards-to-graveyard :player-1 7))
          _ (is (true? (conditions/threshold? db-7 :player-1))
                "Precondition: should have threshold with 7 cards")
          ;; Remove 1 card by moving to exile
          gy-card (first (q/get-graveyard db-7 :player-1))
          db-6 (zones/move-to-zone db-7 (:object/id gy-card) :exile)]
      (is (= 6 (count (q/get-graveyard db-6 :player-1)))
          "Should have 6 cards after removal")
      (is (false? (conditions/threshold? db-6 :player-1))
          "Should lose threshold when dropping below 7"))))
```

**Counter Edge Case:**
Storm counts are tracked in mana_pool, not counters. Focus on :loyalty-counters or similar if implemented.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Replacing `!= nil` with `!= undefined` (still weak)
- ❌ Adding edge cases without meaningful assertions
- ❌ Weakening tests to make them pass
- ❌ Testing helper functions MORE than production code
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 1: Create views/modals.cljs with shared components and migrate player-target modals
design:
## Goal
Create the new views/modals.cljs file with shared style constants and reusable components, then migrate the 3 player-target modals as the first extraction — merging them into 1 parameterized function. This validates the extraction pattern before migrating remaining modals.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- core.cljs:467-476 — overlay style (reference copy)
- core.cljs:477-490 — container style with max-width and border variations
- core.cljs:485-488 — header h2 style
- core.cljs:462-508 — player-target-modal (the template)
- core.cljs:511-558 — ability-player-target-modal (differs at line 557)
- core.cljs:641-688 — cast-time-player-target-modal (differs at line 687)
- core.cljs:444-459 — player-target-button helper (used by all 3)
- core.cljs:1375-1435 — selection-modal router (dispatches to modals by type)

### 2. Create src/main/fizzle/views/modals.cljs
```clojure
(ns fizzle.views.modals
  (:require
    [fizzle.events.abilities :as ability-events]
    [fizzle.events.game :as events]
    [fizzle.events.selection :as selection-events]
    [fizzle.subs.game :as subs]
    [re-frame.core :as rf]))
```

### 3. Extract shared style constants
```clojure
(def overlay-style
  {:position "fixed" :top 0 :left 0 :right 0 :bottom 0
   :background "rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.8)"
   :display "flex" :align-items "center" :justify-content "center"
   :z-index 1000})

(defn container-style
  [{:keys [max-width border-color]
    :or {max-width "600px" border-color "#4A9BD9"}}]
  {:background "#1a1a2a"
   :border (str "2px solid " border-color)
   :border-radius "12px"
   :padding "24px"
   :max-width max-width
   :width "90%"})

(def header-style
  {:color "#eee" :margin "0 0 16px 0" :font-size "18px"})
```

### 4. Extract shared button components
```clojure
(defn confirm-button
  [{:keys [label valid? on-confirm padding enabled-bg]
    :or {padding "8px 20px" enabled-bg "#2a6a2a"}}]
  [:button {:style {:padding padding
                    :border "none"
                    :border-radius "4px"
                    :cursor (if valid? "pointer" "not-allowed")
                    :background (if valid? enabled-bg "#333")
                    :color (if valid? "#fff" "#666")
                    :font-size "14px"
                    :font-weight "bold"}
            :disabled (not valid?)
            :on-click on-confirm}
   label])

(defn cancel-button
  [{:keys [label on-cancel]}]
  [:button {:style {:padding "8px 20px"
                    :border "1px solid #555"
                    :border-radius "4px"
                    :cursor "pointer"
                    :background "#333"
                    :color "#ccc"
                    :font-size "14px"}
            :on-click on-cancel}
   label])
```

### 5. Extract modal-wrapper component
Use Reagent Form-1 with `into` pattern for children:
```clojure
(defn modal-wrapper
  [{:keys [title max-width border-color]} & children]
  [:div {:style overlay-style}
   (into [:div {:style (merge (container-style {:max-width max-width
                                                :border-color border-color})
                              {:text-align "center"})}
          [:h2 {:style header-style} title]]
         children)])
```
NOTE: Use `into` to splice children into the container div. This is the idiomatic Reagent pattern for wrapper components.

### 6. Migrate player-target-button helper
Copy core.cljs:444-459 to modals.cljs. Change from `defn-` to `defn` (no longer private since callers are in same file, but keep as defn since it is now the only namespace).

### 7. Create parameterized player-target-modal
Signature: `[selection confirm-event]`

CRITICAL: The 3 original modals differ in their confirm dispatch:
- player-target-modal: `(rf/dispatch [::selection-events/confirm-player-target-selection])` — NO selected-target arg
- ability-player-target-modal: `(rf/dispatch [::ability-events/confirm-ability-target selected-target])` — WITH selected-target arg
- cast-time-player-target-modal: `(rf/dispatch [::selection-events/confirm-cast-time-target selected-target])` — WITH selected-target arg

Solution: Always dispatch `[confirm-event selected-target]`. Re-frame ignores extra vector elements that the handler doesn't destructure. The `confirm-player-target-selection` handler will simply ignore the extra arg.

```clojure
(defn player-target-modal
  [selection confirm-event]
  (let [selected-target (:selection/selected-target selection)
        valid? (some? selected-target)]
    [modal-wrapper {:title "Choose target player" :max-width "400px"}
     [:div {:style {:display "flex" :justify-content "center" :margin-bottom "20px"}}
      [player-target-button :player-1 "You" (= selected-target :player-1)]
      [player-target-button :opponent "Opponent" (= selected-target :opponent)]]
     [:div {:style {:display "flex" :justify-content "center"}}
      [confirm-button {:label "Confirm"
                       :valid? valid?
                       :on-confirm #(rf/dispatch [confirm-event selected-target])
                       :padding "10px 32px"}]]]))
```

### 8. Update core.cljs selection-modal router (KEEP ROUTER IN core.cljs)
DO NOT move the router to modals.cljs yet. Moving it would create a circular dependency (modals.cljs would need core.cljs for non-migrated modals, and core.cljs requires modals.cljs).

In the selection-modal router, update only the 3 player-target cases:
```clojure
;; Line 1398-1399: was [cast-time-player-target-modal selection]
(and (= selection-type :cast-time-targeting) targets-player?)
[modals/player-target-modal selection ::selection-events/confirm-cast-time-target]

;; Line 1406-1407: was [ability-player-target-modal selection]
(and (= selection-type :ability-targeting) targets-player?)
[modals/player-target-modal selection ::ability-events/confirm-ability-target]

;; Line 1414-1415: was [player-target-modal selection]
(= effect-type :player-target)
[modals/player-target-modal selection ::selection-events/confirm-player-target-selection]
```

### 9. Update core.cljs requires and cleanup
- Add `[fizzle.views.modals :as modals]` to ns require
- Delete `player-target-button` (lines 444-459)
- Delete `player-target-modal` (lines 462-508)
- Delete `ability-player-target-modal` (lines 511-558)
- Delete `cast-time-player-target-modal` (lines 641-688)

### 10. Verify
- `make fmt` — auto-fix any formatting issues (MEMORY.md: extractions often cause formatting issues)
- `make validate` — must pass lint + format-check + all tests

## Success Criteria
- [ ] src/main/fizzle/views/modals.cljs exists with namespace declaration
- [ ] overlay-style, container-style, header-style defined exactly once in modals.cljs
- [ ] modal-wrapper component renders overlay + container + header + children
- [ ] confirm-button and cancel-button components accept options map
- [ ] player-target-button helper moved to modals.cljs
- [ ] 3 player-target modals replaced by 1 parameterized `player-target-modal` function taking `[selection confirm-event]`
- [ ] core.cljs router updated to call `modals/player-target-modal` for 3 cases
- [ ] core.cljs contains zero player-target modal definitions
- [ ] core.cljs line count reduced by ~120 lines (3 modals + helper removed)
- [ ] `make validate` passes (lint + format-check + all 741 tests)
- [ ] No circular dependencies between core.cljs and modals.cljs

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO moving the selection-modal router to modals.cljs in this task (causes circular dep with non-migrated modals still in core.cljs)
- ❌ NO changing any re-frame event handler behavior
- ❌ NO changing event keyword namespaces
- ❌ NO visual or behavioral changes to any modal
- ❌ NO adding text-align center to modal-wrapper if original modals that will use it later don't all have it (verify per-modal in subsequent tasks)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Circular Dependency Prevention:**
Router MUST stay in core.cljs for this task. modals.cljs -> core.cljs would create circular require. Router moves to modals.cljs only in the final task when ALL modals have been migrated.

**Confirm Dispatch Arg Difference:**
player-target-modal dispatches [::confirm-player-target-selection] (no arg), while the other two dispatch [::confirm-ability-target selected-target] and [::confirm-cast-time-target selected-target] (with arg). Parameterized version always passes selected-target. Re-frame ignores extra vector elements the handler doesn't destructure. Safe to unify.

**defn- to defn Visibility Change:**
Original modals are defn- (private) in core.cljs. In modals.cljs they become defn (public). This is expected and correct — they need to be callable from core.cljs router. Not a risk since they were only private to avoid polluting the namespace.

**Reagent Children Pattern:**
modal-wrapper uses `into` to splice children into the container div. This is the idiomatic Reagent Form-1 pattern. Do NOT use Form-2 (fn [] (fn [] ...)) — it's unnecessary for a stateless wrapper.

**Formatting:**
Always run `make fmt` after extraction. cljstyle may flag formatting differences between the source and destination files (per MEMORY.md lessons).
close_reason: Closed

title: Epic: Fork/Replay Minimal UI
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

1. Right sidebar panel (fixed 250px) displays history alongside the game area
2. Action log shows flat chronological list of entries with turn number prefix and description
3. Current entry highlighted with blue accent; entries past current position are dimmed
4. Back/Forward step buttons navigate through history, restoring game state snapshots
5. Branch indicator shows current branch name (main or fork name)
6. Fork list shows all available branches; clicking switches branch and restores tip state
7. Auto-fork only — no manual fork creation button (engine handles auto-fork on action from rewound position)
8. All UI state derived from re-frame subscriptions; all mutations via re-frame dispatch

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] Sidebar renders alongside game area in a flex layout
- [ ] Action log shows chronological entries with turn prefix and description
- [ ] Current position entry highlighted blue, entries after position dimmed
- [ ] Step back/forward buttons navigate history and restore game-db snapshots
- [ ] Step buttons disabled when at boundaries (can't step back from 0, can't step forward from tip)
- [ ] Current branch name displayed
- [ ] Fork list shows all branches; clicking switches branch
- [ ] UI updates reactively when history state changes (new entries, position changes, branch switches)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- NO direct app-db reads in views (must use re-frame subscriptions)
- NO history logic in UI components (delegate to history/core.cljs pure functions via events)
- NO placing history events in fizzle.events.history namespace (MUST be fizzle.history.events — interceptor skip depends on namespace starting with fizzle.history)
- NO manual fork creation UI (auto-fork only — Complete UI epic adds manual fork)
- NO turn grouping or collapsible sections (deferred to Complete UI epic)
- NO click-to-jump on action log entries (deferred to Complete UI epic)
- NO CSS files or Tailwind (must use inline JS style objects matching existing codebase pattern)

## Approach

Build three new files (history/events.cljs, subs/history.cljs, views/history.cljs) and modify core.cljs layout. Events wrap history/core.cljs pure functions. Subscriptions extract history state from app-db. Views render a sidebar with action log, step controls, and fork list. Layout change wraps existing vertical game area in a flex row with the new sidebar.

## Architecture

- history/events.cljs — Re-frame events: step-back, step-forward, switch-branch (MUST be in fizzle.history.events namespace for interceptor compatibility)
- subs/history.cljs — Subscriptions: entries, position, current-branch, forks, can-step-back?, can-step-forward?
- views/history.cljs — Sidebar: header, step controls, action log (scrollable), fork list
- core.cljs — Layout modification: wrap game area in flex row, add sidebar

Data flow:
  history/core.cljs (pure fns) <- called by history/events.cljs (re-frame events)
  views/history.cljs <- subscribes to subs/history.cljs <- extracts from app-db

## Design Rationale

### Problem
The fork/replay engine is fully implemented (history/core.cljs, interceptor, descriptions) but has no UI exposure. Players cannot see their action history, navigate to previous positions, or switch between branches. The engine is invisible.

### Research Findings

**Codebase:**
- history/core.cljs:4-139 — Full data model and operations (init, entries, step-to, auto-fork, switch-branch, list-forks)
- history/interceptor.cljs:36-65 — Global interceptor auto-captures entries on game-db changes
- history/interceptor.cljs:10-21 — Skips events with namespace starting with "fizzle.history" (CRITICAL for event placement)
- history/descriptions.cljs:4-36 — Human-readable descriptions for all event types
- core.cljs:148-150 — History state merged into app-db at game init
- core.cljs:452 — Interceptor registered at app startup
- No history subscriptions or events exist yet
- views/modals.cljs — Existing view pattern: inline JS styles, dark theme, monospace font
- subs/game.cljs — Subscription pattern: Layer 2 raw access, Layer 3 derived with :<-

**Conventions discovered:**
- Color palette: #0d0d1a (bg), #1a1a2a (containers), #4A9BD9 (blue accent), #eee (text)
- All inline JS styles, no CSS files
- Monospace font throughout
- Flex layout for everything
- Components subscribe independently via @(rf/subscribe [...])

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Right sidebar with step buttons (CHOSEN)

**What it is:** Fixed 250px sidebar to the right of the game area. Contains step back/forward buttons, a flat action log with current-position highlighting, and an inline fork list.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed core.cljs layout — currently single-column vertical stack, easy to wrap in flex row
- Reviewed existing button patterns — enabled/disabled styling with green/gray convention
- 250px accommodates turn prefix + description text in monospace

**Pros:**
- Always visible without scrolling or toggling
- Matches common IDE/tool sidebar pattern
- Doesn't displace existing game layout vertically

**Cons:**
- Reduces horizontal game area width

**Chosen because:** Persistent visibility is critical for a practice tool — players need constant awareness of their position in history

#### 2. Bottom panel (REJECTED)

**What it is:** New section below the existing vertical stack (below stack/storm views)

**Why we looked at this:** Simpler layout change (just append to existing vertical layout)

**Pros:** No layout restructuring needed
**Cons:** Pushes game content up, requires scrolling to see both game and history

**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Already tall vertical layout — adding more at bottom pushes critical game state off-screen

**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Game layout fundamentally changes to be more compact

#### 3. Collapsible drawer (REJECTED)

**What it is:** Hidden panel that slides in when toggled via a button

**Why we looked at this:** Preserves full game width when not needed

**Pros:** No space taken when hidden
**Cons:** History invisible by default — defeats purpose of practice tool awareness

**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Fork/replay is a core feature, not optional — hiding it discourages use

**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** User explicitly requests a way to hide the sidebar (add toggle later)

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- Sidebar with action log, step buttons, fork list
- Re-frame events and subscriptions for history state
- Layout modification to core.cljs
- Step back/forward with snapshot restoration
- Branch switching
- Visual indicators for current position and current branch

**Out of scope (deferred to Complete UI epic fizzle-ucfj):**
- Turn grouping / collapsible sections
- Click-to-jump on entries
- Inline fork/rewind buttons on hover
- Fork tree visualization
- Fork naming/renaming
- Fork comparison view
- Timeline scrubber

### Open Questions
- Should action log auto-scroll to current position when stepping? (likely yes, decide during implementation)
- Exact entry text format: 'T1: Cast spell' vs 'Turn 1 — Cast spell'? (decide during implementation, keep compact)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Panel placement? | Right sidebar | Layout change: flex row wrapping game + sidebar |
| Step navigation? | Back/Forward buttons | Simple two-button control, no click-to-jump |
| Fork display? | Inline section | Fork list below action log, click to switch |
| Turn grouping? | Flat list | Simple chronological list, no grouping logic |
| Rewind indicator? | Highlight current entry | Blue accent on current, dim entries after position |
| Fork creation? | Auto-fork only | No manual fork button, engine handles auto-fork |
| Width split? | Fixed 250px sidebar | Game area takes remaining width |

### Dead-End Paths
None — all design options were clear and user chose recommended paths.

### Open Concerns Raised
None — straightforward minimal UI scope.
close_reason: All children complete (events/subs + sidebar view). 934 tests pass.

title: Polluted Delta fetch ability should use the stack
design:
## Goal
Polluted Delta's activated ability should go on the stack using existing trigger infrastructure.

## Context
Current `activate-ability` (game.cljs:602-663) executes effects immediately after paying costs. Should instead use the same stack system as triggered abilities.

## Root Cause
The code at lines 640-644 executes effects directly:
```clojure
;; No selection needed - execute all effects
{:db (reduce (fn [db' effect]
               (effects/execute-effect db' player-id effect))
             db-after-costs
             effects-list)
 :pending-selection nil}
```

Should instead add to stack like triggered abilities do.

## Existing Infrastructure (triggers.cljs)
- `create-trigger` - creates trigger entity with type, source, player, data
- `add-trigger-to-stack` - adds trigger with stack-order
- `resolve-trigger` multimethod - dispatches on `:trigger/type`

Activated abilities can use this same system:
- `:trigger/type` → `:activated-ability`
- `:trigger/source` → the permanent's object-id
- `:trigger/effects` → the ability's effects list
- `:trigger/uses-stack?` → true

## Implementation
File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs (activate-ability function)

### Step 1: Modify activate-ability to add to stack instead of executing
```clojure
;; Instead of executing effects directly:
(let [ability-trigger (triggers/create-trigger
                        :activated-ability
                        object-id
                        player-id
                        {:effects effects-list
                         :remaining-effects effects-after})]  ;; for tutor case
  {:db (triggers/add-trigger-to-stack db-after-costs ability-trigger)
   :pending-selection nil})
```

### Step 2: Add resolve-trigger method for :activated-ability
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs

```clojure
(defmethod resolve-trigger :activated-ability
  [db trigger]
  (let [player-id (:trigger/player trigger)
        effects (get-in trigger [:trigger/data :effects])]
    (reduce (fn [d effect]
              (effects/execute-effect d player-id effect))
            db
            effects)))
```

### Step 3: Handle selection effects on resolution
For tutor effects, the selection modal should appear on RESOLUTION, not activation.
This matches MTG rules - you search when the ability resolves.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Polluted Delta ability creates trigger on stack when activated
- [ ] Sacrifice happens immediately (as cost), not on resolution
- [ ] Tutor selection appears when ability RESOLVES from stack
- [ ] Can respond to ability (priority passes after activation)
- [ ] Existing mana abilities still resolve immediately (no stack)
- [ ] Storm trigger, City of Brass trigger still work correctly

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO creating new stack system - use existing trigger infrastructure
- ❌ NO executing effects on activation (except mana abilities)
- ❌ NO breaking mana ability immediate resolution
close_reason: Implemented activated ability stack usage. Costs paid on activation, effects resolve from stack. Added 5 tests. All tests pass.

title: Phase 2: Card Batch + Selection UI
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Basic lands (Island, Swamp) produce typed mana when tapped
- Lotus Petal sacrifices to produce 1 mana of any color
- Lion's Eye Diamond sacrifices + discards hand to produce 3 mana of any color (timing restriction deferred)
- Careful Study draws 2, then player selects 2 cards to discard
- Mental Note mills 2 from self, then draws 1
- Player can select specific cards from hand via modal UI
- Graveyard contents visible with card count for threshold tracking

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Island taps for blue mana
- [ ] Swamp taps for black mana
- [ ] Lotus Petal can be sacrificed for any color mana
- [ ] LED can be activated (sacrifice + discard hand) for 3 mana of chosen color
- [ ] Careful Study draws 2, shows selection modal, discards player's 2 chosen cards
- [ ] Mental Note mills 2 from own library, draws 1
- [ ] Selection modal appears when effect requires player choice
- [ ] Selection modal enforces exact count (must select N cards)
- [ ] Graveyard view shows all cards in graveyard zone
- [ ] Graveyard count visible for threshold tracking (7+ indicator)
- [ ] All new cards added to iggy_pop.cljs all-cards vector
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoded card IDs in engine code (data-over-code: engine interprets card data generically)
- ❌ NO auto-selecting discard targets (user agency: player must choose which cards to discard)
- ❌ NO blocking UI during selection (UX: modal must be dismissable/cancelable)
- ❌ NO skipping selection for 'obvious' cases (consistency: always show modal when :selection :player)

## Approach
Implement cards as pure EDN data following existing patterns. Add new cost types (:sacrifice-self, :discard-hand) to costs.cljs. Add :discard effect type to effects.cljs with :selection :player support. Create modal component for card selection that pauses game resolution until player confirms. Add graveyard-view component showing zone contents with threshold indicator.

## Architecture
- src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Add 5 new card definitions
- src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs - Add :sacrifice-self and :discard-hand cost types
- src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs - Add :discard effect with selection support
- src/main/fizzle/core.cljs - Add selection-modal and graveyard-view components
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs - Add selection state management events
- src/main/fizzle/db/schema.cljs - Add :game/pending-selection schema if needed

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Phase 1 has only 5 cards (Dark Ritual, Cabal Ritual, Brain Freeze, City of Brass, Gemstone Mine). Phase 2 needs the full Iggy Pop deck playable. This batch adds core mana acceleration (Lotus Petal, LED) and card filtering (Careful Study, Mental Note) plus basic lands.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Existing card pattern with :card/abilities, :card/effects
- engine/costs.cljs - Has :tap and :remove-counter costs, needs :sacrifice-self and :discard-hand
- engine/effects.cljs - Has :draw, :mill, :add-mana, needs :discard with selection
- core.cljs - Reagent components, no modal system yet

**External:**
- MTG rules: LED 'activate only as an instant' - deferred to future task per user decision

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Modal overlay for card selection ✓
**What it is:** Popup component showing hand contents, player clicks cards to select, confirm button submits choice.

**Chosen because:** Reusable for Intuition (select from 3), IGG (select from graveyard), Cunning Wish (select from sideboard). User selected this approach.

#### 2. Inline hand selection ❌
**What it is:** Multi-select mode in existing hand view with highlight states.

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** More complex state management, harder to reuse for non-hand selections.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Modal approach proves too intrusive for gameplay flow.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- 5 cards (Island, Swamp, Lotus Petal, LED, Careful Study, Mental Note)
- Selection modal for discard
- Graveyard view with threshold indicator

**Out of scope:**
- LED timing restriction (deferred - separate task)
- Other Iggy Pop cards (future batches)
- Graveyard interaction (return cards, flashback)

### Open Questions
- Should selection modal be cancelable? (default: yes, returns to pre-cast state)
- Graveyard card ordering? (default: most recent on top)

## Design Discovery

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Which cards first? | All 5 (lands, Petal, LED, draw spells) | Batch implementation |
| LED timing restriction? | Defer - just add discard cost | Simpler now, timing task later |
| Discard selection? | Player selects cards | Need selection UI |
| Selection UI style? | Modal overlay | Reusable component |
| Graveyard view? | Yes, include | Track threshold progress |

### Dead-End Paths
#### Random discard
**Why explored:** Simplest implementation
**Why abandoned:** User wants player agency in discard decisions
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 3: Implement Deep Analysis card and counter-exile behavior
design:
## Goal
Create the Deep Analysis card definition and ensure flashback spells exile when countered/fizzled.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context
Tasks 1-2 completed the alternate casting mode infrastructure:
- get-casting-modes returns valid modes for a card
- cast-spell-mode pays costs and tracks mode on object
- resolve-spell uses mode's :mode/on-resolve for destination

Missing:
- Deep Analysis card definition
- Counter/fizzle handling (flashback spells must exile when leaving stack for ANY reason)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Card definition patterns (study careful-study for draw effects)
- src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs - resolve-spell, already has :object/cast-mode handling
- No counter-spell exists yet - create move-spell-off-stack function

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

```clojure
;; In src/test/fizzle/cards/deep_analysis_test.cljs

(ns fizzle.cards.deep-analysis-test
  (:require
    [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.cards.deep-analysis :refer [deep-analysis]]
    [fizzle.db.init :refer [init-game-state]]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
    [fizzle.engine.mana :as mana]
    [fizzle.engine.rules :as rules]
    [fizzle.engine.zones :as zones]))

;; --- Card Definition Tests ---

(deftest deep-analysis-card-definition-test
  (testing "Deep Analysis has correct mana cost"
    (is (= {:colorless 3 :blue 1} (:card/mana-cost deep-analysis))))
  (testing "Deep Analysis is a sorcery"
    (is (contains? (:card/types deep-analysis) :sorcery)))
  (testing "Deep Analysis has flashback alternate cost"
    (let [flashback (first (:card/alternate-costs deep-analysis))]
      (is (= :flashback (:alternate/id flashback)))
      (is (= :graveyard (:alternate/zone flashback)))
      (is (= {:colorless 1 :blue 1} (:alternate/mana-cost flashback)))
      (is (= [{:cost/type :pay-life :cost/amount 3}] (:alternate/additional-costs flashback)))
      (is (= :exile (:alternate/on-resolve flashback))))))

;; --- Cast from hand tests ---

(deftest deep-analysis-cast-from-hand-test
  (testing "Deep Analysis castable from hand for 3U"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          [obj-id db] (add-card-to-zone db :player-1 deep-analysis :hand)
          db (mana/add-mana db :player-1 {:colorless 3 :blue 1})
          modes (rules/get-casting-modes db :player-1 obj-id)
          primary-mode (first (filter #(= :primary (:mode/id %)) modes))]
      (is (some? primary-mode) "Should have primary mode")
      (is (rules/can-cast-mode? db :player-1 obj-id primary-mode)))))

;; --- Flashback cast tests ---

(deftest deep-analysis-flashback-cast-test
  (testing "Deep Analysis castable from graveyard for 1U + 3 life"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          [obj-id db] (add-card-to-zone db :player-1 deep-analysis :graveyard)
          db (mana/add-mana db :player-1 {:colorless 1 :blue 1})
          initial-life (q/get-life-total db :player-1)
          modes (rules/get-casting-modes db :player-1 obj-id)
          flashback-mode (first modes)]
      (is (= :flashback (:mode/id flashback-mode)))
      (is (rules/can-cast-mode? db :player-1 obj-id flashback-mode)))))

;; --- Counter/remove from stack tests ---

(deftest flashback-spell-countered-exiles-test
  (testing "Countered flashback spell goes to exile not graveyard"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          [obj-id db] (add-card-to-zone db :player-1 deep-analysis :graveyard)
          db (mana/add-mana db :player-1 {:colorless 1 :blue 1})
          modes (rules/get-casting-modes db :player-1 obj-id)
          flashback-mode (first modes)
          db-cast (rules/cast-spell-mode db :player-1 obj-id flashback-mode)
          ;; Counter = move off stack without resolving
          db-countered (rules/move-spell-off-stack db-cast :player-1 obj-id)]
      (is (= :exile (:object/zone (q/get-object db-countered obj-id)))
          "Countered flashback spell should go to exile"))))

(deftest normal-spell-countered-goes-to-graveyard-test
  (testing "Countered normal spell goes to graveyard"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          [obj-id db] (add-card-to-zone db :player-1 deep-analysis :hand)
          db (mana/add-mana db :player-1 {:colorless 3 :blue 1})
          modes (rules/get-casting-modes db :player-1 obj-id)
          primary-mode (first modes)
          db-cast (rules/cast-spell-mode db :player-1 obj-id primary-mode)
          db-countered (rules/move-spell-off-stack db-cast :player-1 obj-id)]
      (is (= :graveyard (:object/zone (q/get-object db-countered obj-id)))
          "Countered normal spell should go to graveyard"))))
```

### 3. Implementation checklist

- [ ] Create src/main/fizzle/cards/deep_analysis.cljs with card definition:
  ```clojure
  (ns fizzle.cards.deep-analysis)

  (def deep-analysis
    {:card/id :deep-analysis
     :card/name "Deep Analysis"
     :card/cmc 4
     :card/mana-cost {:colorless 3 :blue 1}
     :card/colors #{:blue}
     :card/types #{:sorcery}
     :card/text "Target player draws two cards. Flashback—{1}{U}, Pay 3 life."
     :card/effects [{:effect/type :draw
                     :effect/amount 2
                     :effect/target :any-player}]
     :card/alternate-costs [{:alternate/id :flashback
                             :alternate/zone :graveyard
                             :alternate/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :blue 1}
                             :alternate/additional-costs [{:cost/type :pay-life :cost/amount 3}]
                             :alternate/on-resolve :exile}]})
  ```

- [ ] Add move-spell-off-stack function to src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs:
  ```clojure
  (defn move-spell-off-stack
    "Remove a spell from stack without resolving (counter, fizzle).
     Uses cast mode's :mode/on-resolve for destination if present.
     Otherwise sends to graveyard."
    [db _player-id object-id]
    (let [obj (q/get-object db object-id)]
      (if (not= :stack (:object/zone obj))
        db  ; No-op if not on stack
        (let [cast-mode (:object/cast-mode obj)
              mode-destination (:mode/on-resolve cast-mode)
              destination (or mode-destination :graveyard)]
          (zones/move-to-zone db object-id destination)))))
  ```

- [ ] Create test file src/test/fizzle/cards/deep_analysis_test.cljs
- [ ] Run tests: make test
- [ ] Run validation: make validate
- [ ] Commit changes

## Success Criteria
- [ ] deep-analysis-card-definition-test passes (card has correct structure)
- [ ] deep-analysis-cast-from-hand-test passes (castable from hand for 3U)
- [ ] deep-analysis-flashback-cast-test passes (castable from gy for 1U + 3 life)
- [ ] flashback-spell-countered-exiles-test passes (countered flashback exiles)
- [ ] normal-spell-countered-goes-to-graveyard-test passes (countered normal goes to gy)
- [ ] All existing tests pass: make test
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass: make validate

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Spell fizzles (no valid targets)**
- Deep Analysis targets "any player" - always has valid target (controller)
- move-spell-off-stack handles both counter and fizzle cases
- Uses same destination logic as resolve-spell (mode's on-resolve)

**Edge Case: Card definition already exists**
- Check if deep-analysis card-id exists before adding
- Tests should be idempotent - add-card-to-zone handles this

**Edge Case: nil cast-mode**
- move-spell-off-stack checks (:mode/on-resolve cast-mode)
- If cast-mode is nil, mode-destination is nil, defaults to :graveyard
- This handles spells cast via old cast-spell (no mode tracking)

**Reference Implementation**
- Study careful-study in iggy_pop.cljs for draw effect pattern
- Study resolve-spell in rules.cljs for destination logic pattern

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO hardcoding "Deep Analysis" checks - use generic :object/cast-mode
- ❌ NO separate counter path for flashback - check mode on any counter/fizzle
- ❌ NO adding deep-analysis to iggy_pop.cljs - create separate file for non-Iggy cards
- ❌ NO modifying existing tests - only add new tests
close_reason: Closed

title: Feature: Event-Driven Trigger System
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Game engine produces events when game state changes (phase entry, tapping, zone changes)
- Cards register triggers dynamically when entering zones where triggers are active
- Cards unregister triggers when leaving those zones
- Triggers match against events and fire when conditions are met
- Turn-based actions (untap, draw, cleanup) are modeled as game-rule triggers
- Turn-based actions execute immediately (no stack), card triggers use the stack
- Draw step automatically draws a card (skip turn 1)
- Triggers can be active from any zone (battlefield, graveyard, library, etc.)
- Existing untap logic refactored to use new system

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Event bus produces events for phase entry, permanent tapping, zone changes
- [ ] Triggers register/unregister dynamically on zone entry/exit
- [ ] Turn-based actions fire before card triggers
- [ ] Draw step draws a card automatically (skipped on turn 1)
- [ ] Untap step untaps permanents via new trigger system (refactored from start-turn)
- [ ] City of Brass trigger migrated to event-driven system
- [ ] Card triggers go on stack, turn-based actions don't
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] New tests for event bus and trigger registration
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO scanning all battlefield cards after every action (use event subscription)
- ❌ NO hardcoded phase logic in advance-phase/start-turn (use registered triggers)
- ❌ NO battlefield-only trigger assumption (must support graveyard, library, etc.)
- ❌ NO breaking existing functionality (City of Brass must still work)

## Approach
Replace current scan-based trigger system with event-driven pub/sub architecture. Game actions produce events, triggers subscribe to event types they care about. Turn-based actions and card triggers unified under same event system with different resolution modes (immediate vs stack).

## Architecture
### New Files
- src/main/fizzle/engine/events.cljs - Event bus, event production
- src/main/fizzle/engine/trigger-registry.cljs - Dynamic trigger registration

### Modified Files
- src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs - Refactor to use event matching
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs - Produce events on phase change, call event bus
- src/main/fizzle/engine/zones.cljs - Register/unregister triggers on zone change

### Event Structure
```clojure
{:event/type :phase-entered
 :event/phase :draw
 :event/turn 2
 :event/player :player-1}

{:event/type :permanent-tapped
 :event/object-id <uuid>
 :event/controller :player-1}

{:event/type :zone-change
 :event/object-id <uuid>
 :event/from-zone :library
 :event/to-zone :graveyard}
```

### Trigger Structure
```clojure
{:trigger/id <uuid>
 :trigger/source :game-rule | <object-id>
 :trigger/event-type :phase-entered
 :trigger/filter {:phase :draw}
 :trigger/effects [...]
 :trigger/uses-stack? false
 :trigger/active-zones #{:battlefield}}
```

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Current trigger system scans all battlefield cards after every action. This doesn't scale and can't handle triggers from other zones (Gaea's Blessing in graveyard). Also, turn-based actions are scattered across functions rather than unified.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- engine/triggers.cljs:54-114 - Current multimethod check/resolve pattern
- engine/triggers.cljs:179-211 - fire-matching-triggers scans battlefield
- events/game.cljs:188-200 - advance-phase just changes phase value
- events/game.cljs:221-236 - start-turn has inline untap logic
- engine/state-based.cljs - Registry pattern example we can follow

**Trigger Types Needed:**
- :phase-entered - for upkeep/draw/end step triggers
- :permanent-tapped - for City of Brass style triggers
- :land-tapped-for-mana - for Mana Flare style triggers
- :zone-change - for Gaea's Blessing, Emrakul style triggers

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Event-Driven Pub/Sub System ✓

**What it is:** Cards register triggers to an event bus when entering active zones. Game produces events, triggers subscribe and fire when matching events occur.

**Pros:**
- Scalable (no scanning)
- Supports any-zone triggers
- Unifies turn-based actions and card triggers
- Matches MTG's event-driven nature

**Cons:**
- Larger refactor than minimal fix
- Need to migrate existing triggers

**Chosen because:** Correct architecture for MTG trigger system, enables future cards

#### 2. Enhanced Scan-Based System ❌

**What it is:** Keep scanning but add event matching instead of state diff.

**Why we looked at this:** Less invasive change

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Still O(n) scan after every action, can't handle non-battlefield triggers cleanly

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Performance profiling shows event registration is actually slower

#### 3. Minimal Draw Step Fix ❌

**What it is:** Just add draw logic inline to advance-phase, deal with proper system later.

**Why we looked at this:** Quick fix for immediate need

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Would be thrown away immediately when building proper system

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Never - user explicitly rejected this approach

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Event bus and production
- Trigger registry with zone-aware registration
- Phase-entered, permanent-tapped, zone-change events
- Turn-based actions (draw, untap, cleanup)
- City of Brass migration to new system

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Mana Flare style global land triggers (add when card implemented)
- Combat damage triggers (add when combat implemented)
- Death triggers (add when destroy effects implemented)

### Open Questions
- Should event history be retained for debugging/replay? (decide during implementation)
- How to handle replacement effects that modify events? (defer until needed)

## Design Discovery

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Automatic or manual draw? | Automatic | Draw fires as turn-based action on phase entry |
| Skip turn 1 draw? | Yes, matches MTG rules | Turn-based actions need access to turn number |
| Full system or minimal fix? | Full system now | Build event-driven architecture, not a hack |
| Turn-based before or after card triggers? | Turn-based first | Two-phase resolution: immediate actions, then stack |
| Refactor untap? | Yes, unify everything | All phase actions go through same system |
| Dynamic registration? | Yes, zone-aware | Register on zone entry, unregister on zone exit |
| Battlefield only? | No, any zone | Gaea's Blessing needs library/graveyard triggers |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Current Trigger System Analysis
**Question explored:** How do triggers currently work?
**Sources consulted:**
- engine/triggers.cljs - Full file review

**Findings:**
- Multimethod dispatch on :trigger/type for check and resolve
- fire-matching-triggers scans ALL battlefield objects
- Two functions: fire-matching-triggers (stack) vs fire-and-resolve-triggers (immediate)
- :becomes-tapped compares old-db vs new-db state

**Conclusion:** Good pattern with multimethod dispatch, but scan-based approach won't scale

#### State-Based Actions Registry Pattern
**Question explored:** Is there a registry pattern to follow?
**Sources consulted:**
- engine/state-based.cljs

**Findings:**
- Uses atom-based registry: (defonce sbas (atom {}))
- register-sba! adds handlers
- check-and-execute-sbas iterates registry

**Conclusion:** Can use similar pattern for trigger registry
close_reason: All success criteria met, automated review passed, LIFO stack bug fixed

title: Create player library on game init
design:
## Goal
Initialize player's 60-card shuffled library at game start so draw effect works across multiple turns.

## Context
Epic success criterion: 'Player starts with 7-card hand drawn from shuffled 60-card library'
Currently ::init-game creates opponent library (40 cards) but NOT player library.
Player starts with hardcoded 3-card hand instead of drawing from library.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours (TDD cycle with 6 tests + implementation)

## Files to Modify
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs - Modify ::init-game event handler
- src/test/fizzle/events/init_game_test.cljs - NEW: Test file for game initialization

## Implementation Checklist
1. [ ] Create test file src/test/fizzle/events/init_game_test.cljs
2. [ ] RED: Write test_player_library_has_60_cards_after_init
   - Call init-game, query player's library zone
   - Assert count = 60
3. [ ] GREEN: Add library creation to ::init-game
   - Create make-test-deck function: 12x each of dark-ritual, cabal-ritual, brain-freeze, city-of-brass, gemstone-mine
   - Use (shuffle ...) to randomize order
   - Create objects with :object/zone :library and :object/position 0-59
4. [ ] RED: Write test_player_library_cards_have_correct_distribution
   - Query library cards by type
   - Assert 12x each of 5 card types
5. [ ] GREEN: Verify deck composition is correct
6. [ ] RED: Write test_player_starts_with_7_card_hand
   - Call init-game, query player's hand zone
   - Assert count = 7
7. [ ] GREEN: After creating library, call draw effect 7 times OR create 7 hand objects from top of library
8. [ ] RED: Write test_library_has_53_cards_after_initial_draw
   - Assert library count = 53 (60 - 7)
9. [ ] REFACTOR: Remove hardcoded 3-card hand creation (lines 59-65 in game.cljs)
10. [ ] VERIFY: make test passes, no hardcoded starting hands remain

## Pattern to Follow
Opponent library creation in game.cljs lines 66-70:
```clojure
;; Create opponent library (40 cards so mill has targets)
(d/transact! conn (vec (for [i (range 40)]
                         {:object/id (random-uuid) :object/card dr-eid :object/zone :library
                          :object/owner opp-eid :object/controller opp-eid
                          :object/tapped false :object/position i})))
```

## Test Deck Composition
Per epic requirements:
- 12x City of Brass (land)
- 12x Gemstone Mine (land)
- 12x Dark Ritual (instant)
- 12x Cabal Ritual (instant)
- 12x Brain Freeze (instant)
Total: 60 cards

## Key Considerations

### Shuffle Testing Approach
ClojureScript's shuffle uses Math.random() which is non-deterministic.
- Do NOT test for specific shuffled order (flaky)
- DO test that library contains correct cards (order-independent)
- OPTIONALLY: Test two shuffles produce different orders (statistically likely but not guaranteed)
- Acceptable: Trust ClojureScript's shuffle implementation

### Drawing Initial Hand
Two approaches:
1. Use existing draw effect 7 times (preferred - exercises existing code)
2. Create hand objects directly during init (faster but bypasses draw)

Recommendation: Create library first, then use draw effect loop for initial 7 cards.
This ensures draw effect is tested and works correctly.

### Code to Remove
MUST remove hardcoded hand creation (game.cljs lines 59-65):
```clojure
;; Create game objects (3 cards in hand) - REMOVE THIS
(d/transact! conn [{:object/id (random-uuid) :object/card dr-eid :object/zone :hand ...}
                   {:object/id (random-uuid) :object/card cr-eid :object/zone :hand ...}
                   {:object/id (random-uuid) :object/card bf-eid :object/zone :hand ...}])
```

### Card Entity ID Lookup
Need to look up all 5 card entity IDs, not just 3:
```clojure
(let [dr-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?cid :where [?e :card/id ?cid]] db :dark-ritual)
      cr-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?cid :where [?e :card/id ?cid]] db :cabal-ritual)
      bf-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?cid :where [?e :card/id ?cid]] db :brain-freeze)
      cob-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?cid :where [?e :card/id ?cid]] db :city-of-brass)
      gm-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?cid :where [?e :card/id ?cid]] db :gemstone-mine)]
  ...)
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Player library created with 60 cards on game init
- [ ] Library contains correct distribution: 12x each of 5 card types
- [ ] Player starts with 7-card hand drawn from library
- [ ] Library has 53 cards remaining after initial draw
- [ ] No hardcoded starting hands in codebase
- [ ] All tests in init_game_test.cljs pass
- [ ] make test passes (all project tests)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoded starting hands (epic anti-pattern)
- ❌ NO deterministic deck order (must shuffle)
- ❌ NO skipping the draw step (hand comes from library via draw)
- ❌ NO tests that depend on specific shuffle order (flaky)
- ❌ NO leaving old 3-card hand code in place
close_reason: Player library now created with 60 shuffled cards (12x each of 5 card types). 7-card opening hand drawn from library. Tests verify total deck = 60, correct distribution, hand = 7, library = 53.

title: Feature: Ray of Revelation with destroy effect
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Ray of Revelation card implemented with oracle-accurate text from Scryfall
- Flashback {G} casts from graveyard and exiles on resolution
- New :destroy effect type moves target permanent to owner's graveyard
- Seal of Cleansing implemented as test enchantment (using implement-card skill)
- Type-based targeting for enchantments on battlefield working

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Ray of Revelation passes Scryfall oracle validation
- [ ] Seal of Cleansing passes Scryfall oracle validation  
- [ ] :destroy effect moves target to owner's graveyard
- [ ] Ray can target any enchantment on battlefield
- [ ] Ray flashback works from graveyard for {G}
- [ ] Ray exiles after flashback resolution
- [ ] Seal of Cleansing can target artifact OR enchantment
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing (make validate)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoded card text (must match Scryfall oracle text exactly)
- ❌ NO generic :move-to-zone effect (use specific :destroy for semantic clarity)
- ❌ NO skipping implement-card skill for Seal of Cleansing (requires Scryfall validation)
- ❌ NO mocking zone transitions in tests (test real zone movement)
- ❌ NO controller's graveyard for destroy (must be OWNER's graveyard)

## Approach
Add :destroy effect type to effects.cljs following existing pattern of specific effect types.
Implement Ray of Revelation with type-based enchantment targeting and flashback via alternate-costs.
Implement Seal of Cleansing as test enchantment using implement-card skill for oracle accuracy.

## Architecture
- src/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs - Add :destroy effect multimethod
- src/fizzle/cards/ray_of_revelation.cljs - Card definition
- src/fizzle/cards/seal_of_cleansing.cljs - Test enchantment card
- src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs - :destroy effect tests
- src/test/fizzle/cards/ray_of_revelation_test.cljs - Card integration tests

## Design Rationale
### Problem
No destroy effect exists in the engine. Ray of Revelation needs to destroy enchantments.
Current effect types are specific (:exile-self, :sacrifice) not generic zone movement.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- effects.cljs - Uses specific effect types, not generic
- deep_analysis.cljs - Flashback pattern with :alternate/zone :graveyard
- recoup.cljs - Type-based targeting with :target/criteria
- targeting.cljs:25-31 - matches-criteria? checks :card/types

**External:**
- Scryfall API - Ray of Revelation: {1}{W} Instant, Destroy target enchantment. Flashback {G}
- Scryfall API - Seal of Cleansing: {1}{W} Enchantment, Sacrifice: Destroy target artifact or enchantment

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Specific :destroy effect ✓
**What it is:** Dedicated effect type that moves target to owner's graveyard.
**Chosen because:** Matches codebase pattern of semantic effect names.

#### 2. Generic :move-to-zone effect ❌
**What it is:** General zone movement with destination parameter.
**Why explored:** Could handle destroy, exile, bounce with one effect.
**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Breaks pattern, loses semantic meaning for game rules.
**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Refactoring entire effect system.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- :destroy effect for permanents
- Ray of Revelation with flashback
- Seal of Cleansing as test enchantment

**Out of scope:**
- Death triggers (deferred - not needed for current cards)
- Indestructible keyword (deferred)
- Regeneration (deferred)

### Open Questions
- None - design is complete

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Use case for Ray? | Both/testing purposes | Full implementation for any deck |
| Add test enchantments? | Yes, recommended | Seal of Cleansing provides test target |
| Approach? | Approach 1 - specific :destroy | Semantic effect type, not generic |

### Research Deep-Dives
#### Effect System Pattern
**Question:** How are effects implemented?
**Sources:** effects.cljs multimethod dispatch
**Conclusion:** Use specific effect types (:destroy) not generic (:move-to-zone)

### Dead-End Paths
#### Generic :move-to-zone
**Why explored:** Flexibility for future effects
**Why abandoned:** Breaks existing pattern, YAGNI

### Open Concerns Raised
- Owner vs controller graveyard → Must be owner's graveyard (MTG rules)
close_reason: Closed

title: Add turn-bar component to core.cljs
design:
## Goal
Add a turn-bar component that displays turn counter, phase strip, and Next Phase/New Turn button.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Implementation

1. Study existing patterns
   - src/fizzle/core.cljs:45-64 - mana-pool-view pattern (subscribe, render with Tailwind)
   - src/fizzle/core.cljs:145-169 - life-view pattern
   - src/fizzle/events/game.cljs:173 - phases vector for phase list

2. Add phase-indicator component
   ```clojure
   (defn phase-indicator [phase current-phase]
     [:span {:class (if (= phase current-phase)
                      "px-2 py-1 bg-blue-500 text-white rounded"
                      "px-2 py-1 text-gray-500")}
      (name phase)])
   ```

3. Add turn-bar component
   ```clojure
   (defn turn-bar []
     (let [current-phase @(rf/subscribe [::subs/current-phase])
           current-turn @(rf/subscribe [::subs/current-turn])
           at-cleanup? (= current-phase :cleanup)]
       [:div {:class "flex items-center gap-4 p-2 bg-gray-100 border-b"}
        [:span {:class "font-bold"} (str "Turn " (or current-turn 1))]
        [:div {:class "flex gap-1"}
         (for [phase events/phases]
           ^{:key phase} [phase-indicator phase current-phase])]
        [:button {:class "px-3 py-1 bg-blue-600 text-white rounded"
                  :on-click #(rf/dispatch [(if at-cleanup? ::events/start-turn ::events/advance-phase)])}
         (if at-cleanup? "New Turn" "Next Phase")]]))
   ```

4. Insert turn-bar at top of app component
   - Find app component (around line 233)
   - Add [turn-bar] before battlefield-view

5. Add require for events/phases vector if not already imported

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Turn number displays (verify: load app, see 'Turn 1')
- [ ] All 8 phases visible in strip (count: untap, upkeep, draw, main1, combat, main2, end, cleanup)
- [ ] Current phase has blue background (main1 on startup)
- [ ] Click 'Next Phase' changes highlight to next phase
- [ ] At cleanup phase, button text shows 'New Turn'
- [ ] Click 'New Turn' resets to Turn 2, untap phase
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (Edge Cases)

**Nil Subscription Values:**
- current-phase or current-turn may be nil before game initializes
- Use `(or current-turn 1)` for turn display
- Use `(or current-phase :main1)` or handle nil in phase-indicator

**Phase List Import:**
- events/phases is defined in events/game.cljs:173
- May need to require `[fizzle.events.game :as events]` in core.cljs
- If already required, verify the alias used

**Styling Consistency:**
- Follow existing Tailwind patterns in core.cljs
- Keep POC simple - no hover states or animations needed

## Anti-patterns (Task-specific)
- ❌ NO hardcoded phase list (use events/phases vector)
- ❌ NO complex conditional styling (simple bg-blue-500 for current is sufficient)
- ❌ NO error throwing on nil (gracefully handle with defaults)
close_reason: Implemented turn-bar component with phase-indicator, turn counter, and Next Phase/New Turn button. Commit a861b40.

title: Task 5: Implement Opt card with scry 1 draw 1
design:
## Goal
Create the Opt card definition that uses the scry effect system.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours (simple card definition + tests following established patterns)

## Context
All scry infrastructure is complete:
- Task 1: :scry effect type in effects.cljs
- Task 2: Scry selection state creation
- Task 3: Scry confirmation handler (library reordering)
- Task 4: Scry modal UI component

Reference files:
- src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Card definition patterns
- src/test/fizzle/cards/careful_study_test.cljs - Test patterns for instants

## Implementation

### 1. Add Opt card definition in iggy_pop.cljs

After mental-note definition (~line 262), add:

```clojure
;; Opt - Invasion cantrip
;; U - Instant: Scry 1, then draw a card.
(def opt
  {:card/id :opt
   :card/name "Opt"
   :card/cmc 1
   :card/mana-cost {:blue 1}
   :card/colors #{:blue}
   :card/types #{:instant}
   :card/text "Scry 1, then draw a card."
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :scry
                   :effect/amount 1}
                  {:effect/type :draw
                   :effect/amount 1}]})
```

### 2. Add opt to all-cards vector

Update all-cards vector (~line 306-310):

```clojure
(def all-cards
  [dark-ritual brain-freeze cabal-ritual city-of-brass city-of-traitors
   gemstone-mine island swamp underground-river lotus-petal lions-eye-diamond
   careful-study mental-note opt merchant-scroll polluted-delta
   deep-analysis/deep-analysis cephalid-coliseum/cephalid-coliseum])
```

### 3. Create test file: src/test/fizzle/cards/opt_test.cljs

Follow careful_study_test.cljs pattern:

```clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.opt-test
  "Tests for Opt with scry effect.

   Opt: U - Instant
   Scry 1, then draw a card.

   Tests verify:
   - Card definition (type, cost, effects)
   - Scry selection state created
   - Remaining draw effect preserved
   - Full resolution (scry confirm triggers draw)"
  (:require
    [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.cards.iggy-pop :as cards]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
    [fizzle.db.schema :refer [schema]]
    [fizzle.engine.rules :as rules]
    [fizzle.events.game :as game]))

;; Test helpers (reuse patterns from careful_study_test)
```

### 4. Test scenarios (specific assertions)

**test-opt-is-instant-type**
- Bug caught: Wrong card type
- Assert: (contains? (:card/types cards/opt) :instant)

**test-opt-costs-one-blue**
- Bug caught: Wrong mana cost
- Assert: (= {:blue 1} (:card/mana-cost cards/opt))

**test-opt-has-scry-then-draw-effects**
- Bug caught: Missing/wrong effect order
- Assert: First effect is :scry with amount 1, second is :draw with amount 1

**test-opt-creates-scry-selection-state**
- Bug caught: Scry effect not triggering selection
- Setup: Library with 3 cards, Opt in hand, U mana
- Action: Cast and resolve Opt
- Assert: Result has :pending-selection with :selection/type :scry and 1 card

**test-opt-scry-preserves-draw-effect**
- Bug caught: Draw effect lost during selection chaining
- Assert: (:selection/remaining-effects result) contains draw effect

**test-opt-full-resolution-draws-card**
- Bug caught: Draw not executing after scry confirm
- Setup: Library with 3 cards, Opt in hand, U mana
- Action: Cast Opt, confirm scry (put top card on bottom)
- Assert: Hand increased by 1 card (the draw)

**test-opt-empty-library-scry-still-draws-attempt**
- Bug caught: Empty library crash or skip draw
- Setup: Empty library, Opt in hand, U mana
- Action: Cast Opt
- Assert: Scry selection nil (no cards), draw executes (0 cards drawn from empty)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] opt card definition exists in iggy_pop.cljs with:
  - :card/id :opt
  - :card/name "Opt"
  - :card/mana-cost {:blue 1}
  - :card/types #{:instant}
  - :card/effects with scry 1 FIRST, draw 1 SECOND
- [ ] opt added to all-cards vector in iggy_pop.cljs
- [ ] opt_test.cljs created with 7 test cases matching specifications above
- [ ] All 7 tests pass: make test shows 0 failures
- [ ] make validate passes (lint, format, all tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Empty library when Opt resolves**
- Scry returns nil selection (no cards to scry)
- Draw still executes (draws 0 from empty library)
- No crash, just no cards drawn
- Test: test-opt-empty-library-scry-still-draws-attempt

**Edge Case: Effect ordering**
- Scry MUST happen before draw (per card text)
- Selection state for scry must preserve draw in :remaining-effects
- Already tested in scry_selection_test.cljs build-scry-selection tests

**Reference Implementation**
- careful_study_test.cljs for test setup helpers
- scry_selection_test.cljs for scry-specific behavior

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO skipping the scry step (must scry before draw)
- ❌ NO hardcoding draw without scry working
- ❌ NO using :sorcery type (Opt is instant)
- ❌ NO putting draw effect before scry effect in :card/effects
- ❌ NO tautological tests (must verify specific bug scenarios)
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement Polluted Delta fetchland
description: Implement Polluted Delta fetchland using existing tutor infrastructure. Card sacrifices self, searches library for Island or Swamp (by subtype), puts it onto battlefield tapped.
design:
## Goal
Implement Polluted Delta - a fetchland that sacrifices itself and tutors for a land with Island or Swamp subtype, putting it directly onto the battlefield tapped.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context (VERIFIED)
- Tutor infrastructure exists from Merchant Scroll (fizzle-pk2)
- :tutor effect uses :effect/target-zone (currently only :hand supported for movement)
- :sacrifice-self cost exists in costs.cljs (used by Lotus Petal, LED)
- Basic lands have :card/subtypes #{:island} or #{:swamp} (verified in iggy_pop.cljs:142,159)
- query-library-by-criteria already supports OR logic for :card/subtypes (queries.cljs:225-226)

## Key Technical Findings

**REQUIRES CODE CHANGE:**
The current execute-tutor-selection (game.cljs:810-826) moves card to target-zone but does NOT support:
1. :battlefield as target zone (move-to-zone supports it, but no tapped state handling)
2. Setting tapped state on ETB

**Solution:** Modify execute-tutor-selection to check target-zone and set :object/tapped true when zone is :battlefield and effect specifies tapped.

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Extend execute-tutor-selection for battlefield + tapped (game.cljs)

Modify execute-tutor-selection around line 820-826:

BEFORE:
```clojure
(let [card-id (first selected)
      db-after-move (zones/move-to-zone db card-id target-zone)]
  ...)
```

AFTER:
```clojure
(let [card-id (first selected)
      db-after-move (zones/move-to-zone db card-id target-zone)
      ;; If entering battlefield tapped, set tapped state
      enters-tapped? (and (= target-zone :battlefield)
                         (:selection/enters-tapped selection))
      db-with-tapped (if enters-tapped?
                       (let [obj-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?oid :where [?e :object/id ?oid]]
                                         db-after-move card-id)]
                         (d/db-with db-after-move [[:db/add obj-eid :object/tapped true]]))
                       db-after-move)]
  ...)
```

Also update build-tutor-selection (line 631-649) to pass :selection/enters-tapped from effect:
```clojure
:selection/enters-tapped (:effect/enters-tapped tutor-effect)
```

### Step 2: Implement Polluted Delta card definition (iggy_pop.cljs)

Add after merchant-scroll definition (~line 250):

```clojure
;; Polluted Delta - Fetchland
;; Land - Tap, Pay 1 life, Sacrifice: Search your library for an Island or Swamp card,
;; put it onto the battlefield, then shuffle. (Premodern: no life payment)
(def polluted-delta
  {:card/id :polluted-delta
   :card/name "Polluted Delta"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:land}
   :card/text "{T}, Sacrifice Polluted Delta: Search your library for an Island or Swamp card, put it onto the battlefield, then shuffle."
   :card/abilities [{:ability/type :activated
                     :ability/costs [{:cost/type :tap}
                                     {:cost/type :sacrifice-self}]
                     :ability/effects [{:effect/type :tutor
                                        :effect/criteria {:card/subtypes #{:island :swamp}}
                                        :effect/target-zone :battlefield
                                        :effect/enters-tapped true
                                        :effect/shuffle? true}]}]})
```

Add to all-cards vector.

### Step 3: Write tests (src/test/fizzle/cards/polluted_delta_test.cljs)

Create new test file:

```clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.polluted-delta-test
  (:require [cljs.test :refer [deftest is testing]]
            [fizzle.test-helpers :as h]))

(deftest polluted-delta-fetches-island
  (testing "Polluted Delta can fetch an Island to battlefield tapped"
    (let [game (-> (h/new-game)
                   (h/put-on-battlefield :polluted-delta)
                   (h/put-in-library :island)
                   (h/put-in-library :dark-ritual)  ; Non-target
                   (h/activate-ability :polluted-delta 0)
                   (h/select-card :island))]
      ;; Island on battlefield, tapped
      (is (h/on-battlefield? game :island))
      (is (h/tapped? game :island))
      ;; Polluted Delta sacrificed (in graveyard)
      (is (h/in-graveyard? game :polluted-delta))
      ;; Dark Ritual still in library (not selected)
      (is (h/in-library? game :dark-ritual)))))

(deftest polluted-delta-fetches-swamp
  (testing "Polluted Delta can fetch a Swamp to battlefield tapped"
    (let [game (-> (h/new-game)
                   (h/put-on-battlefield :polluted-delta)
                   (h/put-in-library :swamp)
                   (h/activate-ability :polluted-delta 0)
                   (h/select-card :swamp))]
      (is (h/on-battlefield? game :swamp))
      (is (h/tapped? game :swamp))
      (is (h/in-graveyard? game :polluted-delta)))))

(deftest polluted-delta-fail-to-find
  (testing "Polluted Delta allows fail-to-find when no valid targets"
    (let [game (-> (h/new-game)
                   (h/put-on-battlefield :polluted-delta)
                   (h/put-in-library :dark-ritual)  ; No valid targets
                   (h/activate-ability :polluted-delta 0)
                   (h/select-nothing))]  ; Fail to find
      ;; Delta still sacrificed (cost paid)
      (is (h/in-graveyard? game :polluted-delta))
      ;; Library unchanged (only shuffled)
      (is (h/in-library? game :dark-ritual)))))

(deftest polluted-delta-shuffles-library
  (testing "Library is shuffled after search (even on fail-to-find)"
    ;; This tests shuffle happened - verify library order changed
    ;; Implementation: check shuffle was called via library positions
    ))

(deftest polluted-delta-selection-shows-only-valid-targets
  (testing "Selection modal only shows Island/Swamp, not other cards"
    (let [game (-> (h/new-game)
                   (h/put-on-battlefield :polluted-delta)
                   (h/put-in-library :island)
                   (h/put-in-library :swamp)
                   (h/put-in-library :dark-ritual)
                   (h/activate-ability :polluted-delta 0))
          candidates (h/get-selection-candidates game)]
      ;; Only Island and Swamp should be candidates
      (is (= 2 (count candidates)))
      (is (every? #{:island :swamp} (map :card/id candidates))))))
```

### Step 4: Verify and commit

```bash
make validate   # Must pass
# Manual test in UI: play Polluted Delta, activate, verify selection modal, verify land enters tapped
git add -A && git commit -m "Add Polluted Delta fetchland

- Extend tutor effect to support battlefield target zone with tapped state
- Add Polluted Delta card definition with sacrifice-self + tutor ability
- Add tests for fetch, fail-to-find, and selection filtering

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Polluted Delta card definition exists in iggy_pop.cljs
- [ ] Polluted Delta added to all-cards vector
- [ ] Activation requires tap + sacrifice (ability costs verified)
- [ ] Selection modal shows only Island/Swamp subtype cards
- [ ] Selected land enters battlefield TAPPED (not untapped)
- [ ] Polluted Delta is in graveyard after activation (sacrificed)
- [ ] Library shuffled after search (verify shuffle-library called)
- [ ] Fail-to-find option available and works (sacrifice still happens)
- [ ] 5+ tests pass in polluted_delta_test.cljs
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO auto-select for tutors (rules: fail-to-find must always be available)
- ❌ NO putting land onto battlefield untapped (Polluted Delta enters tapped per MTG rules)
- ❌ NO fetching non-land cards (criteria must filter by subtype)
- ❌ NO skipping sacrifice cost (sacrifice happens even if fail-to-find)
- ❌ NO hardcoding card IDs in engine code (effect system is generic)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Empty Library After Fetch**
- If library has 1 card (the target), after fetch library is empty
- Shuffle of empty library should be no-op (verified: shuffle-library handles this)

**Edge Case: No Valid Targets in Library**
- User must be shown fail-to-find option
- Sacrifice cost already paid (on stack resolution, not selection)
- Anti-pattern: auto-selecting is forbidden

**Edge Case: Polluted Delta Destroyed While Ability on Stack**
- Ability on stack still resolves (source independence)
- No special handling needed - standard MTG rules

**Edge Case: Multiple Valid Targets**
- Selection modal shows all valid options
- Player chooses exactly 1

**Testing Bug Risk: Tapped State**
- Must verify land enters TAPPED, not untapped
- Regression risk: future changes to move-to-zone could lose tapped state

**Testing Bug Risk: Sacrifice Timing**
- Sacrifice is COST, happens before effect resolves
- Delta should be in graveyard by the time selection modal appears
close_reason: Polluted Delta fetchland implemented with battlefield tutor support and enters-tapped handling

title: Task 2: Create card implementation skill with Scryfall workflow
design:
## Goal
Create .claude/skills/implement-card/SKILL.md with complete 5-phase workflow: Scryfall lookup → skeleton → implement → test → verify.

## Context
Completed Task 1: docs/card-dsl.md now exists with comprehensive DSL reference.
Discovery: DSL docs include 10 effect types, 2 ability types, 5 cost types, 6 trigger types, 1 condition type, and 12 common gotchas.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### Step 1: Create skill directory and file
```bash
mkdir -p .claude/skills/implement-card
touch .claude/skills/implement-card/SKILL.md
```

### Step 2: Write skill header with metadata
```markdown
---
name: implement-card
description: Card implementation workflow with Scryfall validation - enforces oracle-accurate implementation
---
```

Include in overview section:
- Purpose: Prevent rule hallucination via Scryfall validation
- Reference: docs/card-dsl.md (DSL reference)
- Reference: https://media.wizards.com/2026/downloads/MagicCompRules%2020260116.txt (comprehensive rules)

### Step 3: Write Phase 1 - Scryfall Lookup
Include in skill:
- WebFetch URL: `https://api.scryfall.com/cards/named?exact=CARDNAME`
- Extract from response:
  - mana_cost (string like "{1}{U}{B}")
  - cmc (number)
  - colors (array)
  - type_line (string)
  - oracle_text (string)
  - rulings_uri (URL for second fetch)
- Second WebFetch to rulings_uri, extract rulings array
- Display format: Show extracted data for user verification
- Error handling: If 404, prompt user to verify card name spelling

### Step 4: Write Phase 2 - Skeleton Generation
Include actual skeleton template:
```clojure
;; [CARD_NAME] - [TYPE_LINE]
;; [ORACLE_TEXT]
;; Scryfall verified: [DATE]
(def card-name
  {:card/id :[card-id]  ;; Scryfall: name
   :card/name "[NAME]"  ;; Scryfall: name
   :card/cmc [CMC]  ;; Scryfall: cmc
   :card/mana-cost [CONVERTED_MANA]  ;; Scryfall: mana_cost -> convert
   :card/colors #{[COLORS]}  ;; Scryfall: colors
   :card/types #{[TYPES]}  ;; Scryfall: type_line -> parse
   :card/text "[ORACLE_TEXT]"  ;; Scryfall: oracle_text
   ;; TODO: Add effects based on oracle text
   :card/effects []})
```

Include mana cost conversion table reference to docs/card-dsl.md.

### Step 5: Write Phase 3 - Implementation
Include TDD workflow:
1. Create test file: src/test/fizzle/cards/[card_name]_test.cljs
2. Write failing test based on oracle text behavior
3. Implement effects using docs/card-dsl.md effect types
4. Run: make test
5. Iterate until tests pass

Include consultation checkpoint template:
```markdown
## Consultation Checkpoint

**Card:** [Name]
**Oracle Text:** "[exact text]"
**Behavior requiring simplification:** [what oracle says]
**Current DSL support:** [what DSL can express]

**Proposed simplification:**
- Option A: [description]
- Option B: [description]

**Trade-offs:**
- [what behavior is lost]
- [why it's acceptable for practice tool]

**User decision required:** Which approach to take?
```

### Step 6: Write Phase 4 - Testing
Include test derivation rules:
- Every test MUST trace to oracle text line or Scryfall ruling
- Format: Test comment includes source
```clojure
;; Oracle: "Add {B}{B}{B}."
(deftest dark-ritual-adds-three-black-mana ...)

;; Ruling (2004-10-04): "This is a mana ability."
(deftest dark-ritual-is-mana-ability ...)
```

Anti-hallucination check: Before writing test, quote the oracle/ruling source.

### Step 7: Write Phase 5 - Verification
Include verification checklist:
- [ ] All :card/* fields match Scryfall data exactly
- [ ] CMC calculation correct
- [ ] Mana cost conversion correct (use docs/card-dsl.md table)
- [ ] All oracle text behaviors have corresponding effects
- [ ] All tests trace to oracle text or ruling (no invented rules)
- [ ] make validate passes

### Step 8: Validate and commit
Run make validate, commit skill file with message:
"Add card implementation skill with Scryfall workflow"

## Success Criteria
- [ ] .claude/skills/implement-card/SKILL.md exists
- [ ] Skill has 5 phases documented with clear instructions
- [ ] Phase 1 includes WebFetch instructions with exact URL format
- [ ] Phase 1 includes rulings_uri fetch (second request to card's rulings)
- [ ] Phase 1 includes 404 error handling guidance
- [ ] Phase 2 includes skeleton template with Scryfall comment markers
- [ ] Phase 3 includes TDD workflow steps
- [ ] Phase 3 includes consultation checkpoint template (full format shown)
- [ ] Phase 4 includes test-to-oracle traceability requirement
- [ ] Phase 5 includes verification checklist
- [ ] Comprehensive rules URL included: https://media.wizards.com/2026/downloads/MagicCompRules%2020260116.txt
- [ ] docs/card-dsl.md referenced (not duplicated)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Scryfall API Failure Modes:**
- 404 Not Found: Card name misspelled or doesn't exist. Skill must prompt user to verify spelling.
- No rulings: Some cards have empty rulings array. This is normal - proceed without ruling-based tests.
- Rate limiting: Scryfall has generous limits but skill should note to wait between bulk fetches.

**Card Name Edge Cases:**
- Special characters: "Urza's Bauble" (apostrophe), "Fire // Ice" (split card), "Ach! Hans, Run!" (punctuation)
- URL encode card names before fetching
- Split cards return both halves - may need to specify which half

**Mana Cost Edge Cases:**
- Variable costs: {X}, {X}{X} - document how to handle
- Hybrid mana: {W/U} - document conversion
- Phyrexian mana: {W/P} - document conversion
- Snow mana: {S} - document conversion
- If DSL doesn't support format, require consultation checkpoint

**Oracle Text Complexity:**
- Multi-paragraph oracle text: Each ability should be separate effect
- Modal spells ("Choose one") - may require consultation if DSL doesn't support
- Triggered vs activated abilities - skill should help distinguish

**Reference Implementation:**
- Study existing cards in src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs
- Use Dark Ritual as simple example
- Use Polluted Delta as complex example (activated ability with tutor)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoded card data (must fetch from Scryfall at runtime)
- ❌ NO DSL reference duplication (reference docs/card-dsl.md, don't copy content)
- ❌ NO skipping consultation for unsupported behaviors (user must approve simplifications)
- ❌ NO tests without oracle/ruling citation (prevents rule hallucination)
- ❌ NO assuming card attributes (always verify against Scryfall response)
- ❌ NO skipping rulings fetch (even if empty, must check)
