title: Migrate City of Brass trigger to event system and remove dead code
design:
## Goal

Migrate City of Brass :becomes-tapped trigger from scanning-based system to event-based system, then delete the now-dead scanning code.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study Existing Patterns

**Event system (use this):**
- trigger_registry.cljs:12-23 - register-trigger! pattern
- trigger_registry.cljs:39-54 - unregister-by-source! on zone leave
- trigger_dispatch.cljs:34-57 - dispatch-event flow
- events.cljs:25-37 - permanent-tapped-event exists

**Old system (delete this):**
- triggers.cljs:220-253 - fire-matching-triggers
- triggers.cljs:274-310 - fire-and-resolve-triggers
- triggers.cljs:74-83 - check-trigger :becomes-tapped
- triggers.cljs:106-117 - resolve-trigger :becomes-tapped
- game.cljs:529 - call to fire-matching-triggers

### 2. Register City of Brass Trigger on ETB

In game.cljs play-land or a new registration helper:

\`\`\`clojure
;; When City of Brass enters battlefield, register its trigger
(defn register-card-triggers!
  [object-id controller-id card]
  (doseq [trigger (:card/triggers card)]
    (registry/register-trigger!
      {:trigger/id (random-uuid)
       :trigger/event-type :permanent-tapped
       :trigger/source object-id
       :trigger/controller controller-id
       :trigger/filter {:event/object-id :self}  ; only when THIS permanent taps
       :trigger/uses-stack? true
       :trigger/effects (:trigger/effects trigger)
       :trigger/description (:trigger/description trigger)})))
\`\`\`

### 3. Dispatch Event on Tap

In activate-mana-ability (game.cljs), after tapping:

\`\`\`clojure
;; Replace fire-matching-triggers with:
(dispatch/dispatch-event db (game-events/permanent-tapped-event object-id player-id))
\`\`\`

### 4. Add resolve-trigger :permanent-tapped

In triggers.cljs:

\`\`\`clojure
(defmethod resolve-trigger :permanent-tapped
  [db trigger]
  ;; Same logic as old :becomes-tapped resolver
  (let [controller (:trigger/controller trigger)
        effects-list (:trigger/effects trigger)]
    (reduce (fn [db' effect]
              (let [resolved-effect (if (= :controller (:effect/target effect))
                                      (assoc effect :effect/target controller)
                                      effect)]
                (effects/execute-effect db' controller resolved-effect)))
            db
            effects-list)))
\`\`\`

### 5. Unregister Triggers on Zone Leave

Ensure triggers are unregistered when permanent leaves battlefield:

\`\`\`clojure
;; In zone change handling (or sacrifice/destroy):
(registry/unregister-by-source! object-id)
\`\`\`

### 6. Delete Dead Code

**triggers.cljs - Remove:**
- Lines 58-71: check-trigger multimethod and :default
- Lines 74-83: check-trigger :becomes-tapped
- Lines 106-117: resolve-trigger :becomes-tapped
- Lines 212-253: fire-matching-triggers and get-battlefield-objects helper
- Lines 274-310: fire-and-resolve-triggers

**game.cljs - Remove:**
- Line 529: call to triggers/fire-matching-triggers
- Remove fire-matching-triggers from require if no other usages

### 7. Update Tests

**Delete tests for removed functions:**
- triggers_test.cljs: test-fire-matching-triggers-* (lines 238-340)

**Add tests for new event-based flow (in triggers_test.cljs or new file):**
- test-cob-trigger-registered-on-etb: Verify registry/get-all-triggers includes CoB trigger after ETB
- test-cob-trigger-unregistered-on-leave: Verify registry empty after sacrifice/destroy
- test-cob-tap-dispatches-event: Verify dispatch-event called with :permanent-tapped event type
- test-cob-trigger-deals-damage: Tap CoB, resolve trigger, assert player life reduced by 1
- test-cob-multiple-taps: Tap CoB twice, verify two triggers fire, life reduced by 2

## Success Criteria

- [ ] City of Brass trigger works via dispatch-event (verify: tap CoB, life decreases by 1)
- [ ] fire-matching-triggers function deleted: \`rg "fire-matching-triggers" src/\` returns 0 results
- [ ] fire-and-resolve-triggers function deleted: \`rg "fire-and-resolve-triggers" src/\` returns 0 results
- [ ] check-trigger multimethod deleted: \`rg "defmulti check-trigger" src/\` returns 0 results
- [ ] No references to deleted functions: \`rg "(fire-matching-triggers|fire-and-resolve-triggers|check-trigger)" src/\` returns 0 results
- [ ] 5+ new tests for event-based trigger flow pass
- [ ] Existing City of Brass tests still pass (if any)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Trigger Source Leaves Battlefield**
- If City of Brass is sacrificed/destroyed before trigger resolves, trigger should still resolve
- Trigger has controller-id, can still deal damage even if source gone
- Verify: sacrifice CoB with trigger on stack, damage still dealt

**Edge Case: Multiple Permanents with Same Trigger**
- Two City of Brass on battlefield, tap one
- Only the tapped one's trigger should fire (filter by :event/object-id :self)
- Verify: tap one CoB, only 1 damage dealt

**Edge Case: Rapid Multiple Taps**
- Tap same CoB multiple times (if untapped between)
- Each tap should dispatch event and add trigger
- Verify: untap, tap, untap, tap = 2 triggers = 2 damage

**Unregistration on Zone Leave**
- CRITICAL: Must unregister triggers when permanent leaves battlefield
- If not unregistered, ghost triggers remain in registry
- Verify: sacrifice CoB, registry/get-all-triggers no longer contains its trigger

**Reference Implementation**
- Study how turn-based triggers use dispatch-event in turn_based.cljs
- Follow same pattern for card triggers

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO leaving check-trigger or fire-matching-triggers code (even commented out)
- ❌ NO registering triggers without corresponding unregister on zone leave
- ❌ NO hardcoded object-ids in tests (use dynamic test fixture IDs)
- ❌ NO skipping unregister verification test (ghost triggers cause bugs)
- ❌ NO modifying City of Brass card definition (card data stays same, only engine changes)
close_reason: Closed

title: Manual mana allocation for generic costs
description: Implement MTGO-style mana payment: auto-deduct colored requirements, then let the player click mana in their pool to allocate generic costs. Currently auto-pays generic from largest pool which removes player agency on mana decisions (e.g., saving blue for Brain Freeze while spending black on generic). Phase 1 auto-pay is acceptable stopgap.

title: Task 1: Extend casting rules with alternate cost modes
design:
## Goal
Extend the casting system to support alternate casting modes. Cards can define `:card/alternate-costs` and the engine finds all valid modes based on card's current zone.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- `src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs:16-29` - Current `can-cast?` implementation
- `src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs:76-94` - Current `cast-spell` implementation  
- `src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs` - Cost checking/payment patterns (`:pay-life` already exists)
- `src/main/fizzle/engine/mana.cljs` - Mana payment patterns
- `src/test/fizzle/engine/rules_test.cljs` - Existing test patterns to follow

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

Add to `src/test/fizzle/engine/rules_test.cljs`:

```clojure
;; === Alternate casting modes tests ===

;; get-casting-modes tests
(deftest get-casting-modes-primary-only-hand-test
  (testing "get-casting-modes returns primary mode for card in hand without alternates"
    ;; Setup: Dark Ritual in hand (no alternate costs)
    ;; Assert: Returns vector with one mode {:mode/id :primary ...}
    ;; Bug caught: If returns empty, cards can't be cast at all
    ))

(deftest get-casting-modes-no-modes-wrong-zone-test
  (testing "get-casting-modes returns empty for card in graveyard without flashback"
    ;; Setup: Dark Ritual in graveyard
    ;; Assert: Returns empty vector
    ;; Bug caught: If returns mode, all cards could be cast from graveyard
    ))

(deftest get-casting-modes-flashback-in-graveyard-test
  (testing "get-casting-modes returns flashback mode for flashback card in graveyard"
    ;; Setup: Deep Analysis (with flashback) in graveyard
    ;; Assert: Returns vector with flashback mode {:mode/id :flashback ...}
    ;; Bug caught: If returns empty, flashback cards can't be cast from GY
    ))

(deftest get-casting-modes-flashback-not-in-hand-test
  (testing "get-casting-modes does NOT return flashback mode when card in hand"
    ;; Setup: Deep Analysis in hand
    ;; Assert: Returns only primary mode, not flashback
    ;; Bug caught: If returns flashback, cards could pay flashback cost from hand
    ))

(deftest get-casting-modes-multiple-modes-test
  (testing "get-casting-modes returns both modes for card with hand-alternate"
    ;; Setup: Gush-like card with {:alternate/zone :hand} in hand
    ;; Assert: Returns two modes (primary + alternate)
    ;; Bug caught: If returns only one, player can't choose casting mode
    ))

;; can-cast-mode? tests
(deftest can-cast-mode-sufficient-mana-test
  (testing "can-cast-mode? returns true when player has sufficient mana"
    ;; Setup: Player has {B}, mode requires {B}
    ;; Assert: Returns true
    ;; Bug caught: Basic mana check regression
    ))

(deftest can-cast-mode-insufficient-mana-test
  (testing "can-cast-mode? returns false when player lacks mana"
    ;; Setup: Player has {}, mode requires {B}
    ;; Assert: Returns false
    ;; Bug caught: If true, spells cast without mana
    ))

(deftest can-cast-mode-with-life-cost-test
  (testing "can-cast-mode? checks additional life cost"
    ;; Setup: Player has 3+ life, mode has :pay-life 3
    ;; Assert: Returns true
    ;; Bug caught: If additional costs ignored, flashback is free
    ))

(deftest can-cast-mode-insufficient-life-test
  (testing "can-cast-mode? returns false when can't pay life cost"
    ;; Setup: Player has 2 life, mode has :pay-life 3
    ;; Assert: Returns false
    ;; Bug caught: If true, player can pay life they don't have
    ))

;; Edge case tests
(deftest get-casting-modes-nil-alternate-costs-test
  (testing "get-casting-modes handles nil alternate-costs gracefully"
    ;; Setup: Card without :card/alternate-costs key
    ;; Assert: Returns primary mode only, no error
    ;; Bug caught: NPE on cards without alternate costs
    ))

(deftest get-casting-modes-empty-alternate-costs-test
  (testing "get-casting-modes handles empty alternate-costs vector"
    ;; Setup: Card with :card/alternate-costs []
    ;; Assert: Returns primary mode only
    ;; Bug caught: Empty vector treated differently than nil
    ))

(deftest can-cast-mode-empty-mana-cost-test
  (testing "can-cast-mode? returns true for zero mana cost mode"
    ;; Setup: Mode with :mode/mana-cost {}
    ;; Assert: Returns true (free spell)
    ;; Bug caught: Empty cost map treated as unpayable
    ))

;; Backwards compatibility
(deftest can-cast-unchanged-for-existing-cards-test
  (testing "can-cast? behavior unchanged for cards without alternate costs"
    ;; Run same tests as existing can-cast tests
    ;; Bug caught: Regression in existing functionality
    ))
```

### 3. Implementation checklist

- [ ] `rules.cljs` - Add `get-primary-mode` helper (returns mode map for normal casting)
- [ ] `rules.cljs` - Add `get-alternate-modes` helper (filters alternates by current zone)
- [ ] `rules.cljs` - Add `get-casting-modes` function:
  ```clojure
  (defn get-casting-modes
    "Returns all valid casting modes for a card based on its current zone.
     Each mode is a map with :mode/id, :mode/mana-cost, :mode/additional-costs, :mode/on-resolve"
    [db player-id object-id]
    ...)
  ```
- [ ] `rules.cljs` - Add `can-cast-mode?` function:
  ```clojure
  (defn can-cast-mode?
    "Check if player can pay all costs for a specific casting mode.
     Checks mana cost AND all additional costs."
    [db player-id object-id mode]
    ...)
  ```
- [ ] `rules.cljs` - Update `can-cast?` to use `get-casting-modes`:
  ```clojure
  ;; Backwards compatible: true if ANY mode is castable
  (defn can-cast? [db player-id object-id]
    (let [modes (get-casting-modes db player-id object-id)]
      (some #(can-cast-mode? db player-id object-id %) modes)))
  ```
- [ ] `rules.cljs` - Add `get-castable-cards` function:
  ```clojure
  (defn get-castable-cards
    "Returns all cards (from hand and graveyard) with at least one valid casting mode."
    [db player-id]
    ...)
  ```
- [ ] `rules_test.cljs` - All 12+ tests listed above

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `get-casting-modes` returns primary mode for hand cards
- [ ] `get-casting-modes` returns flashback mode for graveyard cards with flashback
- [ ] `get-casting-modes` returns empty vector for cards in wrong zone without alternates
- [ ] `get-casting-modes` returns multiple modes when applicable (Gush pattern)
- [ ] `can-cast-mode?` validates mana cost via `mana/can-pay?`
- [ ] `can-cast-mode?` validates additional costs via `costs/can-pay?`
- [ ] `can-cast-mode?` returns false when any cost is unpayable
- [ ] Existing `can-cast?` tests pass unchanged (backwards compatible)
- [ ] All 12+ new tests pass: `make test`
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass: `make validate`

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Card without mana cost**
- Some cards may have nil or missing `:card/mana-cost`
- Treat as zero cost `{}` (e.g., Lotus Petal pattern)
- Test: `get-casting-modes-nil-mana-cost-test`

**Edge Case: Empty alternate-costs**
- Card may have `:card/alternate-costs []` (empty vector)
- Must return primary mode only, not fail
- Treat same as nil

**Edge Case: Unknown additional cost type**
- If `:cost/type` not recognized by `costs/can-pay?`
- Current costs.cljs throws - let it bubble up (fail-fast)
- Document this behavior, fix in future task if needed

**Edge Case: Invalid player-id**
- If player-id doesn't exist, return empty modes
- Follow pattern from existing `can-cast?` which returns nil/false

**Backwards Compatibility**
- Existing cards have no `:card/alternate-costs` key
- `get-casting-modes` must handle missing key gracefully
- All existing `can-cast?` tests must continue passing

**Reference Implementation**
- Study `rules.cljs:16-29` for `can-cast?` pattern
- Study `costs.cljs:167-206` for `:pay-life` pattern

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO hardcoding specific cards (Deep Analysis, Gush) - use generic alternate-costs structure
- ❌ NO breaking existing can-cast? tests - backwards compatibility required
- ❌ NO adding new effect types - this task is casting rules only
- ❌ NO modifying stack resolution - that's a separate task
- ❌ NO modifying UI - this task is engine only
close_reason: Implemented get-casting-modes, can-cast-mode?, get-castable-cards. All 15 new tests pass. Pre-commit hooks pass.

title: Add schema fields and new effect types
design:
## Goal
Add foundational schema fields and effect types needed for data-driven ability system.

## Context
First task of epic fizzle-dg4. Establishes data structures before implementing ability/cost/trigger systems.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation Steps

### 1. RED: Write tests for :add-counters effect type
File: src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs (add to existing)

```clojure
(deftest test-add-counters-to-permanent
  (testing "add-counters sets counters on permanent with no existing counters"
    ;; Setup: permanent on battlefield with nil :object/counters
    ;; Action: execute {:effect/type :add-counters :effect/counters {:mining 3}}
    ;; Assert: permanent has {:mining 3} in :object/counters
    ))

(deftest test-add-counters-merges-existing
  (testing "add-counters merges with existing counters (does not overwrite)"
    ;; Setup: permanent with {:charge 2} counters
    ;; Action: execute {:effect/type :add-counters :effect/counters {:mining 3}}
    ;; Assert: permanent has {:charge 2 :mining 3}
    ))

(deftest test-add-counters-increments-same-type
  (testing "add-counters increments existing counter of same type"
    ;; Setup: permanent with {:mining 1} counters
    ;; Action: execute {:effect/type :add-counters :effect/counters {:mining 2}}
    ;; Assert: permanent has {:mining 3}
    ))
```

### 2. GREEN: Implement :add-counters in engine/effects.cljs

```clojure
(defmethod execute-effect :add-counters [db player-id effect]
  ;; :effect/target - object-id to add counters to (required)
  ;; :effect/counters - map of counter-type to amount {:mining 3}
  ;; Merges counters, incrementing existing types
  )
```

Reference: Follow pattern at engine/effects.cljs:38-59 (:mill effect)

### 3. RED: Write tests for :deal-damage effect type
File: src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs (add to existing)

```clojure
(deftest test-deal-damage-reduces-life
  (testing "deal-damage reduces target player life total"
    ;; Setup: player at 20 life
    ;; Action: execute {:effect/type :deal-damage :effect/amount 3 :effect/target :player-1}
    ;; Assert: player at 17 life
    ))

(deftest test-deal-damage-zero-amount
  (testing "deal-damage with 0 amount does nothing"
    ;; Setup: player at 20 life
    ;; Action: execute {:effect/type :deal-damage :effect/amount 0 :effect/target :player-1}
    ;; Assert: player still at 20 life
    ))

(deftest test-deal-damage-preserves-source
  (testing "deal-damage effect includes source for future damage prevention"

    ;; Source field exists for future damage prevention implementation
    ;; This test documents the interface, actual prevention is future work
    ))
```

### 4. GREEN: Implement :deal-damage in engine/effects.cljs

```clojure
(defmethod execute-effect :deal-damage [db player-id effect]
  ;; :effect/amount - damage amount (integer)
  ;; :effect/target - player-id receiving damage
  ;; :effect/source - object-id dealing damage (optional, for future prevention)
  ;; For now: identical to :lose-life, but separate for future damage prevention
  )
```

### 5. Update db/schema.cljs
Add two new fields after :card/abilities (line 25):

```clojure
:card/etb-effects  {}  ; EDN vector of effects to execute on entering battlefield
:card/triggers     {}  ; EDN vector of triggered abilities {:trigger/type :becomes-tapped ...}
```

### 6. VERIFY
- Run: make validate (lint + format + tests)
- Confirm: All 146+ existing tests still pass
- Confirm: New tests pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] test-add-counters-to-permanent passes (catches: counters not set)
- [ ] test-add-counters-merges-existing passes (catches: overwrite bug)
- [ ] test-add-counters-increments-same-type passes (catches: replacement vs increment)
- [ ] test-deal-damage-reduces-life passes (catches: effect not implemented)
- [ ] test-deal-damage-zero-amount passes (catches: edge case handling)
- [ ] :card/etb-effects field in schema (verified: field exists in schema map)
- [ ] :card/triggers field in schema (verified: field exists in schema map)
- [ ] make validate passes (all tests, lint, format)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO mutating counters in place (use Datascript transactions)
- ❌ NO skipping nil/empty counter handling (must handle {:object/counters nil})
- ❌ NO conflating :deal-damage with :lose-life implementation (keep separate methods)
- ❌ NO tests that only verify syntax/existence (tests must catch specific bugs)

## Key Considerations (Edge Cases)

**Nil Counters Map**:
- Permanent may have nil :object/counters (never had counters)
- :add-counters must handle nil → {:mining 3}, not throw

**Counter Merge Semantics**:

**Damage vs Life Loss Distinction**:

**Object Target for Counters**:

- Study engine/effects.cljs:113-133 (:lose-life effect) for life modification pattern

title: Task 3: Zone Transitions (move-to-zone)
design:
## Goal

Implement zone transitions: moving game objects between zones (hand, stack, graveyard, battlefield, library, exile). This is foundational for cast-spell and resolve-spell.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Context
Completed Task 2: Mana system (add, can-pay?, pay-mana).
Discovery: Datascript d/db-with returns new immutable db. Queries use pull for entity data with references.

## Implementation

### 1. Write tests first (TDD)

```clojure
;; test/fizzle/engine/zones_test.cljs

(ns fizzle.engine.zones-test
  (:require [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
            [datascript.core :as d]
            [fizzle.db.init :refer [init-game-state]]
            [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
            [fizzle.engine.zones :as zones]))

;; === move-to-zone tests ===

(deftest move-to-zone-hand-to-stack-test
  (testing "move-to-zone moves object from hand to stack"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))
          db' (zones/move-to-zone db object-id :stack)]
      (is (= :stack (:object/zone (q/get-object db' object-id))))
      (is (empty? (q/get-hand db' :player-1)))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :stack)))))))

(deftest move-to-zone-stack-to-graveyard-test
  (testing "move-to-zone moves object from stack to graveyard"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))
          db' (-> db
                  (zones/move-to-zone object-id :stack)
                  (zones/move-to-zone object-id :graveyard))]
      (is (= :graveyard (:object/zone (q/get-object db' object-id))))
      (is (empty? (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :stack)))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :graveyard)))))))

(deftest move-to-zone-preserves-other-attributes-test
  (testing "move-to-zone only changes zone, not owner/controller/card"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))
          original-obj (q/get-object db object-id)
          db' (zones/move-to-zone db object-id :stack)
          moved-obj (q/get-object db' object-id)]
      (is (= (:object/owner original-obj) (:object/owner moved-obj)))
      (is (= (:object/controller original-obj) (:object/controller moved-obj)))
      (is (= (:object/card original-obj) (:object/card moved-obj))))))

(deftest move-to-zone-same-zone-no-op-test
  (testing "move-to-zone to same zone is a no-op"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))
          db' (zones/move-to-zone db object-id :hand)]
      (is (= :hand (:object/zone (q/get-object db' object-id))))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-hand db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest move-to-zone-battlefield-test
  (testing "move-to-zone can move to battlefield"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))
          db' (zones/move-to-zone db object-id :battlefield)]
      (is (= :battlefield (:object/zone (q/get-object db' object-id))))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :battlefield)))))))

(deftest move-to-zone-exile-test
  (testing "move-to-zone can move to exile"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))
          db' (zones/move-to-zone db object-id :exile)]
      (is (= :exile (:object/zone (q/get-object db' object-id))))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :exile)))))))
```

### 2. Implementation checklist

- [ ] Create src/main/fizzle/engine/zones.cljs - Zone transition operations
  - (move-to-zone db object-id new-zone) -> db with object in new zone
- [ ] Create src/test/fizzle/engine/zones_test.cljs - Unit tests (6 tests)
- [ ] Run tests RED (namespace not found)
- [ ] Implement move-to-zone function
- [ ] Run tests GREEN (all 6 pass)
- [ ] Commit changes

### 3. Key implementation details

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.zones
  "Zone transition operations for Fizzle.

   All functions are pure: (db, args) -> db"
  (:require [datascript.core :as d]
            [fizzle.db.queries :as q]))

(defn move-to-zone
  "Move a game object to a new zone. Pure function: (db, args) -> db

   Valid zones: :hand, :library, :graveyard, :stack, :battlefield, :exile
   Moving to the same zone is a no-op.
   
   IMPORTANT: Caller must ensure object-id exists. If object doesn't exist,
   query returns nil and d/db-with will fail."
  [db object-id new-zone]
  (let [current-zone (:object/zone (q/get-object db object-id))]
    (if (= current-zone new-zone)
      db
      (let [obj-eid (d/q '[:find ?e .
                           :in $ ?oid
                           :where [?e :object/id ?oid]]
                         db object-id)]
        (d/db-with db [[:db/add obj-eid :object/zone new-zone]])))))
```

### 4. Verification

```bash
export PATH="/usr/local/opt/openjdk/bin:$PATH"
npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] move-to-zone moves object from hand to stack
- [ ] move-to-zone moves object from stack to graveyard
- [ ] move-to-zone preserves owner/controller/card references
- [ ] move-to-zone to same zone is no-op
- [ ] move-to-zone works for all zones (battlefield, exile)
- [ ] All 6 zone transition tests pass
- [ ] Verification command passes: `npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js`

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Nonexistent Object**
- If object-id doesn't exist, q/get-object returns nil
- Accessing `:object/zone` on nil returns nil
- Query for entity ID returns nil, d/db-with fails
- This is acceptable: invalid object-id is a programming error
- Caller (rules.cljs) must ensure object exists before calling

**Edge Case: Invalid Zone Keyword**
- No validation of zone keyword (could pass :invalid-zone)
- Datascript will accept any keyword value
- Queries for that zone will return empty results
- Rules layer should only pass valid zones; engine trusts caller

**Performance Note**
- q/get-object does a full pull with card reference
- For zone change, we only need current zone
- Could optimize with direct query if needed later
- Not a concern for MVP (single-card tests)

**Reference: Zone List**
Valid zones per design doc: :hand, :library, :graveyard, :stack, :battlefield, :exile

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO zone-specific side effects (purity: zone change is just a db update)
- ❌ NO validation of legal zone transitions (simplicity: engine trusts caller; rules layer validates)
- ❌ NO automatic triggers (scope: triggers are later; zone change is mechanical)
- ❌ NO defensive nil checks (trust caller: invalid object-id is programming error)
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 4: Migrate Deep Analysis to cast-time targeting
description:
## Goal
Migrate Deep Analysis card to use the new cast-time targeting system as a stepping stone before implementing Recoup.

## Context
Phases 1-3 complete:
- Grant system implemented (grants.cljs)
- Cleanup system implemented (expire-grants trigger)
- Targeting foundation implemented (targeting.cljs with 5 core functions)

Deep Analysis currently uses :effect/target :any-player in its effects. Need to migrate to :card/targeting for cast-time target declaration.

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Add :card/targeting to Deep Analysis
Update deep_analysis.cljs to add targeting requirement:
```clojure
:card/targeting [{:target/id :player
                  :target/type :player
                  :target/options [:self :opponent :any-player]
                  :target/required true}]
```

### Step 2: Modify casting flow for target declaration
In events/game.cljs (cast-spell event):
- After mode selection, check if card has targeting
- If so, prompt for target selection BEFORE moving to stack
- Store chosen targets on object via :object/targets

### Step 3: Update resolution to use stored targets
Modify spell resolution to:
- Read targets from :object/targets map
- Pass target to effect execution
- Remove hardcoded target selection at resolution time

### Step 4: Add fizzle logic
Add to spell resolution:
- Call all-targets-legal? before executing effects
- If false, fizzle: move card per cast mode but skip effects
- Log fizzle event for debugging

### Step 5: Update Deep Analysis tests
- Update existing tests to work with new flow
- Add test for target selection at cast time
- Add test for fizzle when player target becomes invalid (edge case)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Deep Analysis has :card/targeting attribute
- [ ] Casting Deep Analysis prompts for player target before stack
- [ ] Target stored on object, used at resolution
- [ ] Existing Deep Analysis tests pass
- [ ] New test confirms cast-time target selection
- [ ] make test passes with no failures

## Anti-Patterns
- Do NOT break existing Deep Analysis tests (migration, not rewrite)
- Do NOT modify targeting.cljs (foundation already complete)
- Do NOT implement Recoup-specific logic yet
design:
## Goal
Migrate Deep Analysis card to use the new cast-time targeting system as a stepping stone before implementing Recoup.

## Effort Estimate
8-10 hours

## Context
Phases 1-3 complete:
- Grant system implemented (grants.cljs)
- Cleanup system implemented (expire-grants trigger)
- Targeting foundation implemented (targeting.cljs with 5 core functions)

Deep Analysis currently uses :effect/target :any-player in its effects (line 19 of deep_analysis.cljs). Need to migrate to :card/targeting for cast-time target declaration.

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Add :card/targeting to Deep Analysis (TDD)
**File:** src/main/fizzle/cards/deep_analysis.cljs

Add targeting requirement to card definition:
\`\`\`clojure
:card/targeting [{:target/id :player
                  :target/type :player
                  :target/options [:self :opponent :any-player]
                  :target/required true}]
\`\`\`

Keep :effect/target :any-player temporarily for backwards compatibility during migration.

**Test:** Write test that verifies targeting/get-targeting-requirements returns the player target.

### Step 2: Modify casting flow for target declaration
**File:** src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

**Location:** cast-spell-with-mode-on-stack function (around line 800)

After mode selection, BEFORE moving spell to stack:
1. Check if card has :card/targeting via targeting/get-targeting-requirements
2. If targeting exists AND requires selection (not pre-chosen):
   - Call targeting/has-valid-targets? to verify targets exist
   - Build player-target selection state (similar to build-player-target-selection)
   - Return {:db db :pending-selection targeting-selection}
3. When target selection confirmed:
   - Store targets on object via :object/targets map
   - Continue with stack placement

**Key difference from current flow:**
- Currently: Target selected at RESOLUTION time (line 883)
- New: Target selected at CAST time, stored on object, used at resolution

### Step 3: Update resolution to use stored targets
**File:** src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

**Location:** resolve-spell-effects function and execute-effects-with-selection function

Modify to:
1. Read targets from :object/targets map on the spell object
2. Pass stored target to effect execution instead of prompting
3. For :effect/target :any-player effects, look up :player target from stored targets
4. If no stored target (legacy path), fall back to current selection behavior

### Step 4: Add fizzle logic for invalid targets
**File:** src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

**Location:** resolve-stack-top function (around line 750)

Add before executing effects:
\`\`\`clojure
(if (and (seq (targeting/get-targeting-requirements card))
         (not (targeting/all-targets-legal? db object-id)))
  ;; Fizzle: move card to destination without executing effects
  (let [destination (or (get-in obj [:object/cast-mode :mode/on-resolve]) :graveyard)]
    (zones/move-to-zone db object-id destination))
  ;; Normal resolution
  (resolve-spell-effects db player-id object-id))
\`\`\`

### Step 5: Update Deep Analysis tests
**File:** src/test/fizzle/cards/deep_analysis_test.cljs

Update existing tests:
- Tests that cast Deep Analysis need to also provide target selection
- Helper function to simulate target selection at cast time

New tests to add:
1. test_deep_analysis_has_targeting_requirement - verify :card/targeting present
2. test_deep_analysis_target_selection_at_cast_time - verify prompt before stack
3. test_deep_analysis_stored_target_used_at_resolution - verify stored target used
4. test_deep_analysis_player_target_always_legal - player targets don't fizzle (players can't move zones)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Deep Analysis card has :card/targeting attribute with player target
- [ ] targeting/get-targeting-requirements returns 1 requirement for Deep Analysis
- [ ] Casting Deep Analysis prompts for player target BEFORE spell goes on stack
- [ ] :object/targets stores {:player :player-1} (or :player-2) on the spell
- [ ] Resolution reads target from :object/targets, not prompting again
- [ ] all-targets-legal? returns true for player targets (always legal)
- [ ] All 20+ existing Deep Analysis tests pass without modification
- [ ] 4 new tests pass confirming cast-time targeting
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + fmt + tests)

## Key Considerations

**Edge Case: Storm Copies**
- Storm creates copies of spells on the stack
- Copies should inherit targets from original spell
- Verify existing storm copy logic copies :object/targets

**Edge Case: Flashback Deep Analysis**
- Flashback casts from graveyard, not hand
- Targeting should work identically
- Test flashback with both :self and :opponent targets

**Edge Case: Cancelled Target Selection**
- If player dismisses target selection UI
- Spell should NOT go on stack (similar to cancelled mode selection)
- Current UI pattern: return to previous state

**Backwards Compatibility**
- Keep :effect/target :any-player during migration
- Effects module may still check this for legacy behavior
- Remove after Cephalid Coliseum also migrated (Phase 5)

**Reference Implementation**
- Study existing player-target selection: build-player-target-selection (line 883)
- Study mode selection flow: cast-spell-with-mode-on-stack (line 800)
- Study stored target reading: patterns don't exist yet, creating new pattern

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ Do NOT break existing Deep Analysis tests (migration preserves behavior)
- ❌ Do NOT modify targeting.cljs (foundation already complete in Phase 3)
- ❌ Do NOT implement Recoup-specific logic yet
- ❌ Do NOT remove :effect/target :any-player yet (needed for backwards compat)
- ❌ Do NOT hardcode player IDs - use stored target from :object/targets
- ❌ Do NOT duplicate target selection logic - reuse existing build-player-target-selection pattern

## Specific Test Cases (must implement)
1. test_deep_analysis_has_targeting_requirement
   - Catches: Missing :card/targeting attribute on card definition
   - Verifies: targeting/get-targeting-requirements returns vector with 1 player target

2. test_deep_analysis_target_selection_at_cast_time
   - Catches: Target prompt happening at wrong time (resolution vs cast)
   - Verifies: pending-selection returned after mode selection, before stack

3. test_deep_analysis_stored_target_used_at_resolution
   - Catches: Resolution still prompting instead of using stored target
   - Verifies: :object/targets has {:player target-id}, effect uses it

4. test_deep_analysis_flashback_targeting
   - Catches: Flashback path skipping targeting
   - Verifies: Flashback cast also prompts for target, stores it
close_reason: Implemented cast-time targeting for Deep Analysis: added :card/targeting attribute, cast-spell-with-targeting function, confirm-cast-time-target function, and updated resolve-spell-with-selection to use stored targets with fizzle check. All tests passing (520 tests).

title: Add :becomes-tapped trigger type to engine/triggers.cljs
design:
## Goal
Implement the :becomes-tapped trigger type so that cards like City of Brass can trigger abilities when they become tapped.

## Context
Completed fizzle-31d (costs.cljs) and fizzle-4zd (abilities.cljs). Now need trigger infrastructure so City of Brass can deal damage whenever it becomes tapped.

**Key Insight:** The existing trigger system has resolve-trigger multimethod but NO mechanism to detect trigger conditions. We need:
1. A way to check if a trigger condition is met (check-trigger multimethod)
2. A way to fire triggers based on game state changes

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Implementation Steps

### 1. RED: Write tests for check-trigger and :becomes-tapped
File: src/test/fizzle/engine/triggers_test.cljs

Add 8 tests:

```clojure
;; === check-trigger tests ===

(deftest test-check-trigger-becomes-tapped-matches
  (testing "check-trigger :becomes-tapped returns true when object transitions untapped->tapped"
    ;; Setup: old-db with untapped permanent, new-db with same permanent tapped
    ;; Trigger: {:trigger/type :becomes-tapped :trigger/source object-id}
    ;; Assert: (triggers/check-trigger old-db new-db trigger) => true
    ;; Catches: Not detecting state transition
    ))

(deftest test-check-trigger-becomes-tapped-no-change
  (testing "check-trigger :becomes-tapped returns false when object was already tapped"
    ;; Setup: old-db with tapped permanent, new-db with same permanent still tapped
    ;; Assert: (triggers/check-trigger old-db new-db trigger) => false
    ;; Catches: Firing on every tapped state, not just transition
    ))

(deftest test-check-trigger-becomes-tapped-untapped-still
  (testing "check-trigger :becomes-tapped returns false when object remains untapped"
    ;; Setup: old-db with untapped permanent, new-db with same permanent still untapped
    ;; Assert: (triggers/check-trigger old-db new-db trigger) => false
    ;; Catches: Firing when no tap occurred
    ))

(deftest test-check-trigger-becomes-tapped-missing-object
  (testing "check-trigger :becomes-tapped returns false when object doesn't exist"
    ;; Setup: old-db with no object, new-db with no object
    ;; Assert: (triggers/check-trigger old-db new-db trigger) => false (not crash)
    ;; Catches: NPE on missing object
    ))

;; === fire-matching-triggers tests ===

(deftest test-fire-matching-triggers-creates-trigger-on-stack
  (testing "fire-matching-triggers adds trigger to stack when condition matches"
    ;; Setup: permanent with :card/triggers [{:trigger/type :becomes-tapped ...}]
    ;; old-db: untapped, new-db: tapped
    ;; Action: (triggers/fire-matching-triggers old-db new-db)
    ;; Assert: trigger entity exists on stack in returned db
    ;; Catches: Not creating trigger objects
    ))

(deftest test-fire-matching-triggers-no-match-unchanged
  (testing "fire-matching-triggers returns db unchanged when no triggers match"
    ;; Setup: permanent with :card/triggers, but condition not met
    ;; Assert: db unchanged, no new triggers on stack
    ;; Catches: Creating spurious triggers
    ))

(deftest test-fire-matching-triggers-multiple-triggers
  (testing "fire-matching-triggers handles multiple triggers on same permanent"
    ;; Setup: permanent with two :becomes-tapped triggers
    ;; old-db: untapped, new-db: tapped
    ;; Assert: both triggers added to stack
    ;; Catches: Only firing first trigger
    ))

(deftest test-fire-matching-triggers-multiple-permanents
  (testing "fire-matching-triggers checks all permanents with triggers"
    ;; Setup: two permanents each with :becomes-tapped trigger
    ;; old-db: both untapped, new-db: first tapped, second still untapped
    ;; Assert: only first permanent's trigger fires
    ;; Catches: Checking all triggers regardless of condition
    ))
```

### 2. GREEN: Implement check-trigger multimethod

File: src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs

```clojure
(defmulti check-trigger
  "Check if a trigger condition is met.
   
   Arguments:
     old-db - Database state before action
     new-db - Database state after action  
     trigger - Trigger definition from card data
   
   Returns:
     Boolean - true if trigger should fire."
  (fn [_old-db _new-db trigger] (:trigger/type trigger)))

(defmethod check-trigger :default [_old-db _new-db _trigger] false)

(defmethod check-trigger :becomes-tapped
  [old-db new-db trigger]
  (let [source-id (:trigger/source trigger)]
    (if-let [new-obj (q/get-object new-db source-id)]
      (let [old-obj (q/get-object old-db source-id)
            was-tapped (get old-obj :object/tapped false)
            now-tapped (get new-obj :object/tapped false)]
        ;; Trigger fires only on transition: untapped -> tapped
        (and (not was-tapped) now-tapped))
      false)))
```

### 3. GREEN: Implement fire-matching-triggers

```clojure
(defn fire-matching-triggers
  "Check all triggers on battlefield and fire those whose conditions are met.
   
   Arguments:
     old-db - Database state before action
     new-db - Database state after action
   
   Returns:
     New db with matching triggers added to stack."
  [old-db new-db]
  (let [;; Get all permanents on battlefield
        battlefield (q/get-battlefield new-db)
        ;; Collect matching triggers
        matching-triggers
        (for [obj battlefield
              :let [card (q/get-card-data new-db (:object/card obj))
                    triggers (:card/triggers card)]
              trigger triggers
              :let [trigger-with-source (assoc trigger :trigger/source (:object/id obj))]
              :when (check-trigger old-db new-db trigger-with-source)]
          {:trigger trigger-with-source
           :controller (:object/controller obj)})]
    ;; Add all matching triggers to stack
    (reduce (fn [db {:keys [trigger controller]}]
              (let [full-trigger (create-trigger 
                                   (:trigger/type trigger)
                                   (:trigger/source trigger)
                                   controller
                                   {:effects (:trigger/effects trigger)})]
                (add-trigger-to-stack db full-trigger)))
            new-db
            matching-triggers)))
```

### 4. GREEN: Add resolve-trigger for :becomes-tapped

```clojure
(defmethod resolve-trigger :becomes-tapped
  [db trigger]
  (let [controller (:trigger/controller trigger)
        effects (get-in trigger [:trigger/data :effects] [])]
    (reduce (fn [db' effect]
              (effects/execute-effect db' controller effect))
            db
            effects)))
```

### 5. VERIFY
- Run: make validate
- All 8 new tests pass
- All existing 189 tests still pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] check-trigger multimethod exists with :becomes-tapped implementation
- [ ] check-trigger :becomes-tapped returns true ONLY on untapped->tapped transition
- [ ] check-trigger :becomes-tapped returns false for already-tapped or still-untapped
- [ ] check-trigger :becomes-tapped returns false (not crash) for missing object
- [ ] fire-matching-triggers scans battlefield and fires matching triggers
- [ ] fire-matching-triggers handles multiple triggers per permanent
- [ ] fire-matching-triggers handles multiple permanents
- [ ] resolve-trigger :becomes-tapped executes trigger effects
- [ ] 8 new tests pass in triggers_test.cljs
- [ ] All 189+ existing tests still pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**State Transition Detection:**
- Must compare old-db to new-db, not just check current state
- :becomes-tapped fires on transition (untapped->tapped), not on being tapped
- Permanents entering battlefield tapped do NOT trigger :becomes-tapped

**Missing Object Handling:**
- If trigger source no longer exists, check-trigger returns false (not crash)
- Defensive coding: use get with default for :object/tapped

**Controller vs Owner:**
- Triggered abilities are controlled by permanent's controller (may differ from owner)
- Use :object/controller when creating trigger on stack

**Effect Execution:**
- Trigger effects use existing effects/execute-effect multimethod
- Unknown effect types are no-op (graceful degradation)
- Empty effects list is valid (trigger fires but does nothing)

**Integration Point:**
- fire-matching-triggers must be called AFTER any game action that might tap permanents
- Caller (events/game.cljs) responsible for calling this at appropriate times
- This task does NOT wire up the caller - that's a separate integration task

**Reference Implementation:**
- Study resolve-trigger :storm in triggers.cljs for effect execution pattern
- Study costs_test.cljs for add-permanent test helper

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO card ID checks in trigger code (data over code)
- ❌ NO hardcoded trigger logic (use multimethod dispatch)
- ❌ NO triggering on every tap check (only state transitions)
- ❌ NO crashing on missing objects (return false gracefully)
- ❌ NO ignoring multiple triggers on same permanent
close_reason: Closed

title: Add events and subscriptions for fork/replay UI components
design:
## Goal
Add re-frame events and subscriptions that wire the new data model functions to the UI layer. Events handle user actions (jump-to, create/rename/delete fork). Subscriptions derive turn-grouped entries and hierarchical fork tree for the view components.

## Context
Completed fizzle-4r1d: Four pure functions now exist in history/core.cljs:
- rename-fork, delete-fork, entries-by-turn, fork-tree
- All tested and working (21 tests)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- history/events.cljs — existing events: ::step-back, ::step-forward, ::switch-branch
- subs/history.cljs — existing subs: ::entries, ::position, ::current-branch, ::forks, ::can-step-back?, ::can-step-forward?, ::at-tip?, ::entry-count
- history/core.cljs:67-75 — step-to (used by ::jump-to)
- history/core.cljs:105-115 — create-named-fork (used by ::create-fork)
- history/core.cljs:130-134 — rename-fork (used by ::rename-fork)
- history/core.cljs:138-167 — delete-fork (used by ::delete-fork)
- history/core.cljs:175-183 — fork-tree (used by ::fork-tree sub)
- history/core.cljs:186-191 — entries-by-turn (used by ::entries-by-turn sub)

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

**Event tests (in history/events_test.cljs):**
- test-jump-to-event: dispatching ::jump-to with position updates position and game/db
- test-jump-to-event-out-of-bounds: out-of-bounds position leaves db unchanged
- test-create-fork-event: dispatching ::create-fork adds fork to :history/forks
- test-rename-fork-event: dispatching ::rename-fork updates fork name
- test-delete-fork-event: dispatching ::delete-fork removes fork from map
- test-delete-fork-event-cascade: deleting parent fork cascades to children

**Subscription tests (in subs/history_test.cljs):**
- test-entries-by-turn-sub: subscribe to ::entries-by-turn returns grouped structure
- test-fork-tree-sub: subscribe to ::fork-tree returns hierarchical tree

### 3. Implementation checklist

**Events (history/events.cljs):**
- [ ] ::jump-to [db [_ position]] — calls history/step-to
- [ ] ::create-fork [db [_ name]] — calls history/create-named-fork
- [ ] ::rename-fork [db [_ fork-id new-name]] — calls history/rename-fork
- [ ] ::delete-fork [db [_ fork-id]] — calls history/delete-fork

**Subscriptions (subs/history.cljs):**
- [ ] ::entries-by-turn — Layer 3 sub, derives from ::entries, calls history/entries-by-turn
- [ ] ::fork-tree — Layer 3 sub, derives from ::forks (the raw forks map from db), calls history/fork-tree

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 4 events work correctly (6 event tests)
- [ ] Both subscriptions return correct derived data (2 sub tests)
- [ ] Events delegate to history/core.cljs functions (no logic duplication)
- [ ] Subscriptions are Layer 3 (derived from existing Layer 2 subs)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations

**::forks subscription shape**: The existing ::forks sub returns `(vec (vals (:history/forks db)))` — a vector of fork maps. But fork-tree needs the forks MAP (fork-id → fork). May need a new Layer 2 sub `::forks-map` that returns `:history/forks` directly, or adjust fork-tree to accept a vector. Check existing sub shape before implementing.

**create-named-fork does NOT switch branch**: Per existing behavior (core.cljs:105-115), create-named-fork creates the fork but stays on current branch. The UI may want to switch after creation — handle that in the view layer (dispatch switch-branch after create), not in the event.

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO game logic in events (delegate to history/core.cljs functions)
- ❌ NO direct db manipulation — events call pure functions
- ❌ NO subscription that queries app-db directly for derived data (use Layer 2 → Layer 3 pattern)
close_reason: All 4 events (jump-to, create-fork, rename-fork, delete-fork) and 2 subscriptions (entries-by-turn, fork-tree) implemented. 8 new tests, all passing. make validate clean.

title: Task 1: Extend tutor selection for multi-card select
design:
## Goal
Extend the existing tutor selection system to support selecting multiple cards (up to N) instead of just 1. This is the foundational change needed for Intuition.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing tutor selection
- events/game.cljs:911-930 - build-tutor-selection (current single-select, uses :selection/count 1)
- events/game.cljs:1385-1428 - execute-tutor-selection (moves card via `(first selected)`, shuffles)
- events/game.cljs:1431+ - confirm-tutor-selection event handler
- events/game.cljs:951-960 - build-discard-selection (example of multi-count selection)

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Create src/test/fizzle/events/tutor_multi_select_test.cljs:

```clojure
;; Test: Multi-select tutor selects exactly 3 from large library
;; Setup: Library with 10 cards, tutor effect with :effect/select-count 3
;; Action: Build selection, select 3 cards, confirm
;; Assert: All 3 cards moved to target zone, library shuffled, 7 cards remain

;; Test: Multi-select tutor with exact library size
;; Setup: Library with 2 cards, tutor effect with :effect/select-count 3
;; Action: Build selection
;; Assert: :selection/select-count is 2 (min of effect count and library size)
;; Assert: Selection requires exactly 2 (cannot confirm with 1)

;; Test: Multi-select tutor with smaller library
;; Setup: Library with 1 card, tutor effect with :effect/select-count 3
;; Action: Build selection, select 1 card, confirm
;; Assert: 1 card moved to target zone, library shuffled

;; Test: Multi-select tutor fail-to-find (empty library)
;; Setup: Empty library, tutor effect with :effect/select-count 3
;; Action: Build selection
;; Assert: :selection/allow-fail-to-find true, can confirm with 0 selected
;; Assert: Library shuffled, no cards moved

;; Test: Backwards compat - existing single-select tutors work
;; Setup: Library with 5 cards, tutor effect WITHOUT :effect/select-count
;; Action: Build selection
;; Assert: :selection/select-count defaults to 1
;; Assert: Can select exactly 1 card, confirm moves 1 card
```

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] events/game.cljs:911-930 - Modify build-tutor-selection:
      - Add parameter extraction: `(or (:effect/select-count tutor-effect) 1)`
      - Compute actual count: `(min select-count (count candidate-ids))`
      - Add to selection state: `:selection/select-count actual-count`
      - Add to selection state: `:selection/exact? true` (must select exactly this many)
- [ ] events/game.cljs:1385-1428 - Modify execute-tutor-selection:
      - Change `(first selected)` to iterate over all selected cards
      - Move each card to target-zone in sequence
      - Shuffle once at end (not per card)
- [ ] events/game.cljs:1431+ - Verify confirm-tutor-selection handles set correctly (already uses set)
- [ ] Backwards compat: `(or (:effect/select-count tutor-effect) 1)` ensures default is 1

### 4. Verification
- Run: make test (all tests pass)
- Run: make validate (lint, format, tests)
- Manual test: Cast Merchant Scroll, verify still selects 1 card

## Success Criteria
- [ ] build-tutor-selection extracts :effect/select-count (default 1)
- [ ] :selection/select-count = min(effect-count, library-size)
- [ ] :selection/exact? = true in selection state
- [ ] execute-tutor-selection iterates over all selected cards (not just first)
- [ ] All selected cards moved to target-zone before shuffle
- [ ] Shuffle happens once after all moves (not per card)
- [ ] Existing tutors (no :select-count) default to 1 card
- [ ] 5+ unit tests pass with specific assertions
- [ ] make validate passes (lint, format, tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: select-count <= 0**
- If :effect/select-count is 0 or negative, treat as 1 (defensive)
- Use: `(max 1 (or (:effect/select-count tutor-effect) 1))`

**Edge Case: Empty candidates but select-count > 0**
- If no matching cards in library, :selection/select-count = 0
- :selection/allow-fail-to-find = true allows confirming with 0 selected
- This is existing behavior, preserve it

**Edge Case: Selection cancelled**
- User closes modal without confirming
- Existing behavior: selection state cleared, spell remains on stack
- No change needed, but verify tests cover this

**Performance: Multiple card moves**
- Moving 3 cards sequentially is fine (not hot path)
- Shuffle once at end, not per move

**Reference Implementation**
- build-discard-selection (line 951-960) uses :selection/count for multi-select
- Follow same pattern for consistency

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO shuffling between card moves (shuffle once at end)
- ❌ NO changing fail-to-find behavior (must always be allowed per existing anti-pattern)
- ❌ NO breaking single-select default (backwards compat required)
- ❌ NO using (first selected) when multiple cards possible (iterate over all)
close_reason: Multi-select tutor selection implemented with TDD. All tests passing, make validate passes.

title: Feature: Discard to hand size at cleanup
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- At cleanup step, if active player's hand exceeds max hand size, they must discard down to max
- Player chooses which cards to discard via interactive selection UI
- Discard happens BEFORE 'until end of turn' effects end (MTG Rule 514.1 before 514.2)
- Max hand size is stored as :player/max-hand-size in schema (default 7)
- Cleanup step repeats if triggered abilities fire during it (MTG Rule 514.3)
- Cannot start new turn while cleanup discard is pending

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Hand size check triggers automatically when phase advances to cleanup
- [ ] Selection UI appears showing hand cards when hand > max-hand-size
- [ ] Player must select exactly (hand-count - max-hand-size) cards to discard
- [ ] Selected cards moved to graveyard on confirm
- [ ] Grant expiration (expire-grants) occurs AFTER discard, not before
- [ ] If triggered abilities fire during cleanup, cleanup cycle restarts after they resolve
- [ ] New Turn button disabled/unavailable while cleanup discard selection is pending
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] New tests cover: no-discard path, discard path, cleanup repeat

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoding 7 as hand size limit (must read from :player/max-hand-size schema field - future cards modify this)
- ❌ NO auto-discarding or random discard (player MUST choose which cards to discard)
- ❌ NO expiring grants before discard completes (MTG Rule 514: discard is 514.1, expire is 514.2)
- ❌ NO skipping cleanup repeat check after grant expiration (must check for triggered abilities)
- ❌ NO adding test-only methods to production code (test via public API)

## Approach
Handle cleanup as a multi-step process at the re-frame event handler level, building on the existing selection/confirm pattern. Remove the auto-firing :cleanup-expire-grants turn-based trigger and replace it with explicit cleanup flow in the ::advance-phase, ::confirm-selection, and new ::continue-cleanup event handlers.

The key insight is that the selection UI pattern (game/pending-selection) already handles pausing game flow for player input. We reuse this with a new :cleanup-discard effect type. Grant expiration is called explicitly after discard completes rather than auto-firing via trigger dispatch.

## Architecture
### Schema Changes
- Add :player/max-hand-size to schema.cljs
- Initialize to 7 in init-game-state for player-1

### Query Changes  
- Add get-max-hand-size query to queries.cljs

### Turn-Based Trigger Changes
- Remove :cleanup-expire-grants from turn_based.cljs register-turn-based-actions!

### Event Handler Changes
- Modify ::advance-phase: detect cleanup phase, run begin-cleanup logic
- Add :cleanup-discard case to ::confirm-selection handler
- Add ::continue-cleanup event for cleanup repeat cycle
- Modify ::resolve-top: when stack empties during cleanup, dispatch ::continue-cleanup

### Cleanup Flow
1. Phase advances to :cleanup via advance-phase (pure fn, dispatches phase-entered)
2. Re-frame handler checks hand size vs max-hand-size
3. If hand > max: set game/pending-selection with :cleanup-discard type
4. If hand <= max: call grants/expire-grants directly, check for triggers
5. After discard confirmed: expire grants, check for triggers
6. If triggers on stack: player resolves them, then ::continue-cleanup restarts cycle
7. When no discard needed AND no triggers: cleanup complete, New Turn available

## Design Rationale
### Problem
MTG Rule 514 requires discarding to hand size during cleanup. Currently the cleanup step only expires 'until end of turn' grants. Players can accumulate cards beyond 7 without penalty, which is incorrect.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- events/game.cljs:367-386 - advance-phase pure function, dispatches phase-entered
- events/game.cljs:434-438 - ::advance-phase re-frame handler bridges pure fn to app-db
- engine/turn_based.cljs:42-50 - :cleanup-expire-grants fires on :phase-entered :cleanup
- events/game.cljs:1231-1240 - build-discard-selection pattern for interactive discard
- events/game.cljs:1680-1725 - ::confirm-selection handles :discard effect type
- db/schema.cljs:47-55 - Player schema, no max-hand-size field exists
- core.cljs:346-381 - Turn bar UI, cleanup shows New Turn button

**MTG Rules:**
- Rule 514.1: Active player discards to max hand size (first action in cleanup)
- Rule 514.2: Damage removed, 'until end of turn' effects end (second, simultaneous)
- Rule 514.3: If state-based actions or triggers exist, active player gets priority, then new cleanup step begins
- Default max hand size: 7

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Handler-level cleanup flow ✓
**What it is:** Handle cleanup as multi-step process in re-frame handlers. Remove auto-expire-grants trigger, explicitly manage cleanup ordering.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed existing selection pattern (build-discard-selection + confirm-selection)
- Confirmed pending-selection blocks other game actions naturally
- Verified grant expiration can be called as pure function outside trigger system

**Pros:**
- Follows existing selection/confirm pattern
- Minimal new concepts
- No new schema patterns (reuses pending-selection)

**Cons:**
- Cleanup logic across multiple handlers

**Chosen because:** Consistent with existing codebase patterns, minimal architectural change

#### 2. Cleanup state machine in game-db ❌
**What it is:** Add :game/cleanup-step field tracking progress through discrete states (:discard → :expire → :check-triggers → :complete).

**Why we looked at this:** State machines naturally handle multi-step flows with repeat cycles

**Pros:**
- Explicit state easy to reason about
- Natural cleanup repeat support

**Cons:**
- Introduces state machine pattern not used elsewhere in codebase
- More schema changes
- Cleanup step value would need to be managed/reset

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Introduces new pattern (state machines) when existing selection/confirm pattern handles the same flow

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Multiple other game phases need similar multi-step interactive flows

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Hand size check at cleanup
- Interactive discard selection
- Correct ordering (discard before expire)
- Cleanup repeat on triggered abilities
- Schema field for max hand size

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Cards that modify max hand size (future card implementations)
- Opponent hand size check (opponent is bot, not relevant for practice tool)
- Madness mechanic (discard trigger - separate card implementation)

### Open Questions
- Should hand count display show max hand size indicator? (decide during implementation)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Variable max hand size or hardcode 7? | Schema field, default 7 | Add :player/max-hand-size to schema, anti-pattern: no hardcoding |
| Cleanup repeat support? | Yes, support repeat | Need ::continue-cleanup event, hook into stack empty during cleanup |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Cleanup Step Rules (MTG Rule 514)
**Question explored:** What is the correct order of operations during cleanup?
**Sources consulted:**
- MTG Comprehensive Rules 514.1-514.3
- MTG Wiki ending phase article

**Findings:**
- 514.1: Discard to hand size FIRST
- 514.2: Remove damage, end 'until end of turn' effects SECOND
- 514.3: Check for triggers, if any exist grant priority and restart cleanup
- No priority normally during cleanup (simplified for practice tool)

**Conclusion:** Must enforce discard-before-expire ordering, support cleanup restart

#### Existing Selection Pattern
**Question explored:** Can we reuse the existing discard selection UI?
**Sources consulted:**
- events/game.cljs build-discard-selection (line 1231)
- events/game.cljs confirm-selection handler (line 1680)
- core.cljs card-selection-modal

**Findings:**
- Selection system fully supports player-choice discard
- :selection/effect-type dispatches behavior in confirm-selection
- Adding new effect type (:cleanup-discard) follows established pattern

**Conclusion:** Reuse selection pattern with new :cleanup-discard effect type

### Dead-End Paths
None - both approaches are viable, chose simpler one

### Open Concerns Raised
- Cleanup repeat with Madness cards → Deferred to card implementation, not cleanup infrastructure
close_reason: All child tasks complete. 8/9 success criteria fully met, 1 partially met (New Turn button functionally blocked but lacks visual disabled state for pending-selection - minor UX polish). 741 tests pass, make validate clean.

title: Add effect condition system and sacrifice effect type
design:
## Goal
Add infrastructure for conditional effects and the sacrifice effect type. This enables effects to have an optional :effect/condition that gates execution.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- engine/effects.cljs:13-31 - Current execute-effect multimethod pattern, dispatches on :effect/type
- engine/effects.cljs:162-188 - Example effect implementation (:add-counters) showing target resolution pattern
- events/game.cljs:385-388 - How :self is resolved to object-id before calling execute-effect
- db/queries.cljs - Query patterns for object data

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

**File: src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs**

Add new test namespace section for conditional effects:

- test-effect-without-condition-executes-normally
  - Setup: Object with counters, effect with no :effect/condition
  - Execute: Run effect (e.g., :add-mana)
  - Assert: Effect executed (mana added)
  - Bug caught: Regression if condition check breaks normal effects

- test-no-counters-condition-skips-when-counters-positive
  - Setup: Object with {:mining 2} counters
  - Execute: {:effect/type :sacrifice :effect/target obj-id :effect/condition {:condition/type :no-counters :condition/counter-type :mining}}
  - Assert: Object still on battlefield
  - Bug caught: Premature sacrifice when counters remain

- test-no-counters-condition-executes-when-counters-zero
  - Setup: Object with {:mining 0} counters
  - Execute: Same effect as above
  - Assert: Object moved to graveyard
  - Bug caught: Failure to sacrifice when counters depleted

- test-no-counters-condition-executes-when-counter-type-missing
  - Setup: Object with {} (no counters map) or {:charge 1} (different type)
  - Execute: Same effect checking :mining
  - Assert: Object moved to graveyard (nil/missing counts as zero)
  - Bug caught: NPE or incorrect handling of missing counter type

- test-sacrifice-effect-moves-to-graveyard
  - Setup: Object on battlefield
  - Execute: {:effect/type :sacrifice :effect/target obj-id}
  - Assert: Object zone is :graveyard
  - Bug caught: Wrong zone transition

- test-sacrifice-effect-invalid-target-is-noop
  - Setup: No object with given ID
  - Execute: {:effect/type :sacrifice :effect/target (random-uuid)}
  - Assert: db unchanged, no exception
  - Bug caught: NPE on invalid target

- test-unknown-condition-type-skips-effect
  - Setup: Object on battlefield
  - Execute: {:effect/type :sacrifice :effect/target obj-id :effect/condition {:condition/type :unknown-type}}
  - Assert: Object still on battlefield (fail-safe: unknown condition = don't execute)
  - Bug caught: Executing effects with unhandled conditions

### 3. Implementation checklist

- [ ] effects.cljs - Add check-condition multimethod
  ```clojure
  (defmulti check-condition
    "Check if an effect's condition is met. Dispatches on :condition/type.
     Returns true if condition met, false otherwise.
     Unknown condition types return false (fail-safe: don't execute)."
    (fn [_db _player-id _effect condition] (:condition/type condition)))
  
  (defmethod check-condition :default [_db _player-id _effect _condition] false)
  ```

- [ ] effects.cljs - Add :no-counters condition method
  ```clojure
  (defmethod check-condition :no-counters
    [db _player-id effect condition]
    (let [target-id (:effect/target effect)
          counter-type (:condition/counter-type condition)]
      (if-let [obj-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?oid :where [?e :object/id ?oid]] db target-id)]
        (let [counters (or (d/q '[:find ?c . :in $ ?e :where [?e :object/counters ?c]] db obj-eid) {})
              count-value (get counters counter-type 0)]
          (<= count-value 0))
        ;; Object doesn't exist - condition met (vacuously true, effect will be no-op anyway)
        true)))
  ```

- [ ] effects.cljs - Create execute-effect-impl as renamed current multimethod
  - Rename current execute-effect multimethod to execute-effect-impl
  - Keep all existing defmethods unchanged

- [ ] effects.cljs - Create wrapper execute-effect function
  ```clojure
  (defn execute-effect
    "Execute an effect, checking condition first if present.
     If :effect/condition exists and is not met, returns db unchanged.
     If no condition or condition met, dispatches to execute-effect-impl."
    [db player-id effect]
    (if-let [condition (:effect/condition effect)]
      (if (check-condition db player-id effect condition)
        (execute-effect-impl db player-id effect)
        db)
      (execute-effect-impl db player-id effect)))
  ```

- [ ] effects.cljs - Add :sacrifice effect to execute-effect-impl
  ```clojure
  (defmethod execute-effect-impl :sacrifice
    "Sacrifice a permanent - move it to the graveyard.
     Effect keys:
       :effect/target - Object ID (caller must resolve :self before calling)
     Handles edge cases:
       - Invalid target: no-op (returns db unchanged)"
    [db _player-id effect]
    (let [target-id (:effect/target effect)]
      (if (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?oid :where [?e :object/id ?oid]] db target-id)
        (zones/move-to-zone db target-id :graveyard)
        db)))
  ```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] check-condition multimethod exists and dispatches on :condition/type
- [ ] :no-counters condition returns true when counter type is 0, nil, or missing
- [ ] :no-counters condition returns false when counter type > 0
- [ ] Unknown condition types return false (fail-safe)
- [ ] Effects without conditions execute normally (no regression)
- [ ] Effects with conditions only execute when condition met
- [ ] :sacrifice effect moves target to graveyard
- [ ] :sacrifice effect is no-op for invalid target
- [ ] 7+ unit tests pass covering all scenarios above
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing (lint, format, test)

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: :self Resolution**
- :self MUST be resolved to object-id by caller before execute-effect is called
- Pattern already exists in events/game.cljs:385-388 (play-land ETB effects)
- Condition uses :effect/target, so resolving target also resolves for condition
- Document this requirement in execute-effect docstring

**Edge Case: Missing Counter Type**
- Object may have no :object/counters (nil)
- Object may have counters but not the requested type
- Both cases should be treated as "count is zero" (condition met)
- Use (get counters counter-type 0) with fallback

**Edge Case: Invalid Target**
- Object may not exist (moved to graveyard between cost payment and effect)
- :sacrifice should be no-op for invalid target (matches existing patterns)
- :no-counters condition on invalid target: return true (vacuously true, effect will no-op anyway)

**Edge Case: Unknown Condition Type**
- Multimethod :default should return false (fail-safe)
- If we can't evaluate condition, don't execute effect
- Log warning in future (not blocking for this task)

**Reference Implementation**
- Study engine/effects.cljs:162-188 (:add-counters) for target lookup pattern
- Study events/game.cljs:383-391 for :self resolution pattern

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO checking condition AFTER effect execution (must be before)
- ❌ NO panic/throw on unknown condition type (return false, fail-safe)
- ❌ NO assuming :effect/target is always valid UUID (may be invalid)
- ❌ NO modifying existing execute-effect behavior for effects without conditions
close_reason: Implemented effect condition system with check-condition multimethod, :no-counters condition type, and :sacrifice effect. All tests passing.

title: Task 4: Add scry modal UI component
design:
## Goal
Create the scry modal UI component that displays revealed cards and allows player to assign them to top or bottom piles. This connects the selection state (created by Task 2) to the confirmation handler (Task 3).

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context
Completed fizzle-xzc: confirm-scry-selection handler exists
Selection state structure (from build-scry-selection):
- :selection/type :scry
- :selection/cards [obj-id-1 obj-id-2 ...]
- :selection/top-pile []
- :selection/bottom-pile []
- :selection/player-id
- :selection/spell-id
- :selection/remaining-effects

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing selection UI patterns
Reference implementations (ALL IN core.cljs):
- src/main/fizzle/core.cljs:501-603 - card-selection-modal (discard/tutor)
- src/main/fizzle/core.cljs:606-617 - selection-modal (dispatcher)
- src/main/fizzle/core.cljs:413-440 - selection-card-view (card display)
- Pattern: Modal displays cards, tracks selection state, dispatches confirm event

### 2. Add scry-modal component in core.cljs

Location: After card-selection-modal (~line 603), before selection-modal

```clojure
(defn- scry-card-view
  "A card in the scry selection modal, showing pile assignment."
  [obj pile]
  (let [card (:object/card obj)
        card-name (or (:card/name card) "Unknown")]
    [:div {:style {:width "100px"
                   :padding "8px"
                   :border-radius "6px"
                   :background (case pile
                                 :top "#2a4a2a"      ; green tint for top
                                 :bottom "#4a2a2a"   ; red tint for bottom
                                 "#1a2a3a")          ; neutral for unassigned
                   :border "1px solid #4A9BD9"
                   :text-align "center"
                   :font-size "11px"
                   :color "#eee"
                   :cursor "pointer"}}
     card-name]))

(defn- scry-modal
  "Modal for scry selection - assign cards to top or bottom piles."
  [selection]
  (let [scry-cards (:selection/cards selection)
        top-pile (:selection/top-pile selection)
        bottom-pile (:selection/bottom-pile selection)
        assigned (into (set top-pile) bottom-pile)
        unassigned (remove assigned scry-cards)
        ;; Get actual card objects for display
        db @(rf/subscribe [::subs/game-db])
        get-obj (fn [oid] (q/get-object db oid))]
    [:div {:style {:position "fixed" :top 0 :left 0 :right 0 :bottom 0
                   :background "rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.8)"
                   :display "flex" :align-items "center" :justify-content "center"
                   :z-index 1000}}
     [:div {:style {:background "#1a1a2a" :border "2px solid #4A9BD9"
                    :border-radius "12px" :padding "24px"
                    :max-width "600px" :width "90%"}}
      ;; Header
      [:h2 {:style {:color "#eee" :margin "0 0 16px 0"}}
       (str "Scry " (count scry-cards))]
      
      ;; Top Pile section
      [:div {:style {:margin-bottom "16px"}}
       [:h3 {:style {:color "#5CB85C" :font-size "14px" :margin "0 0 8px 0"}}
        "Top of Library (first)"]
       [:div {:style {:display "flex" :flex-wrap "wrap" :gap "8px" :min-height "40px"}}
        (for [oid top-pile]
          ^{:key oid}
          [:div {:on-click #(rf/dispatch [::events/scry-unassign oid])}
           [scry-card-view (get-obj oid) :top]])]]
      
      ;; Unassigned cards section  
      [:div {:style {:margin-bottom "16px"}}
       [:h3 {:style {:color "#aaa" :font-size "14px" :margin "0 0 8px 0"}}
        "Revealed Cards"]
       [:div {:style {:display "flex" :flex-wrap "wrap" :gap "8px" :min-height "40px"}}
        (for [oid unassigned]
          ^{:key oid}
          [:div {:style {:display "flex" :gap "4px" :align-items "center"}}
           [scry-card-view (get-obj oid) nil]
           [:div {:style {:display "flex" :flex-direction "column" :gap "2px"}}
            [:button {:style {:padding "4px 8px" :background "#2a4a2a" :border "1px solid #5CB85C"
                              :border-radius "4px" :color "#eee" :cursor "pointer" :font-size "10px"}
                      :on-click #(rf/dispatch [::events/scry-assign-top oid])}
             "Top"]
            [:button {:style {:padding "4px 8px" :background "#4a2a2a" :border "1px solid #D9534F"
                              :border-radius "4px" :color "#eee" :cursor "pointer" :font-size "10px"}
                      :on-click #(rf/dispatch [::events/scry-assign-bottom oid])}
             "Bottom"]]])]]
      
      ;; Bottom Pile section
      [:div {:style {:margin-bottom "20px"}}
       [:h3 {:style {:color "#D9534F" :font-size "14px" :margin "0 0 8px 0"}}
        "Bottom of Library (last)"]
       [:div {:style {:display "flex" :flex-wrap "wrap" :gap "8px" :min-height "40px"}}
        (for [oid bottom-pile]
          ^{:key oid}
          [:div {:on-click #(rf/dispatch [::events/scry-unassign oid])}
           [scry-card-view (get-obj oid) :bottom]])]]
      
      ;; Confirm button (always enabled per epic anti-pattern)
      [:button {:style {:width "100%" :padding "12px" :background "#4A9BD9"
                        :border "none" :border-radius "8px" :color "#fff"
                        :font-size "16px" :cursor "pointer"}
                :on-click #(rf/dispatch [::events/confirm-scry-selection])}
       "Confirm"]]]))
```

### 3. Add scry events in events/game.cljs

After confirm-scry-selection (~line 1260):

```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::scry-assign-top
  (fn [db [_ obj-id]]
    (-> db
        (update-in [:game/pending-selection :selection/top-pile] conj obj-id)
        (update-in [:game/pending-selection :selection/bottom-pile] 
                   (fn [pile] (vec (remove #{obj-id} pile)))))))

(rf/reg-event-db
  ::scry-assign-bottom
  (fn [db [_ obj-id]]
    (-> db
        (update-in [:game/pending-selection :selection/bottom-pile] conj obj-id)
        (update-in [:game/pending-selection :selection/top-pile]
                   (fn [pile] (vec (remove #{obj-id} pile)))))))

(rf/reg-event-db
  ::scry-unassign
  (fn [db [_ obj-id]]
    (-> db
        (update-in [:game/pending-selection :selection/top-pile]
                   (fn [pile] (vec (remove #{obj-id} pile))))
        (update-in [:game/pending-selection :selection/bottom-pile]
                   (fn [pile] (vec (remove #{obj-id} pile)))))))
```

### 4. Wire scry-modal into selection-modal dispatcher

Update selection-modal (~line 606-617):

```clojure
(defn- selection-modal
  "Modal overlay for player selection.
   Shows when :game/pending-selection exists.
   Handles discard, tutor, scry, and player-target effects."
  []
  (let [selection @(rf/subscribe [::subs/pending-selection])
        cards @(rf/subscribe [::subs/selection-cards])]
    (when selection
      (let [selection-type (:selection/type selection)
            effect-type (:selection/effect-type selection)]
        (cond
          ;; Scry selection (has :selection/type :scry)
          (= selection-type :scry)
          [scry-modal selection]
          
          ;; Player target selection
          (= effect-type :player-target)
          [player-target-modal selection]
          
          ;; Card selection (discard, tutor)
          :else
          [card-selection-modal selection cards])))))
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] scry-modal function exists in core.cljs (~line 603-660)
- [ ] scry-card-view function exists for card display
- [ ] Events ::scry-assign-top, ::scry-assign-bottom, ::scry-unassign registered in game.cljs
- [ ] selection-modal dispatches to scry-modal when :selection/type is :scry
- [ ] Manual test: Cast Opt, scry modal appears showing 1 card
- [ ] Manual test: Click "Top" assigns card to top pile (green background)
- [ ] Manual test: Click "Bottom" assigns card to bottom pile (red background)
- [ ] Manual test: Click assigned card unassigns it back to revealed section
- [ ] Manual test: Click "Confirm" resolves scry and draws card
- [ ] Manual test: Scry 2 shows 2 cards, both can be assigned independently
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Empty scry (0 cards)**
- build-scry-selection returns nil for empty library
- selection-modal won't render if selection is nil
- No special handling needed in UI

**Edge Case: Many cards (Scry N)**
- UI uses flex-wrap, cards will wrap to multiple rows
- Max reasonable N in Premodern is ~5 (Serum Visions, etc.)
- Layout handles up to 5+ cards

**Edge Case: Re-assigning cards**
- Assign to top, then click "Bottom" should move to bottom
- Events handle this by removing from opposite pile first

**Reference: Existing modal pattern**
- core.cljs:501-603 card-selection-modal
- Same overlay style, position fixed, z-index 1000
- Same button styling for consistency

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO auto-assigning cards (player must choose)
- ❌ NO disabling confirm until all assigned (epic anti-pattern)
- ❌ NO hiding card names (need to see what we're scrying)
- ❌ NO blocking UI interaction while processing
- ❌ NO new view files (add to core.cljs like other modals)
close_reason: Closed
