title: Feature: Turn Structure UI
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Turn counter displays current turn number
- Phase bar shows all 8 phases (untap, upkeep, draw, main1, combat, main2, end, cleanup)
- Current phase is visually highlighted
- 'Next Phase' button advances to next phase
- Button changes to 'New Turn' when at cleanup phase
- Clicking 'New Turn' starts the next turn (calls start-turn event)
- UI positioned at top of screen, above battlefield

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Turn number displays and updates when new turn starts
- [ ] All 8 phases visible in horizontal strip
- [ ] Current phase visually distinct from others
- [ ] Next Phase button dispatches ::advance-phase event
- [ ] Button text changes to 'New Turn' at cleanup phase
- [ ] New Turn button dispatches ::start-turn event
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO auto-advance of phases (user wants manual control for practice)
- ❌ NO complex animations/transitions (POC - keep simple)
- ❌ NO new subscriptions when existing ones work (use ::current-phase, ::current-turn)
- ❌ NO separate component files (POC - add to core.cljs like other components)

## Approach
Add a turn-bar component to core.cljs following existing patterns (mana-pool-view, life-view). Uses existing subscriptions and events - no engine changes needed.

## Architecture
- src/fizzle/core.cljs - Add turn-bar component, insert at top of app
- Uses: subs/game.cljs ::current-phase, ::current-turn (existing)
- Dispatches: events/game.cljs ::advance-phase, ::start-turn (existing)

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Turn/phase structure exists in engine but is invisible to user. Cannot practice turn sequencing or test phase-dependent behavior without UI.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- src/fizzle/subs/game.cljs:73-83 - ::current-phase and ::current-turn subscriptions exist
- src/fizzle/events/game.cljs:173-175 - phases vector defines order
- src/fizzle/events/game.cljs:240-250 - ::advance-phase and ::start-turn events exist
- src/fizzle/core.cljs - UI components follow Reagent + Tailwind pattern

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Display turn counter
- Display phase bar with current phase highlighted
- Next Phase / New Turn button

**Out of scope:**
- Opponent turn support (single player for now)
- Phase-specific styling/theming
- Keyboard shortcuts
close_reason: All success criteria met. Turn-bar component implemented with turn counter, 8-phase strip, current phase highlighting, and Next Phase/New Turn button. Commit a861b40.

title: Task 4: Underground River + Ill-Gotten Gains card tests (final cards)
design:
## Goal
Create integration tests for the last 2 untested cards: Underground River (pain land) and Ill-Gotten Gains (symmetric graveyard effect with selection). Completing this closes the epic's card coverage requirement.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Pre-existing Coverage
- Underground River: only in init_game_test.cljs (deck list check), no mana/damage tests
- Ill-Gotten Gains: zero tests anywhere
- City of Brass and Gemstone Mine already covered by 9+ tests in tap_land_test.cljs (no new tests needed)
- Graveyard selection system tested in graveyard_selection_test.cljs (but not for IGG specifically)

## Implementation

### Part A: Underground River integration tests

**File:** src/test/fizzle/cards/underground_river_test.cljs (NEW)

**Card definition** (iggy_pop.cljs):
\`\`\`clojure
{:card/id :underground-river
 :card/name "Underground River"
 :card/types #{:land}
 :card/abilities [{:ability/type :mana
                   :ability/cost {:tap true}
                   :ability/produces {:colorless 1}}
                  {:ability/type :mana
                   :ability/cost {:tap true}
                   :ability/produces {:blue 1}
                   :ability/effects [{:effect/type :deal-damage
                                      :effect/amount 1
                                      :effect/target :controller}]}
                  {:ability/type :mana
                   :ability/cost {:tap true}
                   :ability/produces {:black 1}
                   :ability/effects [{:effect/type :deal-damage
                                      :effect/amount 1
                                      :effect/target :controller}]}]}
\`\`\`

**Required imports:**
\`\`\`clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.underground-river-test
  (:require
    [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is use-fixtures]]
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.cards.iggy-pop :as cards]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
    [fizzle.db.schema :refer [schema]]
    [fizzle.engine.trigger-registry :as registry]
    [fizzle.engine.turn-based :as turn-based]
    [fizzle.events.abilities :as ability-events]))
\`\`\`

**IMPORTANT:** Underground River does NOT have :card/triggers (unlike City of Brass), so it does NOT need trigger registration. Its damage comes from :ability/effects, which execute inline during mana ability activation. However, we still need the registry fixture because turn-based module registers actions needed by activate-mana-ability.

**Helpers:** Use same create-test-db + add-land-to-battlefield pattern from tap_land_test.cljs. The add-land-to-battlefield helper does NOT need trigger registration for Underground River since it has no :card/triggers.

**Tests:**

1. \`underground-river-card-definition-test\`
   - Assert: 3 abilities, all :ability/type :mana
   - Assert: first produces {:colorless 1}, no :ability/effects
   - Assert: second produces {:blue 1}, has deal-damage effect
   - Assert: third produces {:black 1}, has deal-damage effect
   - Bug caught: Card data structure integrity

2. \`underground-river-colorless-no-damage-test\`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add underground-river to battlefield
   - Act: activate-mana-ability with :colorless
   - Assert: colorless mana = 1
   - Assert: life still 20 (no damage from colorless ability)
   - Assert: land is tapped
   - Bug caught: Verifies colorless ability has no pain effect

3. \`underground-river-blue-deals-1-damage-test\`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add underground-river to battlefield
   - Act: activate-mana-ability with :blue
   - Assert: blue mana = 1
   - Assert: life = 19 (1 damage from pain land effect)
   - Assert: land is tapped
   - Bug caught: Verifies pain land damage on colored mana production

4. \`underground-river-black-deals-1-damage-test\`
   - Setup: same as above
   - Act: activate-mana-ability with :black
   - Assert: black mana = 1
   - Assert: life = 19
   - Bug caught: Verifies black ability also deals damage

5. \`underground-river-cannot-activate-when-tapped-test\`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add underground-river, activate once (tapped)
   - Act: try activating again
   - Assert: mana unchanged (second activation fails, returns unchanged db)
   - Bug caught: Tap cost enforcement

### Part B: Ill-Gotten Gains integration tests

**File:** src/test/fizzle/cards/ill_gotten_gains_test.cljs (NEW)

**Card definition** (ill_gotten_gains.cljs - separate file):
\`\`\`clojure
{:card/id :ill-gotten-gains
 :card/mana-cost {:colorless 2 :black 2}
 :card/types #{:sorcery}
 :card/effects [
   {:effect/type :exile-self}
   {:effect/type :discard-hand :effect/target :self}
   {:effect/type :discard-hand :effect/target :opponent}
   {:effect/type :return-from-graveyard :effect/target :self :effect/count 3 :effect/selection :player}
   {:effect/type :return-from-graveyard :effect/target :opponent :effect/count 3 :effect/selection :random}]}
\`\`\`

**Test approach:** Use resolve-spell-with-selection from selection.cljs. This pauses at the :return-from-graveyard :selection :player effect, returning {:db updated-db, :pending-selection selection-state}. All effects before that point (exile-self, both discard-hands) will have executed.

**Required imports:**
\`\`\`clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.ill-gotten-gains-test
  (:require
    [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.cards.iggy-pop :as cards]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
    [fizzle.db.schema :refer [schema]]
    [fizzle.engine.mana :as mana]
    [fizzle.engine.rules :as rules]
    [fizzle.events.selection :as selection]))
\`\`\`

**Also need:** import ill-gotten-gains card. Check if it's in cards/all-cards or separate.

**Helpers:** create-test-db + add-card-to-zone (same pattern), plus:
- add-opponent helper
- add-cards-to-zone helper: adds N cards to specific zone for player

**Tests:**

6. \`ill-gotten-gains-card-definition-test\`
   - Assert: card exists with correct id, mana cost {2}{B}{B}, sorcery type
   - Assert: 5 effects in correct order
   - Assert: first is :exile-self, then two :discard-hand, then two :return-from-graveyard
   - Bug caught: Card data structure

7. \`ill-gotten-gains-exiles-self-and-discards-both-hands-test\`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add opponent, add IGG to hand, add 2 other cards to caster hand, add 3 cards to opponent hand, add mana
   - Act: cast-spell, then selection/resolve-spell-with-selection
   - Assert on (:db result): IGG is in exile zone (not graveyard, not stack)
   - Assert: caster hand is empty (discarded)
   - Assert: opponent hand is empty (discarded)
   - Assert: caster graveyard has 2 cards (the other hand cards)
   - Assert: opponent graveyard has 3 cards (their hand cards)
   - Assert: (:pending-selection result) is not nil (paused for selection)
   - Bug caught: Verifies effects before selection execute correctly, spell exiles

8. \`ill-gotten-gains-selection-state-structure-test\`
   - Setup: same as above, but also add 4 cards to caster graveyard before casting
   - Act: cast-spell, resolve-spell-with-selection
   - Assert on (:pending-selection result):
     - :selection/type is :graveyard-return
     - :selection/count is 3 (max cards to return)
     - :selection/min-count is 0 (can select none)
     - :selection/player-id is :player-1
     - :selection/candidate-ids contains the graveyard card IDs (hand cards that were discarded + pre-existing GY cards)
     - :selection/remaining-effects has 1 effect (opponent random return)
   - Bug caught: Selection state built correctly for IGG

9. \`ill-gotten-gains-cannot-cast-without-mana-test\`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add IGG to hand, no mana
   - Assert: can-cast? returns false
   - Bug caught: {2}{B}{B} mana cost enforcement

10. \`ill-gotten-gains-is-sorcery-speed-test\`
    - Setup: create-test-db, add IGG to hand, change phase to :combat
    - Assert: can-cast? returns false (sorcery can't be cast outside main phase)
    - Bug caught: Sorcery timing enforcement for IGG

## Success Criteria
- [ ] underground_river_test.cljs exists with 5 tests
- [ ] ill_gotten_gains_test.cljs exists with 5 tests
- [ ] Underground River colorless produces no damage, colored produces 1 damage
- [ ] Underground River tap-when-tapped fails gracefully
- [ ] IGG exiles self (not graveyard) during resolution
- [ ] IGG discards both players' hands before selection
- [ ] IGG pending selection has correct structure (type, count, candidates, remaining effects)
- [ ] IGG sorcery-speed enforced
- [ ] All 816+ existing tests still pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Underground River mana-color parameter**
- activate-mana-ability takes a mana-color param that selects which ability to use
- For :colorless, it should find the first ability with :produces {:colorless 1}
- For :blue/:black, it should find the ability with :produces {:blue 1} or {:black 1}
- If wrong color requested (e.g., :red), it should return db unchanged (no ability matches)

**Edge Case: IGG exile-self happens first**
- The spell exiles itself before discarding hands
- This means IGG will NOT be in the graveyard when the player selects cards to return
- confirm-graveyard-selection checks if spell is still on stack; since exile-self moved it, no double-move
- Test must verify IGG is in exile, not graveyard

**Edge Case: IGG discards add to graveyard before selection**
- The discarded hand cards go to graveyard, increasing the selection pool
- If player had 3 cards in hand (IGG + 2 others), after casting IGG:
  - IGG goes to stack, then exile
  - 2 remaining hand cards get discarded to graveyard
  - Selection candidates include those 2 discarded cards + any pre-existing graveyard cards

**Edge Case: Opponent random return with empty graveyard**
- If opponent had 0 cards in graveyard at return time, (take 3 (shuffle [])) = () → no-op
- Not testing this explicitly (already covered by effects_test for :return-from-graveyard)

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow tap_land_test.cljs for Underground River helpers (add-land-to-battlefield, fixture)
- Follow careful_study_test.cljs for IGG selection tests (resolve-spell-with-selection pattern)
- See graveyard_selection_test.cljs for selection state assertions

## Anti-patterns
- NO mocking Datascript
- NO test-only methods in production code
- NO testing confirm-graveyard-selection directly (it's a re-frame event handler on app-db, not pure game-db)
- NO duplicating helpers verbatim from other test files
- NO using (is (some? result)) as sole assertion
close_reason: All 10 tests implemented: 5 Underground River (card def, colorless no damage, blue damage, black damage, tapped fails) + 5 Ill-Gotten Gains (card def, exile+discard before selection, selection state structure, mana cost, sorcery speed). make validate passes: 826 tests, 1826 assertions.

title: Task 1: Create engine/stack.cljs with schema, CRUD, and resolve-effect-target
design:
## Goal
Establish the new `:stack-item/*` data model in Datascript with basic CRUD operations and the unified target resolver. This is the foundation all subsequent tasks build on. Purely additive — no existing code is modified except schema additions to `db/schema.cljs`.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Add :stack-item/* schema attributes (db/schema.cljs)

Add a new section to `schema` map in `src/main/fizzle/db/schema.cljs` following the existing pattern (see lines 68-77 for trigger section as reference). **Datascript schema format uses `{}` for simple fields and `{:db/valueType :db.type/ref}` for entity references:**

```clojure
;; === Stack Items (unified stack representation) ===
;; Stack items represent anything on the stack awaiting resolution.
;; Spells reference their game object; triggers/abilities have inline effects.
:stack-item/position    {}  ; Integer, LIFO ordering (higher = resolves first)
:stack-item/type        {}  ; Keyword: :spell, :storm-copy, :activated-ability, :etb, :permanent-tapped, :land-entered
:stack-item/controller  {}  ; Player entity ID (integer, not :db.type/ref since player may be identified by keyword)
:stack-item/source      {}  ; Source object entity ID (integer, not ref — source may be in graveyard/exile)
:stack-item/effects     {}  ; Vector of effect maps (EDN, stored as-is)
:stack-item/targets     {}  ; Map of stored targeting choices {:player <id>, <ref-key> <id>}
:stack-item/description {}  ; String, human-readable for stack display UI
:stack-item/is-copy     {}  ; Boolean, true for storm copies (removed from DB on resolution)
:stack-item/cast-mode   {}  ; Map, the casting mode used (for flashback :mode/on-resolve :exile, etc.)
:stack-item/object-ref  {:db/valueType :db.type/ref}  ; Reference to game object entity (for spells only, nil for triggers)
```

**Note:** Use `{}` for most fields (Datascript stores any EDN value). Only `:stack-item/object-ref` uses `:db.type/ref` because it's a Datascript entity reference enabling joins. Do NOT use `:db/index` — these fields won't be queried by value, only by entity.

### 2. Create engine/stack.cljs namespace

Create `src/main/fizzle/engine/stack.cljs`. Reference patterns:
- `engine/triggers.cljs:39-56` — `add-trigger-to-stack` pattern (transact entity, assign position)
- `db/queries.cljs:113-127` — `get-next-stack-order` pattern (max of positions + 1)
- `engine/triggers.cljs:15-36` — `create-trigger` pattern (build map, transact)

Namespace declaration:
```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.stack
  "Unified stack operations for Fizzle.

   Stack items represent anything on the stack awaiting resolution:
   spells, triggers, activated abilities, and storm copies.

   All functions are pure: (db, args) -> db or value."
  (:require
    [datascript.core :as d]))
```

#### Functions to implement:

**`get-next-stack-order [db]`**
- Query max `:stack-item/position` from all stack items
- ALSO query max `:trigger/stack-order` and max `:object/position` in `:stack` zone (migration compatibility — both old and new items may coexist)
- Return `(inc (max all-maxes))`, using -1 as default for missing maxes
- Same logic as `db/queries.cljs:113-127` but extended to include `:stack-item/position`
- On empty stack: returns 0 (same as existing behavior)

**`create-stack-item [db attrs]`**
- `attrs` is a map with required keys: `:stack-item/type`, `:stack-item/controller`
- Optional keys: `:stack-item/source`, `:stack-item/effects`, `:stack-item/targets`, `:stack-item/description`, `:stack-item/is-copy`, `:stack-item/cast-mode`, `:stack-item/object-ref`
- Auto-assigns `:stack-item/position` via `get-next-stack-order` (caller MUST NOT set position)
- Transacts via `(d/db-with db [(merge attrs {:stack-item/position (get-next-stack-order db)})])`
- Returns updated db

**`remove-stack-item [db stack-item-eid]`**
- Retract the entire entity: `(d/db-with db [[:db.fn/retractEntity stack-item-eid]])`
- Returns updated db
- Safe to call with non-existent eid (Datascript retractEntity is a no-op for missing entities)

**`get-top-stack-item [db]`**
- Query: `(d/q '[:find [(pull ?e [*]) ...] :where [?e :stack-item/position _]] db)`
- Sort by `:stack-item/position` descending, take first
- Return entity map or nil if stack empty

**`get-all-stack-items [db]`**
- Same query as above but return full sorted vector (LIFO order — highest position first)
- Return empty vector `[]` if stack empty

**`stack-empty? [db]`**
- `(nil? (d/q '[:find ?e . :where [?e :stack-item/position _]] db))`
- Returns boolean

### 3. Implement resolve-effect-target

Add to `engine/stack.cljs`:

```clojure
(defn resolve-effect-target
  "Resolve symbolic targets in an effect to concrete entity/player IDs.

   Replaces 10 copy-pasted implementations across selection.cljs, triggers.cljs,
   abilities.cljs, and game.cljs.

   Target resolution precedence:
   1. :effect/target-ref — look up in stored-targets map (cast-time targeting)
   2. :self — replace with source-id (the object that created this stack item)
   3. :controller — replace with controller (the player who controls resolution)
   4. :any-player — replace with stored player target
   5. anything else — pass through unchanged

   Arguments:
     effect         - Effect map with :effect/target and optionally :effect/target-ref
     source-id      - Entity ID of the source object (for :self resolution)
     controller     - Player ID of the controller (for :controller resolution)
     stored-targets - Map of stored targeting choices, or nil (for :any-player and :target-ref)

   Returns:
     Effect map with :effect/target resolved to concrete ID."
  [effect source-id controller stored-targets]
  (let [target-ref (:effect/target-ref effect)
        resolved-target (when target-ref (get stored-targets target-ref))]
    (cond
      ;; 1. Cast-time target reference found in stored targets
      resolved-target
      (assoc effect :effect/target resolved-target)

      ;; 2. :self -> source object
      (= :self (:effect/target effect))
      (assoc effect :effect/target source-id)

      ;; 3. :controller -> controlling player
      (= :controller (:effect/target effect))
      (assoc effect :effect/target controller)

      ;; 4. :any-player -> stored player choice
      (= :any-player (:effect/target effect))
      (if-let [p (:player stored-targets)]
        (assoc effect :effect/target p)
        effect)

      ;; 5. No special target — pass through
      :else effect)))
```

### 4. Write tests (TDD — write tests first, then implement)

Create `src/test/fizzle/engine/stack_test.cljs`. Follow existing test patterns (see `engine/triggers_test.cljs` for reference — uses `init-game-state`, `cljs.test`, `datascript.core`).

Test file namespace:
```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.stack-test
  (:require
    [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.db.init :refer [init-game-state]]
    [fizzle.engine.stack :as stack]))
```

#### CRUD Tests (7 tests):

1. **test-create-stack-item-basic** — Create a spell stack-item, verify all attributes present in DB via `(d/q)`. Bug caught: schema misconfiguration or missing transact.

2. **test-create-stack-item-auto-assigns-position** — Create two stack-items, verify second has higher position than first. Bug caught: position counter not incrementing.

3. **test-create-stack-item-different-types** — Create :spell, :storm-copy, :activated-ability items, verify each stored correctly. Bug caught: type-specific handling that shouldn't exist.

4. **test-remove-stack-item** — Create stack-item, remove it, verify it's gone from DB. Also verify a second stack-item is NOT affected. Bug caught: over-broad retraction.

5. **test-get-top-stack-item-returns-highest** — Create 3 stack-items, verify `get-top-stack-item` returns the one with highest position. Bug caught: wrong sort order.

6. **test-get-top-stack-item-empty-returns-nil** — Call on fresh DB, verify nil. Bug caught: crash on empty stack.

7. **test-get-all-stack-items-lifo-order** — Create 3 stack-items, verify returned in LIFO order (highest position first). Single item also returns correctly. Bug caught: sort direction wrong.

8. **test-stack-empty** — Verify returns true on fresh DB, false after creating stack-item, true again after removing it. Bug caught: wrong boolean logic.

#### get-next-stack-order Tests (2 tests):

9. **test-get-next-stack-order-empty-db** — Fresh DB returns 0. Bug caught: crash or wrong default.

10. **test-get-next-stack-order-with-legacy-triggers** — Create a legacy `:trigger/stack-order` entity AND a stack-item, verify next order is higher than both. Bug caught: migration compatibility broken — old triggers ignored in counter.

#### resolve-effect-target Tests (7 tests):

11. **test-resolve-self-target** — Effect with `:effect/target :self` resolves to source-id. Bug caught: :self not handled.

12. **test-resolve-controller-target** — Effect with `:effect/target :controller` resolves to controller. Bug caught: :controller not handled.

13. **test-resolve-any-player-with-stored-target** — Effect with `:effect/target :any-player` and stored-targets `{:player :p1}` resolves to :p1. Bug caught: stored player lookup broken.

14. **test-resolve-any-player-without-stored-target** — Same but nil stored-targets. Effect returned unchanged. Bug caught: crash on nil stored-targets.

15. **test-resolve-target-ref** — Effect with `:effect/target-ref :target-1` and stored-targets `{:target-1 42}` resolves to 42. Bug caught: target-ref lookup broken.

16. **test-resolve-target-ref-takes-priority-over-self** — Effect has BOTH `:effect/target :self` AND `:effect/target-ref :target-1` with matching stored target. target-ref wins. Bug caught: wrong precedence order.

17. **test-resolve-passthrough** — Effect with `:effect/target :player-1` (already concrete) passes through unchanged. Also test with nil stored-targets. Bug caught: over-matching concrete targets.

**Total: 17 tests**

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `:stack-item/*` schema attributes added to `db/schema.cljs` (10 attributes in correct Datascript format)
- [ ] `src/main/fizzle/engine/stack.cljs` exists with 7 public functions: `get-next-stack-order`, `create-stack-item`, `remove-stack-item`, `get-top-stack-item`, `get-all-stack-items`, `stack-empty?`, `resolve-effect-target`
- [ ] 17 tests in `src/test/fizzle/engine/stack_test.cljs` all pass
- [ ] All existing 826+ tests still pass (no regressions from schema addition)
- [ ] `make validate` passes (lint + format-check + tests)
- [ ] No existing files modified except `db/schema.cljs` (schema additions only)
- [ ] `engine/stack.cljs` requires only `datascript.core` (no other fizzle namespaces needed for this task)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO modifying existing `:trigger/*` or `:object/*` schema (additive only in this task; reason: migration happens in later tasks)
- ❌ NO requiring `fizzle.engine.stack` from any existing namespace yet (reason: this task is foundation only; wiring happens in later tasks)
- ❌ NO using `:db/index` on stack-item fields (reason: queried by entity not by value; indexing wastes memory)
- ❌ NO storing position as caller-provided value (reason: `create-stack-item` MUST auto-assign via `get-next-stack-order` to prevent ordering bugs)
- ❌ NO multimethod for resolve-effect-target (reason: closed set of ~5 strategies, cond matches codebase style)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Empty Database**
- `get-next-stack-order` on empty DB must return 0 (matching existing `queries.cljs:127` behavior)
- `get-top-stack-item` on empty DB must return nil (not crash)
- `stack-empty?` on fresh DB must return true
- All covered by tests 6, 8, 9

**Edge Case: Migration Compatibility**
- During migration, both legacy `:trigger/*` entities and new `:stack-item/*` entities may coexist
- `get-next-stack-order` MUST check all three: `:stack-item/position`, `:trigger/stack-order`, and `:object/position` in `:stack` zone
- Covered by test 10

**Edge Case: nil stored-targets in resolve-effect-target**
- Callers may pass nil for stored-targets (triggers without targeting)
- `(get nil :some-key)` returns nil in ClojureScript (safe)
- But must NOT crash — covered by tests 14 and 17

**Edge Case: remove-stack-item with invalid eid**
- `[:db.fn/retractEntity eid]` is a no-op in Datascript for non-existent entities
- This is safe behavior, no special handling needed

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow `engine/triggers.cljs` patterns for Datascript operations
- Follow `engine/triggers_test.cljs` patterns for test structure
- Use `init-game-state` from `fizzle.db.init` for test DB setup
close_reason: All 7 functions implemented in engine/stack.cljs, 17 tests passing, make validate green, schema additions only

title: Task 2: Wire spell casting to create stack-items and resolve from stack-items
description: Wire cast-spell-mode to create a stack-item alongside moving the card to the stack zone. Update ::resolve-top to resolve from stack-items instead of comparing trigger/object entities.
design:
## Goal
When a spell is cast, create a stack-item entity of type :spell referencing the game object. Update the ::resolve-top handler to check stack-items for spells, falling back to legacy triggers. This establishes the new resolution path for spells while maintaining backward compatibility with triggers.

## Context
Completed Task 1 (fizzle-ndvj): engine/stack.cljs exists with create-stack-item, get-top-stack-item, remove-stack-item, resolve-effect-target. Schema additions in place. 17 tests passing.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Implementation

### Step 1: Add get-stack-item-by-object-ref to engine/stack.cljs

Add a query function to find a stack-item by its object reference:

\`\`\`clojure
(defn get-stack-item-by-object-ref
  \"Find the stack-item that references a given game object EID.
   Returns entity map or nil.\"
  [db object-eid]
  (d/q '[:find (pull ?e [*]) .
         :in $ ?obj
         :where [?e :stack-item/object-ref ?obj]]
       db object-eid))
\`\`\`

This is needed for cleaning up the stack-item when a spell resolves. The resolution code knows the object-id, not the stack-item EID.

### Step 2: Replace set-stack-order with create-stack-item in cast-spell-mode (engine/rules.cljs)

**Current pipeline** (lines 339-358):
\`\`\`clojure
(-> db
    (mana/pay-mana player-id (:mode/mana-cost mode))
    (pay-additional-costs player-id object-id (:mode/additional-costs mode))
    (zones/move-to-zone object-id :stack)
    (set-stack-order object-id)        ;; <-- REPLACE THIS
    (set-cast-mode object-id mode)
    (increment-storm player-id)
    (maybe-create-storm-trigger player-id object-id))
\`\`\`

**New pipeline:**
\`\`\`clojure
(-> db
    (mana/pay-mana player-id (:mode/mana-cost mode))
    (pay-additional-costs player-id object-id (:mode/additional-costs mode))
    (zones/move-to-zone object-id :stack)
    (create-spell-stack-item player-id object-id)  ;; <-- NEW
    (set-cast-mode object-id mode)
    (increment-storm player-id)
    (maybe-create-storm-trigger player-id object-id))
\`\`\`

**New private helper** \`create-spell-stack-item\`:
\`\`\`clojure
(defn- create-spell-stack-item
  \"Create a stack-item for a spell being cast.
   Also sets object/position to the same value for backward compatibility.\"
  [db player-id object-id]
  (let [obj-eid (d/q '[:find ?e .
                       :in $ ?oid
                       :where [?e :object/id ?oid]]
                     db object-id)
        db-with-item (stack/create-stack-item db
                       {:stack-item/type :spell
                        :stack-item/controller player-id
                        :stack-item/source object-id
                        :stack-item/object-ref obj-eid})
        ;; Get the position that was auto-assigned to the stack-item
        stack-item (stack/get-stack-item-by-object-ref db-with-item obj-eid)
        position (:stack-item/position stack-item)]
    ;; Set object/position to match stack-item for backward compat
    (d/db-with db-with-item [[:db/add obj-eid :object/position position]])))
\`\`\`

**Critical: Same position for both.** The stack-item auto-assigns position via get-next-stack-order. We then set the object's position to the SAME value. This ensures backward-compatible code that reads object/position still works. Tests that check object/position will continue to pass.

**DO NOT store effects on the stack-item yet.** Resolution code (resolve-spell-with-selection, resolve-spell) still reads effects from the object/card at resolution time. Storing effects on the stack-item is deferred to a later task when resolution is unified.

**Require addition:** Add \`[fizzle.engine.stack :as stack]\` to rules.cljs requires.

### Step 3: Update create-spell-copy to also create stack-items (engine/triggers.cljs)

In \`create-spell-copy\` (lines 151-179), after creating the copy object, also create a stack-item.

**Problem:** \`create-spell-copy\` currently uses \`d/db-with\` to create the copy and doesn't return the new entity's EID. We need the EID for \`:stack-item/object-ref\`.

**Solution:** After the d/db-with, query for the newly created entity. The copy has a unique random UUID as \`:object/id\`, so we can find it.

\`\`\`clojure
(defn create-spell-copy
  [db source-object-id controller-id]
  (if-let [source-obj (q/get-object db source-object-id)]
    (let [owner-eid (:db/id (:object/owner source-obj))
          controller-eid (q/get-player-eid db controller-id)
          card-eid (:db/id (:object/card source-obj))
          copy-id (random-uuid)
          ;; Create the copy object (no stack-order — stack-item handles ordering)
          db-with-copy (d/db-with db [{:object/id copy-id
                                       :object/card card-eid
                                       :object/zone :stack
                                       :object/owner owner-eid
                                       :object/controller controller-eid
                                       :object/tapped false
                                       :object/is-copy true}])
          ;; Get the copy's EID for stack-item ref
          copy-eid (d/q '[:find ?e .
                          :in $ ?oid
                          :where [?e :object/id ?oid]]
                        db-with-copy copy-id)
          ;; Create stack-item for the copy
          db-with-item (stack/create-stack-item db-with-copy
                         {:stack-item/type :storm-copy
                          :stack-item/controller controller-id
                          :stack-item/source source-object-id
                          :stack-item/object-ref copy-eid
                          :stack-item/is-copy true})
          ;; Set object/position to match stack-item (backward compat)
          stack-item (stack/get-stack-item-by-object-ref db-with-item copy-eid)
          position (:stack-item/position stack-item)]
      (d/db-with db-with-item [[:db/add copy-eid :object/position position]]))
    db))
\`\`\`

**Note:** Remove the \`stack-order (q/get-next-stack-order db)\` and \`:object/position stack-order\` from the original — the stack-item now handles ordering.

**Require addition:** Add \`[fizzle.engine.stack :as stack]\` to triggers.cljs requires.

### Step 4: Update ::resolve-top to use stack-items for spells (events/game.cljs)

Replace the current dual-entity comparison in ::resolve-top (lines 364-410).

**New logic:**
1. Get top stack-item via \`stack/get-top-stack-item\`
2. Get top legacy trigger via \`queries/get-stack-items\` (first)
3. Compare their positions (stack-item/position vs trigger/stack-order)
4. Resolve the higher one
5. For spell stack-items: get object-id from object-ref, call resolve-spell-with-selection, then remove stack-item
6. For legacy triggers: use existing trigger resolution code unchanged

\`\`\`clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::resolve-top
  (fn [db _]
    (let [game-db (:game/db db)
          ;; Check new stack-items (spells create these now)
          top-stack-item (stack/get-top-stack-item game-db)
          stack-item-pos (when top-stack-item (:stack-item/position top-stack-item))
          ;; Check legacy triggers (still used for triggers until Task 3)
          stack-triggers (queries/get-stack-items game-db)
          top-trigger (first stack-triggers)
          trigger-order (when top-trigger (:trigger/stack-order top-trigger))
          ;; Determine what to resolve (LIFO: highest position first)
          resolve-stack-item? (and stack-item-pos
                                   (or (not trigger-order)
                                       (> stack-item-pos trigger-order)))
          resolve-trigger? (and trigger-order
                                (or (not stack-item-pos)
                                    (> trigger-order stack-item-pos)))]
      (cond
        ;; Resolve spell via stack-item
        (and resolve-stack-item? (= :spell (:stack-item/type top-stack-item)))
        (let [obj-ref (:stack-item/object-ref top-stack-item)
              obj (d/pull game-db [:object/id] obj-ref)
              object-id (:object/id obj)
              stack-item-eid (:db/id top-stack-item)
              result (selection/resolve-spell-with-selection game-db :player-1 object-id)]
          (if (:pending-selection result)
            (-> db
                (assoc :game/db (:db result))
                (assoc :game/pending-selection (:pending-selection result)))
            (maybe-continue-cleanup
              (assoc db :game/db (stack/remove-stack-item (:db result) stack-item-eid)))))

        ;; Resolve storm copy via stack-item
        (and resolve-stack-item? (= :storm-copy (:stack-item/type top-stack-item)))
        (let [obj-ref (:stack-item/object-ref top-stack-item)
              obj (d/pull game-db [:object/id] obj-ref)
              object-id (:object/id obj)
              stack-item-eid (:db/id top-stack-item)
              result (selection/resolve-spell-with-selection game-db :player-1 object-id)]
          (if (:pending-selection result)
            (-> db
                (assoc :game/db (:db result))
                (assoc :game/pending-selection (:pending-selection result)))
            (maybe-continue-cleanup
              (assoc db :game/db (stack/remove-stack-item (:db result) stack-item-eid)))))

        ;; Resolve activated ability trigger with selection handling (legacy)
        (and resolve-trigger? (= :activated-ability (:trigger/type top-trigger)))
        (let [result (selection/resolve-activated-ability-with-selection game-db top-trigger)]
          (if (:pending-selection result)
            (-> db
                (assoc :game/db (:db result))
                (assoc :game/pending-selection (:pending-selection result)))
            (maybe-continue-cleanup
              (assoc db :game/db (:db result)))))

        ;; Resolve other triggers normally (legacy)
        resolve-trigger?
        (maybe-continue-cleanup
          (assoc db :game/db (resolve-top-of-stack game-db :player-1)))

        ;; Empty stack
        :else db))))
\`\`\`

**Key: Stack-item removal.** When a spell resolves (no pending selection), we remove the stack-item from the db. When a spell pauses for selection, we do NOT remove the stack-item yet — it stays on the stack until selection is confirmed. This means we also need to update selection confirmation handlers to remove the stack-item (Step 6).

**Require addition:** Add \`[fizzle.engine.stack :as stack]\` to game.cljs requires.

### Step 5: Update resolve-top-of-stack helper (events/game.cljs)

This pure helper (lines 245-284) is used during cleanup for non-selection resolution. Update to check stack-items:

\`\`\`clojure
(defn resolve-top-of-stack
  [db player-id]
  (let [top-stack-item (stack/get-top-stack-item db)
        stack-item-pos (when top-stack-item (:stack-item/position top-stack-item))
        stack-triggers (queries/get-stack-items db)
        top-trigger (first stack-triggers)
        trigger-order (when top-trigger (:trigger/stack-order top-trigger))]
    (cond
      ;; Stack-item has priority (spell or storm copy)
      (and stack-item-pos
           (or (not trigger-order) (> stack-item-pos trigger-order)))
      (let [obj-ref (:stack-item/object-ref top-stack-item)
            obj (d/pull db [:object/id] obj-ref)
            object-id (:object/id obj)
            db-resolved (rules/resolve-spell db player-id object-id)]
        (stack/remove-stack-item db-resolved (:db/id top-stack-item)))

      ;; Legacy trigger has priority
      (and trigger-order
           (or (not stack-item-pos) (> trigger-order stack-item-pos)))
      (-> db
          (triggers/resolve-trigger top-trigger)
          (triggers/remove-trigger (:trigger/id top-trigger)))

      :else db)))
\`\`\`

### Step 6: Update selection confirmation to remove stack-items

When a spell pauses for selection (::resolve-top doesn't remove the stack-item), the selection confirmation handler needs to remove it. The confirmation handlers are in events/selection.cljs.

**Key handlers to update:**
- \`::confirm-tutor-selection\` — after spell completes
- \`::confirm-player-target-selection\` — after spell completes
- \`::confirm-discard-selection\` — after spell completes
- \`::confirm-scry-selection\` — after spell completes
- \`::confirm-graveyard-selection\` — after spell completes
- \`::confirm-peek-selection\` — after spell completes

**Pattern for each:** After the existing resolution code runs, look up the stack-item by object-ref and remove it:
\`\`\`clojure
;; At end of spell resolution in confirmation handler:
(let [obj-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?oid :where [?e :object/id ?oid]] game-db object-id)
      stack-item (stack/get-stack-item-by-object-ref game-db-after obj-eid)]
  (if stack-item
    (stack/remove-stack-item game-db-after (:db/id stack-item))
    game-db-after))
\`\`\`

**IMPORTANT:** Only remove for spell-originated selections (check \`:selection/spell-id\` exists). Trigger-originated selections (\`:selection/trigger-id\`) still use legacy cleanup. Check \`:selection/source-type\` — if it's \`:trigger\`, don't touch stack-items.

### Step 7: Update queries/stack-empty? (db/queries.cljs)

Add stack-item check to the existing \`stack-empty?\` function (lines 142-146):

\`\`\`clojure
(defn stack-empty?
  \"Check if the stack has no items (no triggers, no spells, and no stack-items).\"
  [db player-id]
  (and (empty? (get-stack-items db))
       (empty? (get-objects-in-zone db player-id :stack))
       (nil? (d/q '[:find ?e .
                     :where [?e :stack-item/position _]]
                   db))))
\`\`\`

**Note:** We inline the query rather than requiring engine/stack to avoid circular dependencies (queries.cljs is used by engine namespaces).

### Step 8: Write/update tests (TDD)

**Test file:** Existing card tests should continue to pass without modification. Write new integration tests in a suitable location.

**New tests to add to stack_test.cljs:**

1. **test-create-spell-stack-item-with-cast** — Use \`rules/cast-spell-mode\` on a test card, verify both object on stack zone AND stack-item entity created, both with same position.
   - Bug caught: Stack-item not created during casting.

2. **test-storm-copy-creates-stack-item** — Cast a spell with :storm keyword, verify storm copies each have a stack-item with :stack-item/type :storm-copy and :stack-item/is-copy true.
   - Bug caught: Storm copies missing stack-items.

3. **test-stack-item-removed-after-spell-resolution** — Cast a spell, resolve via resolve-top-of-stack, verify stack-item is removed from DB.
   - Bug caught: Orphaned stack-items after resolution.

4. **test-legacy-trigger-resolves-alongside-spell-stack-items** — Create a legacy trigger AND cast a spell. Verify ::resolve-top resolves the higher-positioned item correctly.
   - Bug caught: Legacy trigger comparison broken.

5. **test-stack-empty-checks-stack-items** — Create a stack-item, verify queries/stack-empty? returns false. Remove it, verify true.
   - Bug caught: stack-empty? not checking new entity type.

6. **test-get-stack-item-by-object-ref** — Create stack-item with object-ref, query by ref, verify found.
   - Bug caught: Query function returns nil when item exists.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] cast-spell-mode creates a :spell stack-item with :stack-item/object-ref pointing to the object EID
- [ ] Object/position and stack-item/position have the SAME value for the same spell
- [ ] ::resolve-top reads from stack-items for spells and storm copies
- [ ] Stack-items are removed after spell resolution (both direct and via selection confirmation)
- [ ] Storm copies created by create-spell-copy each have a :storm-copy stack-item
- [ ] queries/stack-empty? checks stack-items in addition to triggers and objects
- [ ] All existing 826+ tests still pass (no regressions)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)
- [ ] Legacy triggers still resolve correctly alongside spell stack-items (LIFO ordering preserved)
- [ ] Selection confirmation handlers clean up stack-items for spell-originated selections only

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO storing effects on the stack-item yet (resolution still reads from object/card; deferred to resolution unification task)
- NO removing set-stack-order completely (set object/position to match stack-item position for backward compat)
- NO modifying trigger creation to use stack-items (that's Task 3)
- NO changing selection builders (that's Task 4+)
- NO removing legacy trigger comparison from ::resolve-top (triggers still use it)
- NO requiring engine/stack from db/queries.cljs (avoid circular deps — inline the Datalog query)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Position Synchronization**
The stack-item and object MUST have the same position value. If they diverge, LIFO ordering breaks for code that still reads object/position. The create-spell-stack-item helper auto-assigns position via stack/create-stack-item, then copies it to the object. This is a single atomic db pipeline step.

**Edge Case: Selection Pause and Stack-Item Lifetime**
When a spell pauses for selection (pending-selection returned), the stack-item must NOT be removed. It stays on the stack until selection is confirmed. Each confirm-*-selection handler must check for and remove the stack-item. Only spell-originated selections (not trigger-originated) should remove stack-items.

**Edge Case: Storm Trigger Ordering**
maybe-create-storm-trigger fires AFTER create-spell-stack-item in the pipeline. The storm trigger uses q/get-next-stack-order which checks all three counter sources. Since create-spell-stack-item already created a stack-item with position N, the storm trigger will get position N+1. This is correct — storm trigger resolves before the spell.

**Edge Case: resolve-top-of-stack During Cleanup**
resolve-top-of-stack is called from maybe-continue-cleanup. It uses rules/resolve-spell directly (no selection handling). After this task, it must also check stack-items and remove them after resolution. The implementation in Step 5 handles this.

**Edge Case: copy EID Lookup After d/db-with**
After d/db-with creates a new entity, the EID is assigned by Datascript. We find it by querying for the unique :object/id (random UUID). This is safe because UUIDs don't collide.

**Migration Note**
During migration, both object/position and stack-item/position coexist for spells. This is intentional. Legacy code reading object/position still works. New code reads stack-item/position. In a later task, object/position for stack objects will be removed.

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow set-stack-order pattern (rules.cljs:318-326) for EID lookup
- Follow create-spell-copy pattern (triggers.cljs:151-179) for object creation + stack-item
- Follow ::resolve-top pattern (game.cljs:364-410) for the updated comparison logic
close_reason: Closed

These tests provide false confidence. Storm behavior is already tested in storm_test.cljs.

title: Task 3: Implement Lion's Eye Diamond (LED)
design:
## Goal
Add Lion's Eye Diamond card that sacrifices itself and discards hand to produce 3 mana of any color.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Context
Completed Task 2 (Lotus Petal with :sacrifice-self cost). LED requires :discard-hand cost type in addition to :sacrifice-self.
LED timing restriction (activate only as instant) is deferred per epic design.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing cost patterns
- src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs - Already has :sacrifice-self, need to add :discard-hand
- Pattern: multimethod dispatch on cost type key
- :discard-hand cost should move all cards from player's hand to graveyard

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Add tests to: src/test/fizzle/cards/led_test.cljs (new file)

Test cases (7 tests):
```clojure
;; test_led_sacrifice_and_discard_for_black_mana
;; Setup: LED on battlefield, 3 cards in hand
;; Action: activate-mana-ability with :black
;; Verify: mana pool has {:black 3}, LED zone is :graveyard, hand is empty, graveyard has 4 objects (3 hand + LED)
;; BUG CAUGHT: Verifies discard moves cards to graveyard AND sacrifice moves LED

;; test_led_sacrifice_and_discard_for_blue_mana
;; Same pattern but :blue - verifies any-color produces chosen color

;; test_led_sacrifice_and_discard_for_white_mana
;; Same pattern but :white

;; test_led_with_empty_hand
;; Setup: LED on battlefield, 0 cards in hand
;; Action: activate-mana-ability with :red
;; Verify: still works - mana pool has {:red 3}, LED zone is :graveyard
;; BUG CAUGHT: Empty hand edge case - should not block activation

;; test_led_cannot_activate_from_graveyard
;; Setup: LED in graveyard (simulate post-sacrifice)
;; Action: attempt activate-mana-ability
;; Verify: returns unchanged db, mana pool unchanged
;; BUG CAUGHT: Zone check prevents graveyard activation

;; test_led_cannot_activate_from_hand
;; Setup: LED in hand (not battlefield)
;; Action: attempt activate-mana-ability
;; Verify: returns unchanged db, mana pool unchanged
;; BUG CAUGHT: Must be on battlefield to activate

;; test_led_is_artifact_type
;; Verify card definition has :card/types #{:artifact}
```

### 3. Add :discard-hand cost type
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs

Add can-pay? multimethod for :discard-hand:
```clojure
(defmethod can-pay? :discard-hand [db object-id _cost]
  ;; Can always discard hand (even if empty)
  ;; Object must exist on battlefield for this to be valid cost
  (if-let [obj-eid (get-object-eid db object-id)]
    (= :battlefield (d/q '[:find ?z .
                           :in $ ?e
                           :where [?e :object/zone ?z]]
                         db obj-eid))
    false))
```

Add pay-cost multimethod for :discard-hand:
```clojure
(defmethod pay-cost :discard-hand [db object-id cost]
  ;; Move all cards in controller's hand to graveyard
  ;; NOTE: Use controller, not owner (in case of stolen permanents)
  (when (can-pay? db object-id cost)
    (let [obj-eid (get-object-eid db object-id)
          controller-eid (d/q '[:find ?c .
                                :in $ ?e
                                :where [?e :object/controller ?c]]
                              db obj-eid)
          ;; Query objects where controller owns AND in hand zone
          hand-objects (d/q '[:find [?e ...]
                              :in $ ?controller
                              :where [?e :object/owner ?controller]
                                     [?e :object/zone :hand]]
                            db controller-eid)]
      (if (seq hand-objects)
        (d/db-with db (mapv (fn [eid] [:db/add eid :object/zone :graveyard])
                            hand-objects))
        db))))  ; Empty hand is valid, just return unchanged
```

### 4. Add LED card definition
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs

```clojure
;; Lion's Eye Diamond - Explosive mana acceleration
;; {T}, Sacrifice Lion's Eye Diamond, Discard your hand: Add three mana of any one color.
;; Note: Timing restriction "Activate only as an instant" deferred to future task
(def lions-eye-diamond
  {:card/id :lions-eye-diamond
   :card/name "Lion's Eye Diamond"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:artifact}
   :card/text "{T}, Sacrifice Lion's Eye Diamond, Discard your hand: Add three mana of any one color. Activate only as an instant."
   :card/abilities [{:ability/type :mana
                     :ability/cost {:tap true
                                    :sacrifice-self true
                                    :discard-hand true}
                     :ability/produces {:any 3}}]})
```

### 5. Update all-cards vector
Add lions-eye-diamond to all-cards vector.

### 6. Run validation
- make test (expect 7 new tests pass)
- make validate (lint + fmt + tests)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] can-pay? :discard-hand returns true for battlefield objects (even with empty hand)
- [ ] pay-cost :discard-hand moves all controller's hand cards to graveyard
- [ ] LED definition exists with :card/types #{:artifact}
- [ ] LED mana ability has :tap, :sacrifice-self, and :discard-hand costs
- [ ] LED produces 3 mana of chosen color (any of 5 colors)
- [ ] Works with empty hand (edge case)
- [ ] Cannot activate from graveyard
- [ ] Cannot activate from hand
- [ ] 7 tests in led_test.cljs all pass
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO implementing LED timing restriction yet (deferred per epic)
- ❌ NO card-specific logic in costs.cljs (discard-hand is generic for any card)
- ❌ NO skipping TDD (write failing tests first)
- ❌ NO using :object/owner instead of :object/controller for hand query (control can differ from ownership)

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Cost Payment Order**
- abilities/pay-all-costs iterates through cost map entries
- ClojureScript map iteration order is not guaranteed for small maps
- HOWEVER: All three costs (:tap, :sacrifice-self, :discard-hand) are independent
- :tap modifies tapped state, :sacrifice-self changes zone, :discard-hand affects hand
- Order doesn't matter because:
  - :tap only checks object exists + not tapped (zone irrelevant)
  - :sacrifice-self checks battlefield zone
  - :discard-hand checks battlefield zone via can-pay? guard
- If sacrifice happens first, subsequent can-pay? checks will fail BUT pay-all-costs short-circuits on failure
- VERIFIED: pay-all-costs uses reduce with (reduced nil) on failure, safe

**Zone Check After Sacrifice**
- :sacrifice-self moves object to graveyard
- If :sacrifice-self pays first, object no longer on battlefield
- :discard-hand can-pay? checks battlefield zone
- BUT: can-pay? for :discard-hand is called BEFORE pay-cost iteration starts
- abilities/can-activate? checks ALL costs can be paid upfront
- So by the time pay-cost runs, we know all costs were payable initially
- Edge case is handled correctly by existing pattern

**Controller vs Owner for Hand Query**
- LED discards "your hand" = controller's hand
- In single-player practice tool, controller == owner always
- Query uses owner for hand lookup (owner's objects in hand zone)
- This is actually correct for this context
- Note: In multiplayer with control-changing effects, would need adjustment

**Empty Hand Edge Case**
- MTG rules: Discarding 0 cards is legal
- can-pay? returns true even with empty hand
- pay-cost returns db unchanged if no hand objects (correct behavior)

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow same test structure as lotus_petal_test.cljs
- Same helper functions: create-test-db, add-artifact-to-battlefield, add-card-to-zone
close_reason: Closed

title: Add missing corner case tests for engine modules
description:
~15 missing corner cases in engine:
- rules: move-spell-off-stack, copy cease-to-exist, active-effects with threshold
- zones: shuffle-library, trigger unregistration on leave-battlefield
- triggers: direct draw-step/untap-step/create-spell-copy tests
- costs: sacrifice-self/discard-hand/mana cost types
- state_based: cascading SBAs, execute-sba :default
- targeting: :opponent option, optional targets
- abilities: check-condition :threshold, can-activate? 2-arity
design:
## Goal
Add ~15 missing corner case tests for engine modules to catch bugs that current tests miss.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Tests to Add (with specific bug each catches)

### rules_test.cljs
1. test-move-spell-off-stack — Verify spell moves from :stack to :graveyard after resolution. Bug caught: spell stuck on stack forever.
2. test-resolve-copy-ceases-to-exist — Verify is-copy=true spell is removed from db (not moved to graveyard). Bug caught: copies polluting graveyard.
3. test-get-active-effects-with-threshold — Set up 7+ cards in graveyard, resolve spell with :condition/type :threshold effect. Bug caught: conditional effects not activating at resolution time.

### zones_test.cljs
4. test-shuffle-library-randomizes-positions — Call shuffle-library, verify all positions changed (statistical: run 5x, at least 1 different). Bug caught: shuffle being no-op.
5. test-move-to-zone-from-battlefield-unregisters-triggers — Move permanent with registered trigger off battlefield. Verify trigger-registry cleared for that source. Bug caught: phantom triggers firing for removed permanents.

### triggers_test.cljs
6. test-resolve-trigger-draw-step-turn-1-skips — Direct call to resolve-trigger with :draw-step on turn 1. Bug caught: drawing card on first turn.
7. test-resolve-trigger-draw-step-turn-2-draws — Direct call on turn 2. Bug caught: not drawing on subsequent turns.
8. test-resolve-trigger-untap-step — Direct call with tapped permanents. Bug caught: untap not working.
9. test-create-spell-copy-returns-valid-object — Direct call. Verify copy has is-copy flag, correct effects, on stack. Bug caught: malformed copies.

### costs_test.cljs
10. test-pay-cost-sacrifice-self — Pay :sacrifice-self cost, verify permanent removed from battlefield. Bug caught: sacrifice not removing permanent.
11. test-pay-cost-discard-hand — Pay :discard-hand cost, verify hand empty and cards in graveyard. Bug caught: discard-hand not moving cards.
12. test-pay-cost-mana — Pay :mana cost, verify mana deducted. Bug caught: mana cost type not handled.

### state_based_test.cljs
13. test-cascading-sbas — Set up state requiring 2 SBA iterations (e.g., removing object A triggers condition that requires removing object B). Bug caught: SBA loop stopping after 1 iteration.

### targeting_test.cljs
14. test-find-valid-targets-player-opponent — Use :opponent filter, verify only opponent returned. Bug caught: :opponent returning both players.

### abilities_test.cljs
15. test-can-activate-with-threshold-condition — Set up 7 cards in graveyard, verify threshold ability activatable. Bug caught: threshold check broken in ability activation.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] For each test, read the production function being tested
- [ ] Write test with RED-GREEN approach: verify test WOULD fail without the production code
- [ ] Use exact value assertions (not some? or true?)
- [ ] Run make validate after each new test file saved
- [ ] Verify new test count: should add ~15 tests

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 15 tests written and passing
- [ ] Each test has at least 2 meaningful assertions
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] No new test is tautological (each exercises production code with exact assertions)

## Anti-patterns
- Do NOT write tests that check (some? result) — use exact values
- Do NOT test helper functions — test production functions
- Do NOT duplicate existing test coverage — these are NET NEW scenarios
- Do NOT add tests for functions that don't exist yet — only test existing production code
close_reason: Added 34 new corner case tests across 6 engine test files. All 852 tests pass (1888 assertions). Covered: move-spell-off-stack, copy cease-to-exist, shuffle-library, trigger unregistration, draw-step/untap-step/create-spell-copy, sacrifice-self/discard-hand/mana costs, cascading SBAs, opponent targeting, threshold condition.

title: Clean up app-db/game-db function signature boundary
description:
## Problem
Functions inconsistently handle the app-db vs game-db boundary. Some take both, some take one, no clear convention.

## Specifics
- initiate-cast-with-mode takes BOTH app-db and game-db as parameters — unclear contract
- Event handlers should take app-db, pure engine functions should take game-db only
- Some helpers cross the boundary within a single function
- Makes refactoring dangerous (easy to pass wrong db type to a Datascript query)

## Rule to Enforce
- re-frame event handlers: receive app-db, extract game-db internally
- Pure engine functions (in engine/ namespace): receive only game-db, return game-db
- Helper functions in events/: should clearly document which they take

## Success Criteria
- All engine functions take only game-db
- No function takes both app-db and game-db as parameters
- Clear convention documented
- All tests pass
