title: Task 3: Create interactive UI components for goldfishing
design:
## Goal
Create Reagent view components that enable interactive goldfishing: clickable hand cards, Cast/Resolve buttons, mana pool display, storm count, and stack view. This completes the Phase 1 milestone.

## Context
Completed Task 1 (re-frame infrastructure) and Task 2 (events/subscriptions wrapping engine). Events and subscriptions are fully wired. Now need components that subscribe to state and dispatch events.

## Implementation

### 1. Update core.cljs with interactive app component

Replace the text-only display with a full interactive layout. All views can live in core.cljs for now (3 cards, simple UI) - extract to separate files only if it becomes unwieldy.

Component structure:
- Hand: shows each card as a clickable div with card name, highlights selected
- Mana pool: shows each color with non-zero amount
- Storm count: shows current storm count
- Stack: shows items on stack (triggers and spells) with names
- Controls: Cast button (enabled when card selected and castable), Resolve button (enabled when stack non-empty)

Key events to dispatch:
- `::events/select-card` on card click (pass object-id)
- `::events/cast-spell` on Cast button click
- `::events/resolve-top` on Resolve button click

Subscriptions to consume:
- `::subs/hand` - cards in hand
- `::subs/mana-pool` - mana by color
- `::subs/storm-count` - storm count
- `::subs/stack` - stack items
- `::subs/selected-card` - currently selected card
- `::subs/can-cast?` - whether selected card is castable

### 2. Styling
Inline styles only (anti-pattern forbids CSS frameworks).
- Cards: bordered divs, highlighted border when selected
- Mana: colored text or symbols for each mana color
- Buttons: basic button styling, disabled state visible

### 3. Verify in browser
- Run `make dev` and open localhost:8080
- Verify: 3 cards in hand, 2 black mana, 0 storm
- Click Dark Ritual -> Cast -> mana becomes BBB+1=4B (started with 2, -1 cost, +3 from effect... wait: start 2B, cast DR costs 1B -> 1B remaining, resolve DR adds 3B -> 4B)
- Actually: casting puts on stack (pays mana), resolving executes effects
- Flow: Select DR -> Cast (pays 1B, moves to stack, storm=1) -> Resolve (adds 3B, moves to graveyard) -> mana pool should show 4B

### 4. Run make validate

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Browser loads at localhost:8080 without console errors
- [ ] Hand shows 3 clickable cards with names (Dark Ritual, Brain Freeze, Cabal Ritual)
- [ ] Clicking a card highlights it and shows it as selected
- [ ] Cast button works when card selected and castable
- [ ] Resolve button works when stack has items
- [ ] Mana pool updates correctly after casting and resolving
- [ ] Storm count increments on each cast
- [ ] Stack shows triggers and spells
- [ ] Can complete full combo: DR -> CR -> BF with storm copies
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO game logic in components (subscribe and dispatch only)
- NO direct atom manipulation
- NO CSS frameworks (inline styles only)
- NO new engine code
close_reason: Interactive UI components implemented in core.cljs. Hand with clickable cards, mana pool display, storm count, stack view, and Cast/Resolve buttons. All lint/format/tests pass.

title: Task 2: Implement Ray of Revelation card
design:
## Goal
Implement Ray of Revelation card with Scryfall-accurate oracle text, type-based enchantment targeting, and flashback.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Context
Completed task fizzle-0uj: :destroy effect type now exists and handles target-ref resolution.

## Implementation

### 1. Use implement-card skill
REQUIRED: Use the implement-card skill for Scryfall oracle validation.
The skill will fetch oracle text and validate card definition.

### 2. Card definition structure
File: src/fizzle/cards/ray_of_revelation.cljs (new file)

Following deep_analysis.cljs and recoup.cljs patterns:

```clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.ray-of-revelation)

(def ray-of-revelation
  {:card/id :ray-of-revelation
   :card/name "Ray of Revelation"
   :card/cmc 2
   :card/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :white 1}
   :card/colors #{:white}
   :card/types #{:instant}
   :card/text "Destroy target enchantment.\nFlashback {G}"
   
   ;; Type-based targeting for enchantments on battlefield
   :card/targeting 
   [{:target/id :target-enchantment
     :target/type :object
     :target/zone :battlefield
     :target/controller :any
     :target/criteria {:card/types #{:enchantment}}
     :target/required true}]
   
   ;; Destroy effect using target-ref
   :card/effects 
   [{:effect/type :destroy
     :effect/target-ref :target-enchantment}]
   
   ;; Flashback {G} from graveyard, exile on resolve
   :card/alternate-costs 
   [{:alternate/id :flashback
     :alternate/zone :graveyard
     :alternate/mana-cost {:green 1}
     :alternate/on-resolve :exile}]})
```

### 3. Register card
Add to src/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs card pool (or appropriate registry).

### 4. Write integration tests
File: src/test/fizzle/cards/ray_of_revelation_test.cljs (new file)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] implement-card skill validates oracle text matches Scryfall
- [ ] test-ray-can-cast-targeting-enchantment passes
- [ ] test-ray-cannot-cast-without-enchantment-on-battlefield passes
- [ ] test-ray-flashback-casts-from-graveyard passes
- [ ] test-ray-exiles-after-flashback-resolution passes
- [ ] test-ray-rejects-non-enchantment-targets passes
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + all tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: No enchantments on battlefield**
- Card should NOT be castable when no valid targets exist
- Test: has-valid-targets? returns false with empty battlefield
- Reference: targeting.cljs:80 has-valid-targets?

**Edge Case: Target removed before resolution**
- If enchantment destroyed/exiled in response, effect fizzles
- :destroy effect already handles this (returns db unchanged if target missing)
- Test: Verify spell resolves gracefully when target gone

**Edge Case: Flashback with no targets**
- Same as regular cast - cannot cast flashback if no valid targets
- Flashback changes cost and zone, not targeting requirements

**Oracle Text Accuracy**
- MUST use implement-card skill to validate against Scryfall
- Card text must match exactly: "Destroy target enchantment.\nFlashback {G}"
- Anti-pattern: NO hardcoding text without Scryfall validation

**Reference Implementations**
- src/fizzle/cards/recoup.cljs - Flashback pattern
- src/fizzle/cards/deep_analysis.cljs - Alternate costs with exile
- src/test/fizzle/engine/targeting_test.cljs - Type-based targeting tests

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoding oracle text without Scryfall validation
- ❌ NO skipping implement-card skill
- ❌ NO targeting artifacts (Ray only targets enchantments)
- ❌ NO :effect/target (must use :effect/target-ref with stored targets)
- ❌ NO manual card text that doesn't match Scryfall exactly
close_reason: Closed

title: Add missing P1 corner case tests (cards/events)
design:
## Goal
Add tests for P1 corner cases in card implementations and event handlers.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Corner Cases to Add

### cards/* (P1 - Card Implementations)

| Corner Case | Bug It Catches | Test Name | File |
|-------------|----------------|-----------|------|
| Flashback on exiled card | Playing spells from wrong zone | test-flashback-from-exile-blocked | recoup_test.cljs |
| Targeting object in wrong zone | Invalid target acceptance | test-target-wrong-zone-rejected | targeting_test.cljs |

### events/* (P1 - Event Handlers)

| Corner Case | Bug It Catches | Test Name | File |
|-------------|----------------|-----------|------|
| Event with nil coeffects | Crash on nil db | test-event-nil-cofx-handled | init_game_test.cljs |
| Selection cancel cleanup | State left in pending-selection | test-selection-cancel-cleanup | tutor_multi_select_test.cljs |
| Double-submit selection | Duplicate card movement | test-selection-double-submit | tutor_multi_select_test.cljs |

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Create test-flashback-from-exile-blocked in recoup_test.cljs
- [ ] Create test-target-wrong-zone-rejected in targeting_test.cljs
- [ ] Create test-event-nil-cofx-handled in appropriate event test file
- [ ] Create test-selection-cancel-cleanup in tutor_multi_select_test.cljs
- [ ] Create test-selection-double-submit in tutor_multi_select_test.cljs
- [ ] Run `make test` after each test addition
- [ ] Run `make validate` at the end

## Test Specifications

### test-flashback-from-exile-blocked
```clojure
(deftest test-flashback-from-exile-blocked
  (testing "Flashback only works from graveyard, not exile"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          ;; Add card with flashback to EXILE (not graveyard)
          [db' obj-id] (add-card-to-zone db :recoup :exile :player-1)
          ;; Add mana to pay flashback cost
          db'' (mana/add-mana db' :player-1 {:red 1 :black 1})]
      ;; Should NOT be castable from exile
      (is (false? (rules/can-cast-flashback? db'' :player-1 obj-id))
          "Flashback should not work from exile"))))
```
**Bug it catches:** Allowing flashback from wrong zone

### test-target-wrong-zone-rejected
```clojure
(deftest test-target-wrong-zone-rejected
  (testing "Targeting rejects objects in wrong zone"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          ;; Add card to LIBRARY (not valid target zone)
          [db' obj-id] (add-card-to-zone db :dark-ritual :library :player-1)
          targeting {:target/type :card
                     :target/zone :hand  ;; Expects hand
                     :target/criteria {}}]
      (is (false? (targeting/valid-target? db' obj-id targeting))
          "Object in wrong zone should not be valid target"))))
```
**Bug it catches:** Accepting targets from any zone

### test-selection-cancel-cleanup
```clojure
(deftest test-selection-cancel-cleanup
  (testing "Cancelling selection clears pending state"
    (let [db (create-pending-selection-state)  ;; Helper to set up pending
          db' (game/cancel-selection db)]
      (is (nil? (:pending-selection db'))
          "Pending selection should be cleared after cancel"))))
```
**Bug it catches:** UI stuck in selection mode after cancel

### test-selection-double-submit
```clojure
(deftest test-selection-double-submit
  (testing "Submitting same selection twice is idempotent"
    (let [db (create-pending-selection-state)
          selection {:selection/selected #{card-id}}
          db' (game/execute-selection db selection)
          db'' (game/execute-selection db' selection)]
      ;; Second submit should either:
      ;; - Return db' unchanged (no-op), OR
      ;; - Throw error (rejected)
      ;; Should NOT move card twice
      (is (= 1 (count (get-cards-moved-to-hand db'')))
          "Double submit should not double-move cards"))))
```
**Bug it catches:** Race condition moving cards twice

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 5 corner case tests written and passing
- [ ] Each test has clear comment stating what bug it catches
- [ ] Tests exercise actual production code paths
- [ ] No tautological assertions
- [ ] `make test` passes
- [ ] `make validate` passes

## Key Considerations

**Flashback Zone Check:**
Flashback is specifically a graveyard mechanic. The rules engine should validate zone before allowing flashback cast. If `can-cast-flashback?` doesn't exist, may need to check `can-cast?` with flashback mode.

**Selection Idempotency:**
Selection handlers may need guards against re-processing. Test documents expected behavior.

**Helper Functions Needed:**
- `create-pending-selection-state` - may need to add this helper
- `get-cards-moved-to-hand` - may need to count zone changes

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Testing mock behavior instead of production code
- ❌ Happy path only assertions
- ❌ Using `some?` as primary assertion
- ❌ Ignoring test if function doesn't exist (should note as blocked)
close_reason: Closed

title: Feature: Alternate Casting System with Flashback
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Cards can define alternate casting modes via `:card/alternate-costs`
- Alternate modes specify: zone restriction, mana cost, additional costs, on-resolve behavior
- Casting system finds all valid modes for a card based on current zone
- Mode selection UI appears when multiple valid modes exist
- Flashback spells exile when leaving stack (resolve, counter, or fizzle)
- Storm count increments on flashback casts (flashback is still casting)
- Deep Analysis card implemented with correct Oracle text

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Deep Analysis castable from hand for {3}{U}, draws 2 cards
- [ ] Deep Analysis castable from graveyard for {1}{U} + 3 life, draws 2 cards
- [ ] Flashback Deep Analysis exiles after resolution
- [ ] Flashback Deep Analysis exiles if countered
- [ ] Storm count increments when casting via flashback
- [ ] Mode selection UI appears for cards with multiple valid modes from same zone
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoding Deep Analysis logic in engine (must use generic alternate cost system)
- ❌ NO separate 'flashback zone' concept (flashback cards live in graveyard, casting checks zone)
- ❌ NO flashback spells going to graveyard on counter (must exile per MTG rules)
- ❌ NO breaking existing card definitions (alternate-costs is additive)
- ❌ NO skipping storm increment for flashback casts (flashback is casting a spell)

## Approach
Implement a generic Alternate Casting System that models flashback as one instance of alternate costs. Cards define `:card/alternate-costs` with zone restrictions, costs, and resolution behavior. The casting system is extended to find all valid modes (primary + alternates) and present choices when multiple modes are available. This approach enables future cards like Gush (return Islands), Foil (discard), Fireblast (sacrifice lands) without engine changes—only new cost types.

## Architecture
### Data Model
```clojure
{:card/alternate-costs 
 [{:alternate/id :flashback
   :alternate/zone :graveyard
   :alternate/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :blue 1}
   :alternate/additional-costs [{:cost/type :pay-life :cost/amount 3}]
   :alternate/on-resolve :exile}]}
```

### Key Components
- `src/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs` - Add `get-casting-modes`, `can-cast-mode?`
- `src/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs` - Already has `:pay-life`, add cost selection flow
- `src/fizzle/engine/stack.cljs` - Track `:stack/cast-mode`, use for resolution destination
- `src/fizzle/events/` - Cast event handles mode selection, storm increment
- `src/fizzle/views/` - Mode selection UI component
- `src/fizzle/cards/` - Deep Analysis definition

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Deep Analysis requires flashback (cast from graveyard for alternate cost). The engine currently only supports casting from hand. Need a system that handles flashback AND future alternate cost cards (Gush, Foil, Fireblast, Daze) without per-card special cases.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- `rules.cljs:28` - `can-cast?` hardcodes `(= :hand (:object/zone obj))`
- `costs.cljs:167-206` - `:pay-life` cost type already implemented
- `effects.cljs:251-282` - `:draw` effect type already implemented
- `iggy_pop.cljs` - Card definition patterns established

**External:**
- Scryfall Oracle: Deep Analysis = {3}{U} sorcery, target draws 2, flashback {1}{U} pay 3 life
- MTG Rules: Flashback exiles when leaving stack for ANY reason (resolve, counter, fizzle)
- MTG Rules: Flashback casts count toward storm

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Alternate Casting Abilities ✓

**What it is:** Generic system where cards define alternate casting modes with zone restrictions, costs, and resolution effects. Casting system finds all valid modes and presents choice when multiple exist.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed card definition patterns in iggy_pop.cljs - data-driven approach
- Verified :pay-life cost already exists in costs.cljs
- Checked stack resolution flow - can add cast-mode tracking

**Pros:**
- Enables Gush, Foil, Fireblast, Daze without engine changes
- Consistent with codebase's data-driven philosophy
- Flashback is just one alternate mode type

**Cons:**
- More upfront work than minimal flashback

**Chosen because:** User confirmed future decks need Gush, Foil, etc. Investment in generic system pays off.

#### 2. Zone-Aware Casting ❌

**What it is:** Minimal extension - just make can-cast? check graveyard for flashback keyword.

**Why we looked at this:** Simpler, faster for just Deep Analysis

**Investigation:**
- Would require special-casing flashback in can-cast?
- Wouldn't generalize to Gush (alternate cost from hand)

**Pros:**
- Less code initially

**Cons:**
- Doesn't handle non-flashback alternates
- Would need rewriting for Gush

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** User plans Premodern/Pauper support - Gush, Foil, Daze all need alternates from hand.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Scope permanently limited to flashback-only cards.

#### 3. Graveyard Action System ❌

**What it is:** Separate system for graveyard-castable cards.

**Why we looked at this:** Clean separation of concerns

**Investigation:**
- Would duplicate casting logic
- Storm interaction unclear

**Pros:**
- Clear separation

**Cons:**
- Code duplication
- Storm might not trigger correctly

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Duplicates casting logic, storm interaction risky.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Complete engine rewrite.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Alternate cost framework
- Deep Analysis card
- Flashback mode (zone=graveyard, on-resolve=exile)
- Mode selection UI
- Storm interaction

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Other cost types (:return-lands, :sacrifice-lands, :sacrifice-creature, :exile-from-gy)
- Recoup (grants flashback to other cards)
- Other flashback cards (Ray of Revelation, Cabal Therapy)

### Open Questions
- Exact UI styling for mode selector (decide during implementation)
- Whether to show mode picker for single-mode cards (decided: no, auto-cast)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| What is 'deep analysis'? | MTG card Deep Analysis | Implement flashback card |
| How handle flashback? | Full flashback mechanic | Need alternate casting system |
| Target player targeting? | Any player | Full rules accuracy |
| Other flashback cards? | Ray of Revelation, Recoup, Flash of Insight | System must be extensible |
| Storm interaction? | Yes, flashback counts | Ensure cast event increments storm |
| Which approach? | Approach 2 (Alternate Casting) | Generic system for Gush, Foil, etc. |
| Data model OK? | Yes | Proceed with :card/alternate-costs |
| Mode selection UX? | Yes, popover when multiple modes | Auto-cast when single mode |
| Build all cost types now? | No, framework + Deep Analysis | Incremental cost types later |
| Phase 1 scope? | Framework + Deep Analysis | Cost types in future epics |
| Edge cases (counter)? | Flashback still exiles | Handle in move-spell-off-stack |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Flashback Implementation Status
**Question explored:** Does flashback exist in codebase?
**Sources consulted:**
- rules.cljs - can-cast? implementation
- fizzle-design.md - flashback design notes
- cards/ - existing card definitions

**Findings:**
- Flashback mentioned in design doc but not implemented
- can-cast? hardcodes hand zone check
- :pay-life cost already exists
- :draw effect already exists

**Conclusion:** Need to extend casting rules, not start from scratch

#### Alternative Cost Cards in Premodern/Pauper
**Question explored:** What other cards need alternate costs?
**Sources consulted:**
- User input on planned decks
- MTG card database knowledge

**Findings:**
- Gush: return 2 Islands instead of {4}{U}
- Foil: discard Island + card instead of {2}{U}{U}
- Daze: return Island instead of {1}{U}
- Fireblast: sacrifice 2 Mountains instead of {4}{R}{R}
- Cabal Therapy: flashback by sacrificing creature

**Conclusion:** Alternate casting system must be generic, not flashback-specific

### Dead-End Paths

#### Zone-Aware Casting (Approach 1)
**Why explored:** Simpler initial implementation
**Investigation:**
- Would require special-casing flashback
- Wouldn't handle Gush (alternate from hand)

**Why abandoned:** User confirmed Gush/Foil needed - can't special-case

#### Graveyard Action System (Approach 3)
**Why explored:** Clean separation of concerns
**Investigation:**
- Would duplicate casting logic
- Storm interaction unclear

**Why abandoned:** Duplication risk, storm might break

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'Build all cost types now?' → No, framework + Deep Analysis only. Cost types added when cards need them.
- 'Countered flashback spells?' → Still exile per MTG rules. Implemented in move-spell-off-stack.
- 'Remand-style effects?' → Deferred, not in Premodern. Can add full replacement effect later if needed.
close_reason: Closed

design:

title: Epic: Best-in-Class Developer Experience
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

- Single command REPL: `make repl` starts node REPL ready to eval ClojureScript
- Single command validation: `make validate` runs lint + format-check + tests, fails fast
- All dev commands documented in CLAUDE.md for agent use
- Makefile is primary interface (familiar to Clojure devs)
- clj-kondo for static analysis/linting
- cljstyle for code formatting (check and fix modes)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] `make repl` starts REPL in one command, ready to eval immediately
- [ ] `make test` runs all tests and reports pass/fail
- [ ] `make lint` runs clj-kondo on src/, reports issues
- [ ] `make fmt-check` verifies formatting without changes
- [ ] `make fmt` auto-fixes formatting
- [ ] `make validate` runs lint + fmt-check + test sequentially, fails on first error
- [ ] `make dev` starts browser watch mode
- [ ] `make clean` removes build artifacts
- [ ] CLAUDE.md updated with Development Commands section
- [ ] All existing tests still pass

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO multi-step REPL setup (simplicity: one command must work)
- ❌ NO npm scripts as primary interface (consistency: Makefile is standard for Clojure projects)
- ❌ NO manual PATH exports in commands (usability: Makefile handles Java path internally)
- ❌ NO hard-coded absolute paths (portability: must work on any machine)

## Approach

Create a Makefile that wraps shadow-cljs commands with proper Java PATH handling. Add clj-kondo via npm for linting and cljstyle as a standalone tool for formatting. Update CLAUDE.md with all commands so the agent uses them consistently.

## Architecture

```
Makefile              # Primary interface
├── repl              # npx shadow-cljs node-repl
├── dev               # npx shadow-cljs watch app  
├── test              # npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js
├── lint              # clj-kondo --lint src/
├── fmt-check         # cljstyle check src/
├── fmt               # cljstyle fix src/
├── validate          # lint && fmt-check && test
└── clean             # rm -rf out/ .shadow-cljs/

CLAUDE.md             # Agent instructions
└── Development Commands section
```

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Current DX requires multiple steps for REPL (watch + connect), complex test commands with PATH exports, and no linting/formatting. This slows development and causes friction.

### Research Findings

**External:**
- shadow-cljs node-repl provides one-command REPL for non-browser dev
- clj-kondo is the standard ClojureScript linter (2M+ weekly downloads)
- cljstyle is the recommended formatter (successor to cljfmt)
- Makefile is idiomatic for Clojure projects

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Makefile with node-repl ✓

**What it is:** Use Makefile as primary interface, shadow-cljs node-repl for instant REPL.

**Chosen because:** Familiar to Clojure devs, one-command REPL, handles PATH internally.

#### 2. npm scripts ❌

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Not familiar to Clojure devs, user explicitly requested Makefile.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** User changes preference.

#### 3. deps.edn aliases ❌

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** shadow-cljs standalone is recommended over JVM invocation per official docs.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Need tight integration with other Clojure tooling.

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- Makefile with all dev commands
- clj-kondo linting
- cljstyle formatting
- CLAUDE.md updates

**Out of scope:**
- Editor integration (Calva/CIDER) - user can configure separately
- CI/CD setup - future epic
- Pre-commit hooks - future epic

## Design Discovery

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| CLI interface? | Makefile (familiar to Clojure devs) | Primary interface is make commands |
| REPL experience? | One command, auto-connects | Use node-repl, no browser needed |
| Validation scope? | All: format + lint + test | make validate runs everything |
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 2: remove-object unit tests + Dark Ritual and Cabal Ritual card tests
design:
## Goal
Add unit tests for remove-object in zones_test.cljs, then create integration tests for Dark Ritual and Cabal Ritual (the two mana ritual spells). These are the simplest untested cards - pure effect spells with no selection UI.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### Part A: remove-object unit tests

**File:** src/test/fizzle/engine/zones_test.cljs (EXTEND existing file)

**Function under test:** `zones/remove-object` at src/main/fizzle/engine/zones.cljs:78-90
```clojure
(defn remove-object [db object-id]
  (if-let [obj-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?oid :where [?e :object/id ?oid]] db object-id)]
    (d/db-with db [[:db.fn/retractEntity obj-eid]])
    db))
```

**Tests to add (append after line 107 in zones_test.cljs):**

1. `remove-object-removes-from-db-test`
   - Setup: init-game-state, get hand, get first object-id
   - Act: `(zones/remove-object db object-id)`
   - Assert: `(q/get-object db' object-id)` returns nil
   - Assert: `(q/get-hand db' :player-1)` is empty (object was the only hand card)
   - Bug caught: Verifies retractEntity actually removes the entity

2. `remove-object-nonexistent-returns-unchanged-test`
   - Setup: init-game-state
   - Act: `(zones/remove-object db (random-uuid))`
   - Assert: `(= db db')` - exact same db returned
   - Bug caught: Verifies graceful handling of missing objects (no crash)

3. `remove-object-nil-id-returns-unchanged-test`
   - Setup: init-game-state
   - Act: `(zones/remove-object db nil)`
   - Assert: `(= db db')` - exact same db returned
   - Bug caught: Verifies nil safety (Datascript query with nil param)

4. `remove-object-double-remove-is-noop-test`
   - Setup: init-game-state, get first object-id, remove once
   - Act: remove again with same object-id
   - Assert: `(= db-after-first db-after-second)` - second remove is no-op
   - Bug caught: Verifies idempotency (important for spell copies that might be resolved twice)

5. `remove-object-other-objects-unaffected-test`
   - Setup: init-game-state, add a second card to hand via d/conn-from-db + transact, get both object-ids
   - Act: remove first object only
   - Assert: second object still exists with correct zone/card data
   - Bug caught: Verifies retractEntity doesn't cascade to unrelated entities

**No new requires needed** - zones_test.cljs already imports `datascript.core`, `fizzle.db.init`, `fizzle.db.queries`, `fizzle.engine.zones`.

### Part B: Dark Ritual integration test

**File:** src/test/fizzle/cards/dark_ritual_test.cljs (NEW)

**Card definition** (src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs:19-28):
```clojure
{:card/id :dark-ritual
 :card/name "Dark Ritual"
 :card/cmc 1
 :card/mana-cost {:black 1}
 :card/colors #{:black}
 :card/types #{:instant}
 :card/effects [{:effect/type :add-mana :effect/mana {:black 3}}]}
```

**Test structure:**
```clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.dark-ritual-test
  (:require
    [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.cards.iggy-pop :as cards]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
    [fizzle.db.schema :refer [schema]]
    [fizzle.engine.mana :as mana]
    [fizzle.engine.rules :as rules]))
```

**Helpers:** Use same create-test-db + add-card-to-zone pattern from led_test.cljs.

**Tests:**

1. `dark-ritual-card-definition-test`
   - Assert card exists in all-cards with correct :card/id, :card/name, :card/cmc, :card/mana-cost, :card/types, :card/colors
   - Assert single effect of type :add-mana with {:black 3}
   - Bug caught: Verifies card data integrity (catches typos in card defs)

2. `dark-ritual-cast-adds-bbb-mana-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add dark-ritual to hand, add {:black 1} mana to pool
   - Act: rules/cast-spell, then rules/resolve-spell
   - Assert: mana pool contains {:black 3} after resolution (paid 1B, got 3B = net +2B, but pool starts empty after payment, so 3B from effect)
   - Assert: card moved to graveyard after resolution (instant)
   - Assert: storm count incremented to 1
   - Bug caught: Verifies the full cast-resolve cycle produces correct mana

3. `dark-ritual-cannot-cast-without-mana-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add dark-ritual to hand, NO mana
   - Assert: `(rules/can-cast? db :player-1 obj-id)` returns false
   - Bug caught: Verifies mana cost enforcement

4. `dark-ritual-increments-storm-count-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add 2 dark rituals to hand, add {:black 1} mana
   - Act: cast and resolve first ritual (produces BBB), cast second ritual (costs B)
   - Assert: storm count is 2 after both casts
   - Assert: mana pool after second resolve = {:black 5} (3 from first - 1 for second + 3 from second = 5)
   - Bug caught: Verifies storm count accumulates correctly across multiple casts

### Part C: Cabal Ritual integration test

**File:** src/test/fizzle/cards/cabal_ritual_test.cljs (NEW)

**Card definition** (src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs:50-60):
```clojure
{:card/id :cabal-ritual
 :card/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :black 1}
 :card/conditional-effects {:threshold [{:effect/type :add-mana :effect/mana {:black 5}}]}
 :card/effects [{:effect/type :add-mana :effect/mana {:black 3}}]}
```

**Test structure:** Same pattern as dark-ritual-test.

**Tests:**

1. `cabal-ritual-card-definition-test`
   - Assert card def has :card/id, :card/name, :card/cmc 2, :card/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :black 1}
   - Assert has both :card/effects and :card/conditional-effects with :threshold key
   - Bug caught: Verifies conditional-effects structure is correct

2. `cabal-ritual-produces-bbb-without-threshold-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add cabal-ritual to hand, add {:black 2} mana (1B + 1 generic)
   - Actually need to check how generic mana works... mana-cost is {:colorless 1 :black 1}
   - Add {:black 2} to pool (1B pays for B, 1B pays for {1})
   - Act: cast-spell, resolve-spell
   - Assert: mana pool after = {:black 3} (paid 2B, gained 3B)
   - Assert: graveyard count = 1 (moved there, but threshold not active since <7 cards)
   - Bug caught: Verifies base mana effect works without threshold

3. `cabal-ritual-produces-bbbbb-with-threshold-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add cabal-ritual to hand, add {:black 2} mana
   - Add 7 cards to graveyard to enable threshold (use d/conn-from-db + transact objects with :object/zone :graveyard)
   - Act: cast-spell, resolve-spell
   - Assert: mana pool after = {:black 5} (paid 2B, gained 5B instead of 3B)
   - Bug caught: Verifies threshold condition detection and conditional effect selection

4. `cabal-ritual-threshold-exactly-7-cards-test`
   - Setup: same as above but with exactly 7 graveyard cards (boundary)
   - Act: cast-spell, resolve-spell
   - Assert: threshold effect fires (5B produced, not 3B)
   - Bug caught: Verifies threshold boundary condition (>= 7, not > 7)

5. `cabal-ritual-threshold-6-cards-no-threshold-test`
   - Setup: same but with only 6 graveyard cards
   - Act: cast-spell, resolve-spell
   - Assert: normal effect fires (3B produced, not 5B)
   - Bug caught: Verifies threshold is NOT active at 6 cards (off-by-one)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] 5 remove-object tests added to zones_test.cljs covering: remove existing, nonexistent, nil, double-remove, unaffected others
- [ ] dark_ritual_test.cljs exists with 4 tests: card def, cast-resolve mana, cannot-cast, storm count
- [ ] cabal_ritual_test.cljs exists with 5 tests: card def, no-threshold, threshold, boundary-7, boundary-6
- [ ] Dark Ritual test verifies mana pool contains {:black 3} after cast-resolve cycle
- [ ] Cabal Ritual threshold test verifies {:black 5} with 7+ graveyard cards vs {:black 3} without
- [ ] Storm count increments correctly verified in Dark Ritual chained-cast test
- [ ] All existing 792+ tests still pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: remove-object with nil**
- Datascript query `[:find ?e . :in $ ?oid :where [?e :object/id ?oid]]` with nil ?oid
- Should return nil (no match), so if-let falls through to return db unchanged
- MUST test this explicitly - nil is a common runtime value for missing objects

**Edge Case: Mana payment for generic costs**
- Cabal Ritual costs {:colorless 1 :black 1}
- The :colorless 1 is a generic cost payable with any color
- Must verify how mana/pay-mana handles this - add enough black mana to cover both
- Test setup: add {:black 2} to pool (1B for the B cost, 1B for the {1} generic)

**Edge Case: Threshold boundary**
- Threshold checks >= 7 cards in graveyard
- The Cabal Ritual itself goes to graveyard AFTER resolution (it's on the stack during resolution)
- So the 7 cards must be in graveyard BEFORE casting
- threshold? is checked at resolution time via get-active-effects (rules.cljs:408)
- Important: the spell resolves while still on stack, threshold checked against graveyard WITHOUT the resolving spell

**Edge Case: Storm count starts at 0**
- init-game-state sets :player/storm-count to 0
- First cast increments to 1, so storm trigger gets copy-count = dec(1) = 0 copies
- This is correct: first spell of the game doesn't get storm copies
- Verify storm count = 1 after first Dark Ritual cast (not 0)

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow led_test.cljs pattern for test DB setup and helpers
- Follow rules_test.cljs pattern for cast-spell/resolve-spell assertions
- See conditions.cljs:10-20 for threshold? implementation

## Anti-patterns
- NO mocking Datascript (pure function tests against real DB)
- NO test-only methods in production code
- NO testing private functions directly (threshold? is public but test through resolve-spell)
- NO using (is (some? result)) as sole assertion - always assert specific values
- NO duplicating the exact same helper functions as led_test.cljs (copy the pattern, not the code verbatim - each test file has its own helpers)
close_reason: All 14 tests added: 5 remove-object unit tests in zones_test.cljs, 4 Dark Ritual integration tests, 5 Cabal Ritual integration tests (including threshold boundary). make validate passes: 806 tests, 1720 assertions.

title: Epic: Composable Fork/Replay UI Components
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

1. Turn-grouped action log component: entries grouped by turn number with collapsible sections
2. Click-to-rewind: click any entry in action log to jump directly to that position
3. Fork CRUD controls component: create named fork, inline rename, cascade delete
4. Fork tree component: indented list showing parent-child fork relationships with branch switching
5. All components are self-contained, subscription-driven, and layout-agnostic (no assumptions about sidebar/panel/modal placement)
6. Cascade delete: deleting a fork removes it and all descendant forks; deleting active branch switches to main

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] action-log component renders entries grouped by turn with collapsible sections
- [ ] Clicking any entry in action-log jumps to that position (restores game state)
- [ ] Current position highlighted, future entries visually distinct
- [ ] fork-controls component supports create, inline rename, and delete
- [ ] Cascade delete removes fork and all descendants
- [ ] Cannot delete main branch
- [ ] fork-tree component shows indented parent-child hierarchy
- [ ] Clicking branch in fork-tree switches to it
- [ ] Active branch visually highlighted in fork-tree
- [ ] All components work independently of layout context
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO layout assumptions in components (no hardcoded widths, sidebar positioning, or flex assumptions — components must be layout-agnostic)
- ❌ NO game logic in view components (views dispatch events and subscribe to data, nothing else)
- ❌ NO component-local atoms for state that belongs in app-db (use re-frame subscriptions)
- ❌ NO direct history/core.cljs calls from views (always go through events/subscriptions)
- ❌ NO comparison view (deferred — premature before full UI design)

## Approach

Build composable, high-quality UI components for fork/replay interaction that can be assembled into any final layout. Components are self-contained Reagent components backed by re-frame subscriptions and events. No assumptions about where they'll be rendered.

Four layers:
1. **Data model** (history/core.cljs) — Pure functions: rename-fork, delete-fork, entries-by-turn, fork-tree
2. **Events** (history/events.cljs) — jump-to, create-fork, rename-fork, delete-fork
3. **Subscriptions** (subs/history.cljs) — entries-by-turn (grouped), fork-tree (hierarchical)
4. **View components** (views/history.cljs) — action-log, fork-controls, fork-tree

## Architecture

### Data Model Additions (history/core.cljs)
- rename-fork [db fork-id new-name] — Updates fork name in :history/forks map
- delete-fork [db fork-id] — Cascade removes fork + descendants. If active branch deleted, switches to main and resets position
- entries-by-turn [entries] — Groups effective-entries by :entry/turn, returns [{:turn N :entries [...]}]
- fork-tree [forks] — Structures flat fork map into tree: [{:fork {...} :children [...]}] rooted at forks with nil parent

### Events (history/events.cljs)
- ::jump-to [_ [_ position]] — Calls step-to to restore snapshot at position
- ::create-fork [_ [_ name]] — Calls create-named-fork at current position
- ::rename-fork [_ [_ fork-id new-name]] — Calls rename-fork
- ::delete-fork [_ [_ fork-id]] — Calls delete-fork with cascade

### Subscriptions (subs/history.cljs)
- ::entries-by-turn — Layer 3, derives from ::entries, returns turn-grouped structure
- ::fork-tree — Layer 3, derives from ::forks, returns hierarchical tree

### View Components (views/history.cljs)
- action-log: Turn headers (collapsible), entry rows (clickable), position highlighting
- fork-controls: New Fork button, inline rename (click-to-edit), delete button per fork
- fork-tree: Indented nested list, active branch highlighted, click to switch

## Design Rationale

### Problem
The minimal fork/replay UI (sidebar with step back/forward, flat fork list) is a POC. The full app UI hasn't been designed yet, so we need high-quality, composable interaction components that can be assembled into any layout later.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- history/core.cljs — Data model with fork/entry structures, step-to exists but no jump-to event
- history/events.cljs — Only step-back, step-forward, switch-branch events exist
- create-named-fork exists in core but not exposed as event
- views/history.cljs — POC sidebar with flat entry list and flat fork list
- subs/history.cljs — Basic subscriptions (entries, position, forks) but no grouped/tree derivations

**Key patterns:**
- Components use re-frame subscriptions for data, dispatch for actions
- History entries have :entry/turn for grouping
- Forks have :fork/parent for tree structure

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Composable layout-agnostic components ✓
**What it is:** Build self-contained components backed by subscriptions/events that work in any container.
**Chosen because:** Full UI not designed yet — components must be flexible for future composition.

#### 2. Complete sidebar redesign ❌
**What it is:** Rebuild the sidebar as a finished product with all features integrated.
**Why explored:** Natural extension of existing sidebar POC.
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Full UI not designed yet. Hardcoding layout assumptions would constrain future design.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Full app UI layout is finalized.

#### 3. Side-by-side comparison view ❌
**What it is:** Component rendering two game states side-by-side for branch comparison.
**Why explored:** Listed in original stub epic requirements.
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Premature — layout assumptions needed. Branch switching is sufficient for now.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Full UI design specifically calls for comparison layout.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** action-log, fork-controls, fork-tree components + backing data/events/subs
**Out of scope:** Comparison view, final layout/positioning, timeline scrubber, fork tree graph/SVG visualization

### Open Questions
- Exact styling of components (colors, spacing) — resolve during implementation, keep minimal
- Whether to update POC sidebar to use new components or leave it — decide after components built

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Build final UI or composable components? | Composable components | No layout assumptions, layout-agnostic requirement |
| Comparison view? | Skip for now | Not in scope, anti-pattern to build it |
| Fork tree style? | Indented list | Pure CSS nesting, no SVG complexity |
| Delete behavior? | Cascade delete | Simpler model, delete fork + all children |
| Which components? | All four: action-log (turn-grouped + click-to-rewind), fork-controls, fork-tree | Full component set |

### Dead-End Paths

#### Complete Sidebar Redesign
**Why explored:** Natural next step from POC sidebar
**Why abandoned:** Full UI not designed — would bake in layout assumptions

#### SVG Graph Visualization
**Why explored:** More visual representation of fork relationships
**Why abandoned:** Harder to compose into arbitrary layouts, indented list sufficient

### Open Concerns Raised
- Full UI layout unknown → Components must be layout-agnostic (captured as anti-pattern)

title: Epic: Unify spell/trigger stack systems via stack-item entity
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
1. All stack entries (spells, triggers, activated abilities, storm copies) represented as `:stack-item/*` Datascript entities
2. Single `resolve-effect-target` function replaces all 10 target resolution implementations
3. Unified resolution path for all stack items (one code path, not 4 branches)
4. Selection builders parameterized by stack-item context (not spell-id vs trigger-id)
5. Game objects track physical card location (`:stack` zone per MTG rules), stack-items track resolution metadata (ordering, effects, targets)
6. Fizzle check applies to all stack items with stored targets
7. `:trigger/*` schema attributes removed entirely after migration
8. Mana abilities and land ETB effects use shared `resolve-effect-target`
9. Storm copies become stack-items with `:stack-item/is-copy true` and inline spell effects (no separate storm trigger entity)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] All existing tests pass (826+ tests, 1826+ assertions)
- [ ] Single Datalog query retrieves entire stack in LIFO order
- [ ] No `:trigger/*` entities remain in Datascript schema
- [ ] No duplicate selection builder functions (0 `-for-trigger` suffixed functions)
- [ ] Target resolution (`:self`, `:controller`, `:any-player`, `:target-ref`) exists in exactly one function
- [ ] Effect before/after splitting exists in exactly one function
- [ ] `make validate` passes (lint + format + tests)
- [ ] Net reduction of 300+ LOC

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO creating game objects for triggers (triggers are virtual — only stack-items, no game object; reason: triggers aren't physical cards)
- ❌ NO keeping parallel resolution paths (reason: the entire point is unification — one path, not 2+)
- ❌ NO `:selection/spell-id` or `:selection/trigger-id` in selection state (reason: use `:selection/stack-item-id` — the dual-ID pattern IS the duplication)
- ❌ NO `:object/position` for stack ordering (reason: `:stack-item/position` is the sole source of truth for LIFO ordering)
- ❌ NO dual-entity stack queries that check both `:trigger/*` and `:object/*` (reason: single entity type = single query)
- ❌ NO multimethod for target resolution (reason: closed set of ~5 strategies, cond is simpler and matches codebase style)
- ❌ NO storm trigger entity that later spawns copies (reason: storm creates N stack-items directly with spell effects)

## Approach
Introduce a new `:stack-item/*` Datascript entity type as the unified stack representation. When a spell is cast, a stack-item is created referencing the game object via `:stack-item/object-ref`. When a trigger fires, a stack-item is created with inline effects (no game object). Resolution code only operates on stack-items.

A single `resolve-effect-target` function (cond-based) replaces 10 copy-pasted target resolution implementations across selection.cljs, triggers.cljs, abilities.cljs, and game.cljs. Selection builders are parameterized by stack-item context, eliminating the 3 duplicated builder pairs. Effect before/after splitting is consolidated into one function.

New `engine/stack.cljs` namespace houses all stack-item operations: creation, removal, querying, resolution, and target resolution.

## Architecture

### New namespace: engine/stack.cljs
- `create-stack-item` — create stack-item entity in Datascript
- `remove-stack-item` — remove after resolution
- `get-top-stack-item` — query highest position
- `get-next-stack-order` — moved from queries.cljs
- `resolve-effect-target` — single target resolver
- `resolve-stack-item` — unified resolution (no selection)
- `resolve-stack-item-with-selection` — unified resolution checking for selection pause
- `execute-resolved-effects` — run effects with target resolution

### Schema additions (db/schema.cljs)
`:stack-item/position`, `:stack-item/type`, `:stack-item/controller`, `:stack-item/source`, `:stack-item/effects`, `:stack-item/targets`, `:stack-item/description`, `:stack-item/is-copy`, `:stack-item/cast-mode`, `:stack-item/object-ref`

### Schema removals
All `:trigger/*` attributes after migration complete.

### Data flow
1. Cast spell → `zones/move-to-zone obj :stack` + `stack/create-stack-item {:type :spell :object-ref obj-eid ...}`
2. Fire trigger → `stack/create-stack-item {:type :etb :effects [...] ...}`
3. Resolve → `stack/get-top-stack-item` → `stack/resolve-stack-item-with-selection`
4. Cleanup → remove stack-item; for spells move object to destination zone

### Files modified
- `db/schema.cljs` — add stack-item attributes, eventually remove trigger attributes
- `db/queries.cljs` — simplify stack queries
- `engine/stack.cljs` — NEW: all stack-item operations
- `engine/rules.cljs` — cast-spell creates stack-item, resolve-spell delegates to stack
- `engine/triggers.cljs` — create stack-items instead of trigger entities
- `events/game.cljs` — `::resolve-top` simplified to one path
- `events/selection.cljs` — unified builders, remove `-for-trigger` functions
- `events/abilities.cljs` — mana abilities use `resolve-effect-target`

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Spells and triggers are conceptually the same thing (resolvable stack items) but implemented as two parallel systems, creating ~1000+ lines of duplicated logic. Fork/replay (Phase 3) needs a uniform stack model. The current dual-entity model (`:object/position` + `:trigger/stack-order`) requires comparing two entity types for LIFO resolution.

### Research Findings

**Codebase:**
- `db/queries.cljs:113-127` — `get-next-stack-order` already shares counter between both entity types
- `events/selection.cljs:512-639` — `resolve-spell-with-selection` (5 code paths, ~130 lines)
- `events/selection.cljs:683-845` — `resolve-activated-ability-with-selection` (3 code paths, ~160 lines)
- `events/game.cljs:364-410` — `::resolve-top` handler with 4 branches comparing both entity types
- 10 distinct target resolution implementations across 4 files
- 3 duplicated selection builder pairs (tutor, player-target, discard)
- 5 effect before/after splitting implementations

**MTG Rules:**
- Stack is a real zone (Rule 601.2a) — cards move hand → stack → graveyard
- Fizzle applies to all stack items with illegal targets (not just spells)
- Storm creates copies of the spell on the stack

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Stack-item wrapper entity ✓

**What it is:** New `:stack-item/*` Datascript entity type as resolution metadata. Game objects still track physical card location. Stack-items are the sole source of stack ordering and resolution context.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed current dual-entity model — both types already share a counter via `get-next-stack-order`
- Checked that `:object/zone :stack` is needed for MTG zone rules (cards physically on stack)
- Verified storm copies need `:object/is-copy` behavior (removed from DB, not sent to graveyard)

**Pros:**
- Clean separation: physical card vs resolution metadata
- Single entity type for all stack queries
- Game object zone tracking unchanged (MTG-correct)

**Cons:**
- Adds indirection (stack-item references game object)
- Schema additions before removals during migration

**Chosen because:** Cleanest unified model without redundant naming on game objects.

#### 2. Triggers become game objects ❌

**What it is:** Give triggers `:object/*` attributes and put them in `:stack` zone like spells.

**Why we looked at this:** Simplest migration — triggers just become objects.

**Investigation:**
- Would require `:object/stack-source`, `:object/stack-controller`, `:object/stack-data`, `:object/stack-description` — duplicative of existing object attributes
- Triggers aren't physical cards — they shouldn't have card-like attributes

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Adding `:object/stack-*` attributes is redundant with existing object schema. Muddies the distinction between physical cards and virtual stack entries.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We decide triggers should be physical objects (unlikely per MTG rules).

#### 3. Normalize at resolution time only ❌

**What it is:** Keep spells and triggers stored separately, create a unified view/map only during resolution. Storage unchanged, resolution code unified.

**Why we looked at this:** Least invasive — no schema changes.

**Investigation:**
- Would still require dual-entity queries for stack ordering
- Fork/replay would still need to capture two entity types
- Doesn't address the root cause (dual representation)

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Doesn't unify storage. Dual-entity queries remain. Fork/replay complexity unchanged.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Storage unification proves too risky (only if migration can't be done safely).

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- All stack item types: spells, storm copies, activated abilities, ETB triggers, permanent-tapped triggers, land-entered triggers
- Target resolution unification across ALL callers (including mana abilities and land ETB)
- Selection builder unification
- Effect before/after splitting unification
- Schema migration (add stack-item, remove trigger)

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Fork/replay implementation (uses the unified model but is a separate epic)
- Moving pending-selection into Datascript (fizzle-4jc, separate epic)
- New trigger types not yet implemented
- UI changes beyond what's needed for stack display

### Open Questions
- Exact migration ordering: can we add stack-items and run both systems in parallel during transition, or must it be atomic?
- Should `engine/triggers.cljs` be renamed/reorganized after the trigger entity type is removed?
- Should `:object/position` be removed from schema or left for potential other uses?

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| How to unify stack items? | Triggers become objects → revised to stack-item wrapper | New `:stack-item/*` entity type, not bolting onto `:object/*` |
| Target resolution: single function or multimethod? | Single cond function | ~15 lines, matches codebase style, closed set of strategies |
| Include mana/ETB in scope? | Yes, include all | `resolve-effect-target` called from abilities.cljs and game.cljs land ETB too |
| Fizzle scope? | All items with stored targets | Not just spells — any stack item with targets gets fizzle-checked |
| Storm copies? | Become stack-items with is-copy | No separate storm trigger entity → N stack-items created directly |
| Does card move to stack zone? | Yes, per MTG rules (601.2a) | Game objects in `:stack` zone for physical location, stack-items for metadata |
| Wrapper vs objects for triggers? | Wrapper (stack-item entity type) | Avoids redundant `:object/stack-*` attributes, clean separation |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Current Stack Representation
**Question explored:** How are spells vs triggers stored and ordered?
**Sources consulted:**
- `db/schema.cljs:35-45` — object schema with `:object/position`
- `db/schema.cljs:68-77` — trigger schema with `:trigger/stack-order`
- `db/queries.cljs:113-127` — shared counter in `get-next-stack-order`

**Findings:**
- Both use shared counter space (max of both types + 1)
- Spells: game objects in `:stack` zone
- Triggers: separate entities with `:trigger/*` attributes
- LIFO comparison at resolution compares both types

**Conclusion:** Shared counter proves unification is natural. Stack-item entity replaces both ordering mechanisms.

#### Target Resolution Duplication
**Question explored:** How many times is :self/:controller resolution reimplemented?
**Sources consulted:**
- 10 distinct locations across selection.cljs, triggers.cljs, abilities.cljs, game.cljs

**Findings:**
- 4 variants: controller-only, self+controller, self+controller+target-ref, any-player
- Every variant is a cond doing the same assoc pattern
- Varying completeness (some handle :self, some don't)

**Conclusion:** Single function with all branches handles every case. ~15 lines replaces ~60 lines of duplication.

#### Resolution Path Duplication
**Question explored:** How similar are spell vs trigger resolution?
**Sources consulted:**
- `selection.cljs:512-639` — resolve-spell-with-selection
- `selection.cljs:683-845` — resolve-activated-ability-with-selection

**Findings:**
- Both: get effects → find selection → split before/after → execute before → build selection → pause
- Spell version: 5 code paths, ~130 lines
- Trigger version: 3 code paths, ~160 lines
- Key difference: trigger keeps stack-item until confirmation, spell removes on resolve

**Conclusion:** Unified with stack-item: both remove stack-item at same point (after effects + cleanup).

### Dead-End Paths

#### Triggers as Game Objects
**Why explored:** Simplest migration — just add :object/stack-* attributes to triggers
**Investigation:** Would add 4+ new :object/ attributes that duplicate existing concepts
**Why abandoned:** User correctly identified this as redundant naming. Triggers aren't physical cards.

#### Multimethod for Target Resolution
**Why explored:** Open dispatch pattern for extensibility
**Investigation:** Counted target types (~5), checked if new types expected (no — MTG targets are closed set via :target-ref)
**Why abandoned:** Too much ceremony for 5 branches. Cond matches codebase style.

### Open Concerns Raised
- 'How will targeting extend for creatures?' → :target-ref mechanism handles all future targeting. Cast-time selection stores chosen target, resolution-time :target-ref resolves it.
- 'Does the card move to the stack zone?' → Yes, per MTG Rule 601.2a. Stack is a real zone. Game objects track physical location, stack-items track resolution metadata.
close_reason: All 4 tasks complete. Stack unified via stack-items. 831 tests pass, make validate clean.

title: Task 4: Implement Careful Study with selection modal
design:
## Goal
Add Careful Study spell that draws 2 cards, then requires player to select and discard 2 cards via a modal UI.

## Effort Estimate
3-5 hours (includes selection modal system)

## Context
Completed Task 3 (LED with discard-hand cost). Careful Study requires:
1. A :discard effect with :selection :player that pauses for player input
2. A modal UI component for selecting cards from hand
3. State management for pending selections

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing effect patterns
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs

Look for:
- How execute-effect multimethod dispatches on :effect/type
- How :draw effect adds cards to hand
- How effects return modified db
- Pattern for effects that need player input (may not exist yet)

### 2. Add pending-selection to app-db
Note: This is app-level state (UI), NOT Datascript game state.
Selection state lives in re-frame app-db, not game/db.

File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs (or new selection.cljs)

Selection state structure in app-db:
```clojure
:game/pending-selection
{:selection/zone :hand           ;; Zone to select from
 :selection/count 2              ;; Exact number to select
 :selection/player-id :player-1  ;; Who is selecting
 :selection/selected #{}         ;; Set of object-ids currently selected
 :selection/effect {:effect/type :discard ...}} ;; Effect to resume after selection
```

### 3. Write tests first (TDD)
Add tests to: src/test/fizzle/cards/careful_study_test.cljs (new file)

Test cases with bug-catching rationale:

```clojure
;; test_careful_study_is_sorcery_type
;; Verify card definition has correct type
;; Bug caught: Wrong card type would affect timing rules

;; test_careful_study_draws_two_cards
;; Setup: Player has 3 cards in hand, cast Careful Study
;; Verify: Hand size increased by 2 (minus the spell = net +1)
;; Bug caught: Missing or broken :draw effect

;; test_careful_study_creates_pending_selection
;; Setup: Player casts Careful Study from hand
;; Action: Resolve spell
;; Verify: :game/pending-selection exists with :count 2, :zone :hand
;; Bug caught: Missing selection state creation

;; test_toggle_selection_adds_and_removes_card
;; Setup: Pending selection exists
;; Action: Toggle card A (add), toggle card A again (remove)
;; Verify: Selected set correctly reflects toggles
;; Bug caught: Broken toggle logic

;; test_confirm_selection_requires_exact_count
;; Setup: Pending selection with count=2, only 1 selected
;; Action: Attempt confirm
;; Verify: Confirm fails or is disabled, selection persists
;; Bug caught: Allowing wrong count to be confirmed

;; test_confirm_selection_discards_selected_cards
;; Setup: Pending selection with 2 cards selected
;; Action: Confirm selection
;; Verify: Those 2 cards move to graveyard, selection cleared
;; Bug caught: Cards not actually discarded

;; test_careful_study_with_exactly_two_cards_after_draw
;; Setup: Player has 0 cards, draws 2 from Careful Study
;; Verify: Must discard both cards (selection required even when obvious)
;; Bug caught: Auto-selecting when hand equals count

;; test_careful_study_with_more_than_two_cards_after_draw
;; Setup: Player has 3 cards, draws 2 (now 5 in hand)
;; Verify: Must select exactly 2 of the 5
;; Bug caught: Not offering full hand for selection
```

### 4. Add :discard effect type with selection support
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs

```clojure
(defmethod execute-effect :discard [db player-id effect]
  (let [selection (:effect/selection effect)
        count-to-discard (:effect/count effect)]
    (if (= :player selection)
      ;; Player selection required - return db unchanged
      ;; The selection state is handled at app-db level, not game-db
      ;; Spell resolution pauses until selection confirmed
      db
      ;; Programmatic discard (random or specified) - not needed for Careful Study
      db)))
```

Note: The actual selection handling happens in re-frame events, not in execute-effect.
The effect returns db unchanged; the calling code checks for :selection :player
and sets up the pending selection.

### 5. Modify spell resolution to handle selection effects
File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

Modify resolve-spell or add wrapper:
```clojure
(defn resolve-spell-with-selection [db player-id object-id]
  (let [spell-obj (queries/get-object db object-id)
        card (:object/card spell-obj)
        effects (:card/effects card)]
    ;; Execute effects until we hit a selection effect
    (loop [db' db
           remaining-effects effects]
      (if (empty? remaining-effects)
        ;; All effects done, move spell to graveyard
        (zones/move-to-zone db' object-id :graveyard)
        (let [effect (first remaining-effects)]
          (if (= :player (:effect/selection effect))
            ;; Pause for selection - return db with spell still on stack
            ;; Pending selection will be set up at app-db level
            {:db db'
             :pending {:selection/zone :hand
                       :selection/count (:effect/count effect)
                       :selection/player-id player-id
                       :selection/remaining-effects (rest remaining-effects)
                       :selection/spell-id object-id}}
            ;; Execute effect and continue
            (recur (effects/execute-effect db' player-id effect)
                   (rest remaining-effects))))))))
```

### 6. Add selection state management events
File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::toggle-selection
  (fn [db [_ object-id]]
    (let [selected (get-in db [:game/pending-selection :selection/selected] #{})
          new-selected (if (contains? selected object-id)
                         (disj selected object-id)
                         (conj selected object-id))]
      (assoc-in db [:game/pending-selection :selection/selected] new-selected))))

(rf/reg-event-db
  ::confirm-selection
  (fn [db _]
    (let [selection (:game/pending-selection db)
          selected (:selection/selected selection)
          count-required (:selection/count selection)]
      (if (= (count selected) count-required)
        ;; Valid selection - discard selected cards and clear selection
        (let [game-db (:game/db db)
              db-after-discard (reduce (fn [gdb obj-id]
                                         (zones/move-to-zone gdb obj-id :graveyard))
                                       game-db
                                       selected)]
          (-> db
              (assoc :game/db db-after-discard)
              (dissoc :game/pending-selection)))
        ;; Invalid count - do nothing
        db))))

(rf/reg-event-db
  ::cancel-selection
  (fn [db _]
    ;; Note: Cancel behavior for Careful Study is complex
    ;; Cards already drawn can't be "undrawn"
    ;; For MVP: Cancel clears selection but keeps drawn cards
    ;; Player must still discard 2 (selection re-opens)
    (assoc-in db [:game/pending-selection :selection/selected] #{})))
```

### 7. Add selection modal component
File: src/main/fizzle/core.cljs

```clojure
(defn selection-modal []
  (let [selection @(rf/subscribe [::subs/pending-selection])
        hand @(rf/subscribe [::subs/hand :player-1])]
    (when selection
      [:div.selection-modal
       {:class "fixed inset-0 bg-black bg-opacity-50 flex items-center justify-center"}
       [:div.modal-content
        {:class "bg-gray-800 p-4 rounded-lg max-w-md"}
        [:h2.text-white.text-lg.mb-2
         (str "Select " (:selection/count selection) " cards to discard")]
        [:p.text-gray-400.mb-4
         (str (count (:selection/selected selection)) " selected")]
        
        ;; Card grid
        [:div.grid.grid-cols-3.gap-2.mb-4
         (for [card hand]
           (let [obj-id (:object/id card)
                 selected? (contains? (:selection/selected selection) obj-id)]
             ^{:key obj-id}
             [:div.card
              {:class (if selected? "ring-2 ring-blue-500" "")
               :on-click #(rf/dispatch [::events/toggle-selection obj-id])}
              (:card/name (:object/card card))]))]
        
        ;; Buttons
        [:div.flex.justify-end.gap-2
         [:button.px-4.py-2.bg-gray-600.rounded
          {:on-click #(rf/dispatch [::events/cancel-selection])}
          "Cancel"]
         [:button.px-4.py-2.bg-blue-600.rounded
          {:disabled (not= (count (:selection/selected selection))
                           (:selection/count selection))
           :on-click #(rf/dispatch [::events/confirm-selection])}
          "Confirm"]]]])))
```

### 8. Add Careful Study card definition
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs

```clojure
;; Careful Study - Card filtering cantrip
;; Draw two cards, then discard two cards.
(def careful-study
  {:card/id :careful-study
   :card/name "Careful Study"
   :card/cmc 1
   :card/mana-cost {:blue 1}
   :card/colors #{:blue}
   :card/types #{:sorcery}
   :card/text "Draw two cards, then discard two cards."
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :draw :effect/amount 2}
                  {:effect/type :discard
                   :effect/count 2
                   :effect/selection :player}]})
```

### 9. Add subscription for pending selection
File: src/main/fizzle/subs.cljs (or core.cljs if subs are there)

```clojure
(rf/reg-sub
  ::pending-selection
  (fn [db _]
    (:game/pending-selection db)))
```

### 10. Update all-cards vector and run validation

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Careful Study definition exists with :card/mana-cost {:blue 1}
- [ ] Careful Study has :card/types #{:sorcery}
- [ ] Casting Careful Study draws 2 cards to hand
- [ ] After draw, pending selection created with :count 2, :zone :hand
- [ ] Selection modal appears when pending-selection exists
- [ ] Clicking card toggles it in/out of :selection/selected set
- [ ] Confirm button disabled when count != required count
- [ ] Confirming valid selection moves selected cards to graveyard
- [ ] Cancel button clears selection (re-prompts)
- [ ] 8+ tests pass in careful_study_test.cljs
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO auto-selecting discard targets (user agency: player must choose)
- ❌ NO blocking UI during selection (must be dismissable)
- ❌ NO skipping selection even if hand has exactly 2 cards (consistency)
- ❌ NO hardcoded card IDs in selection logic (generic system)
- ❌ NO storing selection state in Datascript game-db (use app-db)
- ❌ NO modifying spell resolution to auto-discard on timeout

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Selection State Location**
- Selection state is UI state, NOT game state
- Lives in re-frame app-db at :game/pending-selection
- Do NOT add to Datascript schema - that's for game state persistence
- This allows selection to be cleared on UI reset without affecting game

**Cancel Behavior Complexity**
- Careful Study's cancel is tricky: cards already drawn can't be "undrawn"
- For MVP: Cancel clears selection but player must still discard 2
- Alternative: Cancel reverts entire spell (complex - need state snapshot)
- Decision: MVP approach - cancel just re-prompts selection

**Effect Execution Pause Pattern**
- Effects execute synchronously in engine
- Selection breaks this - need async pattern
- Solution: resolve-spell returns partial state when hitting selection effect
- Remaining effects stored in pending-selection
- On confirm, remaining effects continue

**Hand Size Edge Cases**
- After drawing 2, player might have exactly 2 cards → must still select
- After drawing 2, player might have only 1 card → ERROR CASE
  - This shouldn't happen (Careful Study draws 2, discard 2)
  - But if library was depleted mid-draw, could have <2 cards
  - Decision: If hand < count required, force discard all available
  - Add test for this edge case

**Reference Implementation**
- Study src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs for re-frame event patterns
- Study src/main/fizzle/core.cljs for Reagent component patterns
- Study how :game/selected-card already works for card selection
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 2: Cleanup repeat when triggered abilities fire
design:
## Goal
Implement cleanup repeat: after grant expiration, if triggered abilities fire during cleanup (e.g., discard triggers), the cleanup cycle restarts after they resolve (MTG Rule 514.3).

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Context
Completed fizzle-8rj: Core cleanup discard is working. begin-cleanup and complete-cleanup-discard handle hand size check and grant expiration. Now need the repeat cycle.

## Implementation

### 1. Add maybe-continue-cleanup helper (events/game.cljs)
Pure function that wraps resolution results to check for cleanup continuation:
```clojure
(defn- maybe-continue-cleanup
  "After stack resolution during cleanup, check if cleanup should restart.
   If stack is now empty and phase is :cleanup, apply begin-cleanup.
   Returns updated app-db."
  [app-db]
  (let [game-db (:game/db app-db)]
    (if (and (= :cleanup (:game/phase (queries/get-game-state game-db)))
             (queries/stack-empty? game-db :player-1)
             (not (:game/pending-selection app-db)))  ;; not already prompting
      (let [result (begin-cleanup game-db :player-1)]
        (if (:pending-selection result)
          (-> app-db
              (assoc :game/db (:db result))
              (assoc :game/pending-selection (:pending-selection result)))
          (assoc app-db :game/db (:db result))))
      app-db)))
```
This avoids duplicating the check in each ::resolve-top branch.

### 2. Modify ::resolve-top handler (events/game.cljs, line ~306)
After each resolution branch that resolves a stack item, wrap the result with maybe-continue-cleanup:
- Spell resolution branch (line ~327): wrap result in maybe-continue-cleanup
- Activated ability branch (line ~336): wrap result in maybe-continue-cleanup
- Normal trigger branch (line ~345): wrap result in maybe-continue-cleanup
- Empty stack branch: no change (nothing resolved)

Example for the normal trigger branch:
```clojure
;; Before:
resolve-trigger?
(assoc db :game/db (resolve-top-of-stack game-db :player-1))

;; After:
resolve-trigger?
(maybe-continue-cleanup
  (assoc db :game/db (resolve-top-of-stack game-db :player-1)))
```

### 3. Also wrap ::confirm-selection for :cleanup-discard
The existing :cleanup-discard case in ::confirm-selection calls complete-cleanup-discard which expires grants. After grant expiration, triggers might fire. Wrap the result:
```clojure
;; In the :cleanup-discard branch of ::confirm-selection:
(maybe-continue-cleanup
  (-> db
      (assoc :game/db (:db result))
      (dissoc :game/pending-selection)))
```
Wait — actually, after complete-cleanup-discard, triggers aren't on the stack yet. Triggers get placed on the stack by trigger dispatch, which happens during phase advancement (not during grant expiration). So the stack won't have new triggers after confirm-selection. The repeat only applies when existing triggers resolve and empty the stack. Skip this for now.

### 4. TDD Test Cases (events/turn_test.cljs)
1. `test-cleanup-repeat-after-trigger-resolves` - Set up: phase :cleanup, add trigger to stack. Resolve trigger via resolve-top-of-stack. After stack empties, begin-cleanup should re-run (re-expire grants). Bug caught: cleanup doesn't restart after triggers resolve.
2. `test-cleanup-no-repeat-when-stack-not-empty` - Set up: phase :cleanup, add 2 triggers. Resolve one. Stack still has items. begin-cleanup should NOT re-run. Bug caught: false positive - cleanup restarts with items still on stack.
3. `test-cleanup-repeat-does-not-trigger-at-other-phases` - Set up: phase :main1, add trigger. Resolve trigger. Stack empties. begin-cleanup should NOT run. Bug caught: cleanup logic fires during non-cleanup phases.
4. `test-cleanup-repeat-rechecks-hand-size` - Set up: phase :cleanup, 9 cards in hand. Complete cleanup discard (hand goes to 7). Add trigger that draws a card (would need to simulate). After trigger resolves and stack empties, begin-cleanup re-checks hand size. If still over max, new selection appears. Bug caught: hand size not rechecked after cleanup repeat.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Resolving last stack item during cleanup phase re-runs begin-cleanup
- [ ] Stack items remaining during cleanup do NOT trigger cleanup restart
- [ ] Cleanup restart does NOT fire during non-cleanup phases
- [ ] After cleanup restart, hand size is rechecked against max
- [ ] Normal cleanup (no triggers) still works correctly (existing tests pass)
- [ ] All 737+ tests pass (make test)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Infinite Loop**
- If a trigger during cleanup causes another trigger, cleanup could loop forever
- In practice this is bounded: triggers resolve finite effects, hand size eventually <= max
- No explicit loop guard needed — game state converges naturally
- If needed later, could add a max-cleanup-repeat counter

**Edge Case: Pending Selection During Cleanup**
- If resolve-top returns a pending-selection (spell with selection effect resolving during cleanup), maybe-continue-cleanup must NOT run begin-cleanup
- The guard `(not (:game/pending-selection app-db))` prevents this

**Edge Case: Stack Has Both Triggers and Spells**
- resolve-top already handles this via LIFO ordering
- maybe-continue-cleanup only checks after resolution, regardless of what was resolved

**Edge Case: resolve-top-of-stack Returns db Unchanged**
- If the trigger type has no matching resolve-trigger method (uses :default), db is unchanged
- Stack item is still removed (triggers/remove-trigger called), so stack DOES shrink
- maybe-continue-cleanup will still fire correctly when stack empties

**Reference Implementation**
- maybe-continue-cleanup follows same pattern as begin-cleanup check in ::advance-phase handler
- Reuses begin-cleanup pure function — no duplication of logic

## Anti-patterns (from epic)
- ❌ NO hardcoding 7 (uses begin-cleanup which reads get-max-hand-size)
- ❌ NO skipping cleanup repeat check (the whole point of this task)
- ❌ NO expiring grants before discard (begin-cleanup handles ordering)
- ❌ NO infinite loop without convergence guarantee
close_reason: Implemented maybe-continue-cleanup, wired into ::resolve-top handler. All 4 tests pass, make validate clean.

title: Task 1: Add Underground River card definition and deck entry
design:
## Goal
Add Underground River card to iggy_pop.cljs with three mana abilities and include in deck list.

## Effort Estimate
1-2 hours

## Implementation Checklist

### Step 1: Add card definition to iggy_pop.cljs
Location: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs after line 167 (after Swamp)

```clojure
;; Underground River - Pain land producing blue or black mana
;; T: Add {C}. T: Add {U} or {B}. Underground River deals 1 damage to you.
(def underground-river
  {:card/id :underground-river
   :card/name "Underground River"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:land}
   :card/text "{T}: Add {C}. {T}: Add {U} or {B}. Underground River deals 1 damage to you."
   :card/abilities [{:ability/type :mana
                     :ability/cost {:tap true}
                     :ability/produces {:colorless 1}}
                    {:ability/type :mana
                     :ability/cost {:tap true}
                     :ability/produces {:blue 1}
                     :ability/effects [{:effect/type :deal-damage
                                        :effect/amount 1
                                        :effect/target :controller}]}
                    {:ability/type :mana
                     :ability/cost {:tap true}
                     :ability/produces {:black 1}
                     :ability/effects [{:effect/type :deal-damage
                                        :effect/amount 1
                                        :effect/target :controller}]}]})
```

## Success Criteria

title: Task 3: Brain Freeze integration tests + storm copy regression tests
design:
## Goal
Create integration tests for Brain Freeze (the deck's win condition - storm mill spell) and regression tests for storm copy behavior (copies cease to exist when leaving stack). These are closely related: Brain Freeze exercises the full storm pipeline end-to-end.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Pre-existing Coverage
- rules_test.cljs has cast-spell-increments-storm-test (storm count only, not trigger/copies)
- queries_test.cljs has storm count query tests
- turn_test.cljs has storm count reset test
- NO tests exist for: storm trigger resolution, storm copy creation, copies ceasing to exist, mill effect

## Implementation

### Part A: Brain Freeze card integration tests

**File:** src/test/fizzle/cards/brain_freeze_test.cljs (NEW)

**Card definition** (iggy_pop.cljs):
\`\`\`clojure
{:card/id :brain-freeze
 :card/name "Brain Freeze"
 :card/cmc 2
 :card/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :blue 1}
 :card/colors #{:blue}
 :card/types #{:instant}
 :card/keywords #{:storm}
 :card/effects [{:effect/type :mill :effect/amount 3 :effect/target :opponent}]}
\`\`\`

**Required imports:**
\`\`\`clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.brain-freeze-test
  (:require
    [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.cards.iggy-pop :as cards]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
    [fizzle.db.schema :refer [schema]]
    [fizzle.engine.mana :as mana]
    [fizzle.engine.rules :as rules]
    [fizzle.engine.triggers :as triggers]
    [fizzle.engine.zones :as zones]))
\`\`\`

**Helpers:** Use same create-test-db + add-card-to-zone pattern from dark_ritual_test.cljs. Also need:
- add-opponent helper: transact {:player/id :opponent :player/name "Opponent" :player/life 20 ...}
- add-cards-to-library helper: takes card-ids vector and adds to library with sequential positions for player

**Tests:**

1. `brain-freeze-card-definition-test`
   - Assert: card exists in all-cards with :card/id :brain-freeze
   - Assert: :card/keywords contains :storm
   - Assert: :card/mana-cost is {:colorless 1 :blue 1}
   - Assert: single effect of type :mill with :effect/amount 3 and :effect/target :opponent
   - Bug caught: Card data integrity, ensures storm keyword present

2. `brain-freeze-mills-opponent-3-cards-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add opponent, add brain-freeze to hand, add 5 cards to opponent library, add {:blue 1 :colorless 1} mana
   - Act: cast-spell, resolve-spell
   - Assert: opponent library size decreased by 3
   - Assert: opponent graveyard size increased by 3
   - Assert: brain-freeze in player graveyard (instant)
   - Assert: storm count = 1
   - Bug caught: Verifies mill effect targets opponent correctly

3. `brain-freeze-cannot-cast-without-mana-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add brain-freeze to hand, NO mana
   - Assert: (rules/can-cast? db :player-1 obj-id) returns false
   - Bug caught: Mana cost enforcement for {1}{U}

4. `brain-freeze-creates-storm-trigger-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, cast 2 Dark Rituals first (storm count = 2), add brain-freeze to hand, add mana
   - Act: cast brain-freeze (storm count becomes 3)
   - Assert: storm trigger exists on stack
   - Assert: storm trigger has :count = 2 (spells cast BEFORE brain freeze, i.e. dec(3) = 2)
   - Bug caught: Storm trigger creation with correct copy count

5. `brain-freeze-storm-copies-mill-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add opponent, add 15+ cards to opponent library
   - Cast 2 Dark Rituals first (storm count = 2)
   - Cast brain-freeze (storm count = 3, trigger with count=2)
   - Act: resolve storm trigger (creates 2 copies on stack), resolve each copy + original
   - Assert: opponent milled 9 total cards (3 per copy * 3 instances = original + 2 copies)
