   - Assert: copies no longer exist in db (removed via remove-object)
   - Assert: original brain-freeze in graveyard
   - Bug caught: Full storm pipeline end-to-end, copies cease to exist

6. `brain-freeze-storm-zero-previous-spells-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add opponent, add 5 cards to opponent library
   - Cast brain-freeze as FIRST spell (storm count = 1)
   - Assert: storm trigger has count = 0 (dec(1) = 0, no copies)
   - Act: resolve trigger (no copies created)
   - Assert: only 3 cards milled (original only, no copies)
   - Bug caught: Storm edge case - first spell of game

### Part B: Storm copy regression tests

**File:** src/test/fizzle/cards/brain_freeze_test.cljs (same file, separate testing section)

These regression tests specifically verify the bug fix from commit 46a281f where storm copies were going to graveyard instead of ceasing to exist.

7. `storm-copy-ceases-to-exist-on-resolve-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add a spell to stack
   - Create a copy via triggers/create-spell-copy
   - Assert: copy has :object/is-copy true
   - Act: rules/resolve-spell on the copy
   - Assert: (q/get-object db' copy-id) returns nil (entity removed from db entirely)
   - Assert: copy is NOT in graveyard zone
   - Bug caught: Regression for storm copies going to graveyard instead of ceasing to exist

8. `storm-copy-ceases-to-exist-when-countered-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add a spell, create copy
   - Act: rules/move-spell-off-stack on the copy
   - Assert: (q/get-object db' copy-id) returns nil
   - Bug caught: Copies also cease to exist when countered/fizzled, not just resolved

9. `storm-copies-dont-trigger-storm-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add brain-freeze to hand, cast it (storm=1)
   - Resolve storm trigger to create copy
   - Manually cast the copy (should not happen in game, but defensive test)
   - Assert: no new storm trigger created from the copy
   - Bug caught: Verifies :object/is-copy prevents recursive storm triggers

### Part C: Mill effect edge cases

10. `mill-with-fewer-cards-than-amount-test`
    - Setup: create-test-db, add opponent with only 1 card in library
    - Execute mill effect with amount=3
    - Assert: mills only 1 card (all available), doesn't crash
    - Bug caught: Mill graceful degradation with insufficient library

## Success Criteria
- [ ] brain_freeze_test.cljs exists with 10 tests
- [ ] Brain Freeze mills 3 cards from opponent's library when resolved
- [ ] Storm trigger created with correct copy count (dec of storm count)
- [ ] Storm copies resolve correctly and each mills 3 additional cards
- [ ] Storm copies cease to exist (removed from db) after resolution - regression for 46a281f
- [ ] Storm copies cease to exist when countered/moved off stack
- [ ] Storm copies do not trigger storm themselves
- [ ] First spell of game creates storm trigger with count=0 (no copies)
- [ ] Mill with fewer cards than amount handles gracefully
- [ ] All 806+ existing tests still pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Resolving storm copies in correct order**
- Stack is LIFO. After storm trigger creates copies, they sit on top of stack above original.
- Copies resolve first (mill 3 each), then original resolves (mill 3).
- Test must resolve in stack order to be realistic.
- Use rules/resolve-spell directly on each copy ID, not resolve-top-of-stack (which involves app-db).

**Edge Case: Opponent library too small for full mill**
- get-top-n-library returns available cards when n > library size
- Mill effect uses (or (q/get-top-n-library ...) [])
- With 0 library cards, returns [], reduce over empty vec = no-op
- Test: opponent with 1 library card, mill 3 → should mill 1, not crash

**Edge Case: Storm count timing**
- cast-spell-mode calls increment-storm BEFORE maybe-create-storm-trigger
- So storm trigger gets copy-count = dec(storm-count) which equals spells before this one
- If 2 previous spells, storm count = 3 after casting brain freeze, trigger count = dec(3) = 2
- Test must verify this exact arithmetic

**Edge Case: Storm trigger with source removed**
- If source spell no longer exists when storm trigger resolves, resolve-trigger :storm returns db unchanged
- This is defensive code, but worth noting - not testing it here (unlikely scenario)

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow dark_ritual_test.cljs for create-test-db + add-card-to-zone helpers
- Follow rules_test.cljs for cast/resolve assertions
- See triggers.cljs:182-203 for storm trigger resolution
- See rules.cljs:430-454 for resolve-spell copy handling

## Anti-patterns
- NO mocking Datascript (pure function tests against real DB)
- NO test-only methods in production code
- NO testing through re-frame events (test pure functions directly)
- NO using (is (some? result)) as sole assertion - always assert specific values
- NO duplicating the exact same helper functions verbatim from other files
close_reason: All 10 tests implemented: card definition, mill effect, storm trigger creation, full storm pipeline (2 copies + original = 9 milled), storm-zero edge case, copy ceases-to-exist on resolve, copy ceases-to-exist when countered, copies don't trigger storm, mill with insufficient library. make validate passes: 816 tests, 1764 assertions.

title: Phase 1: Storm Keyword + Card Definitions
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

- Storm triggers on CAST, not resolution (rules-accurate timing)
- Storm creates a separate trigger object on the stack
- Storm trigger resolves BEFORE original spell (trigger goes on top)
- Storm copies are NOT cast (do not increment storm count)
- Storm copies do not have active storm keyword (no cascade)
- Storm count = spells cast BEFORE the storm spell this turn
- Threshold activates at exactly 7+ cards in graveyard
- Conditional effects use card-level modes (not per-effect condition checks)
- Mill handles partial library gracefully (mill N or remaining, whichever is less)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] Brain Freeze, Cabal Ritual, LED, Dark Ritual, basic lands implemented
- [ ] Storm trigger creates correct number of copies
- [ ] Copies resolve before original spell
- [ ] Threshold condition affects Cabal Ritual output
- [ ] Mill effect moves cards from library to graveyard
- [ ] Integration test: Cast 2 rituals + Brain Freeze = 3 mill resolutions (9 cards)
- [ ] All edge case tests pass (zero storm, empty library, threshold boundary)
- [ ] All existing tests still pass
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass (lint, format)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO storm copies incrementing storm count (breaks rules: copies are not cast)
- ❌ NO condition checks inside individual effect methods (duplication: use card-level modes)
- ❌ NO storm triggering on resolution (timing: MTG rules say cast-time trigger)
- ❌ NO hardcoded card logic in engine (architecture: cards are data, engine interprets)
- ❌ NO skipping edge case tests (quality: edge cases reveal design flaws)

## Approach

Implement storm as a rules-accurate triggered ability system:

1. Extend stack to hold trigger objects alongside spells
2. When casting a spell with :storm keyword, create trigger on top of spell
3. Storm trigger resolution creates N copies (N = spells cast before this turn)
4. Copies are new objects with :object/is-copy true
5. Conditional effects use card-level :card/conditional-effects for threshold

This approach matches MTG rules precisely while building infrastructure for future triggered abilities (Prowess, ETB effects, etc).

## Architecture

### New Schema
- :trigger/id, :trigger/type, :trigger/source, :trigger/controller, :trigger/data
- :object/is-copy (boolean, default false)

### Modified Functions
- rules/cast-spell: Check for :storm keyword, create trigger if present
- rules/resolve-spell: Handle trigger resolution separately from spell resolution

### New Functions
- rules/create-storm-trigger: Creates trigger object
- rules/resolve-trigger: Multimethod dispatching on :trigger/type
- rules/create-spell-copy: Creates copy of spell on stack
- effects/execute-effect :mill: Move cards from library to graveyard
- conditions/threshold?: Check graveyard count >= 7
- rules/get-active-effects: Select effects based on conditions

### Card Definitions
- brain-freeze: {instant, :storm, mill 3}
- cabal-ritual: {instant, :threshold, add-mana BBB/BBBBB}
- lions-eye-diamond: {artifact, activated ability}
- Basic lands (swamp, island, etc)

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Phase 1 requires storm combo to work. Current engine has no triggered ability system and only one card (Dark Ritual). Cannot test storm combos.

### Research Findings

**Codebase:**
- engine/effects.cljs:1-20 - Multimethod dispatch on :effect/type, easy to extend
- engine/rules.cljs:30-35 - Storm count tracked, incremented on cast-spell
- db/queries.cljs - get-storm-count, get-objects-in-zone available
- No infrastructure for triggers or copies

**External:**
- MTG rules: Storm triggers on cast, creates copies equal to prior spell count
- Copies are NOT cast (critical for storm count accuracy)
- Trigger resolves before spell (copies first, then original)

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Strict Rules-Accurate (Full Trigger Model) ✓

**What it is:** Model storm as a triggered ability with explicit trigger objects on stack. Trigger resolves first, creates copies, then original spell resolves.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed MTG comprehensive rules for storm timing
- Examined codebase for extension points (multimethod pattern)
- Confirmed stack can hold different object types

**Pros:**
- Accurate MTG timing
- Builds trigger infrastructure for future abilities (ETB, Prowess)
- Supports future counter-spell interactions

**Cons:**
- More complex than simplified approaches

**Chosen because:** User explicitly chose rules-accurate approach; infrastructure investment pays off for future phases

#### 2. Simplified Cast-Time Copies ❌

**What it is:** Create copies during cast-spell without explicit trigger objects. Correct resolution order but no separate trigger.

**Why we looked at this:** Simpler implementation for goldfish practice

**Investigation:**
- Would work for Phase 1 but limits future interactions
- Cannot model 'counter the trigger' scenarios

**Pros:**
- Simpler implementation
- Correct resolution order

**Cons:**
- No trigger interaction support
- Technical debt for Phase 3+ (fork/replay of trigger decisions)

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** User chose rules-accurate; future phases need trigger model

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Requirements change to goldfish-only permanently

#### 3. Ultra-Simplified Resolution Multiplier ❌

**What it is:** Execute effects N+1 times during resolve-spell. No copies on stack.

**Why we looked at this:** Minimal code change

**Investigation:**
- Breaks MTG timing model entirely
- Cannot visualize copies on stack for practice

**Pros:**
- Simplest implementation

**Cons:**
- Rules-inaccurate timing
- No stack visualization for learning
- Breaks mental model for combo practice

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Defeats purpose of practice tool (learning real timing)

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Requirements pivot to non-educational tool

### Conditional Effects Design Decision

**Decision:** Card-level effect modes (Option B) over per-effect condition checks

**Rationale:**
- Avoids duplicating condition checks in every effect method
- Effects stay pure and simple
- Rules layer handles condition selection once
- Matches MTG card text structure ('Add BBB. Threshold - Add BBBBB instead.')

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- Storm keyword with full trigger model
- Brain Freeze, Cabal Ritual, LED, basic lands
- Mill effect, threshold condition
- Edge case tests

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- UI (Phase 1 completion, separate task)
- Other keywords (flashback, madness)
- Sideboard cards
- Deck import/export

### Open Questions
- LED mana color choice: UI required, may stub initially
- Threshold during resolution: Should graveyard count update mid-stack? (Default: yes, recalculate per effect)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Which Phase 1 area to tackle? | Cards + Storm together | Implement both in single epic |
| Storm implementation approach? | Strict Rules-Accurate | Full trigger model, not simplified |
| Triggered ability architecture? | Yes, proceed | Explicit trigger objects on stack |
| Card definitions correct? | Yes for Phase 1 | Brain Freeze, Cabal Ritual, LED, lands |
| Conditional effects approach? | Option B: Card-level modes | No condition checks inside effects |
| Testing strategy? | Add edge cases | Comprehensive tests including boundaries |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Codebase Architecture
**Question explored:** How do effects and rules work?
**Sources consulted:**
- engine/effects.cljs - multimethod dispatch pattern
- engine/rules.cljs - cast/resolve flow, storm count tracking
- db/queries.cljs - available queries for graveyard, storm count

**Findings:**
- Clean extension points via multimethod
- Storm count already tracked but copies not implemented
- No trigger infrastructure exists

**Conclusion:** Extend existing multimethod pattern, add trigger resolution

#### MTG Storm Rules
**Question explored:** Exact timing and mechanics of storm
**Sources consulted:**
- MTG Comprehensive Rules
- Judge blogs on storm mechanics
- Gatherer rulings for Brain Freeze

**Findings:**
- Storm triggers on cast, not resolution
- Copies NOT cast (critical)
- Trigger resolves first, then spell

**Conclusion:** Need explicit trigger model for accuracy

### Dead-End Paths

#### Per-Effect Condition Checks
**Why explored:** Initial design had conditions inside each effect method
**Investigation:** User pointed out duplication concern
**Why abandoned:** Would duplicate condition logic in every effect type

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'Condition checks duplicated?' → Moved to card-level effect modes (Option B)
- 'LED needs color choice UI' → May stub initially, full UI in later task
close_reason: Phase 1 complete: Storm keyword with full trigger model, Brain Freeze/Cabal Ritual/Dark Ritual cards, mill effect, threshold condition. All 75 tests pass. Integration test verifies 2 rituals + Brain Freeze = 9 cards milled.

title: Implement history data model and core operations
design:
## Goal

Build the pure-function foundation for the history system: data structures, initialization, entry management, stepping, forking, branch switching, and effective-entry-list computation. All operations are pure functions on app-db — no re-frame integration yet.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Create src/main/fizzle/history/core.cljs

Define data structures and pure operations:

#### Data initialization
- `init-history` — Returns initial history state map to merge into app-db:
  ```clojure
  {:history/main []
   :history/forks {}
   :history/current-branch nil
   :history/position -1}
  ```
  Position -1 means "no entries recorded yet." After the first entry is appended, position becomes 0.

#### Entry operations
- `make-entry [game-db event-type description turn]` — Creates an entry map:
  ```clojure
  {:entry/snapshot game-db
   :entry/event-type event-type
   :entry/description description
   :entry/turn turn}
  ```
- `append-entry [db entry]` — Appends entry to current branch tip, sets position to new tip index. PRECONDITION: must be at tip (at-tip? returns true) or position is -1 (empty history). If not at tip, returns db unchanged (caller should use auto-fork instead).

#### Stepping
- `step-to [db position]` — Sets :history/position, swaps :game/db to the :entry/snapshot at that position in effective entries. Returns updated db. If position < 0 or position >= entry-count, returns db unchanged (no-op on invalid bounds).
- `at-tip? [db]` — Returns true if position equals (dec (entry-count db)), or if position is -1 and history is empty.
- `can-step-back? [db]` — Returns true if position > 0.
- `can-step-forward? [db]` — Returns true if position < (dec (entry-count db)).

#### Forking
- `auto-fork [db entry]` — Creates new fork at current position with entry as first fork entry. Fork-id generated via (random-uuid). Fork name auto-generated as "Fork N" where N is (inc (count forks)). Sets :history/current-branch to new fork-id. Sets position to (branch-point + 1). Returns updated db.
  ```clojure
  ;; Fork structure:
  {:fork/id <uuid>
   :fork/name "Fork 1"
   :fork/branch-point 5       ;; INCLUSIVE — last index from parent included
   :fork/parent nil            ;; nil = parent is main, or fork-id for nested
   :fork/entries [entry]}      ;; First entry of the new branch
  ```
- `create-named-fork [db name]` — Creates empty fork at current position with given name. Does NOT switch to it. Does NOT add any entries. Returns updated db with new fork in :history/forks.

#### Branch-point semantics (CRITICAL)
Branch-point is INCLUSIVE — it is the last index from the parent's effective entries that is included in this fork's effective entries. Example:
```
main: [e0, e1, e2, e3, e4, e5]
Player at position 3, takes new action → auto-fork
Fork: {:branch-point 3, :entries [new_entry]}
Effective entries: [e0, e1, e2, e3, new_entry]  (indices 0-4)
Main is unchanged: [e0, e1, e2, e3, e4, e5]
```

For nested forks, branch-point refers to an index in the PARENT's effective entry list (not the parent's local entries):
```
Fork A effective: [e0, e1, e2, e3, fa0, fa1]  (6 entries)
Player at position 4 in Fork A, takes new action → auto-fork from Fork A
Fork B: {:branch-point 4, :parent fork-a-id, :entries [new_entry]}
Fork B effective: [e0, e1, e2, e3, fa0, new_entry]  (6 entries)
```

#### Branch operations
- `switch-branch [db fork-id]` — Sets :history/current-branch to fork-id (or nil for main). Sets position to tip of that branch. Swaps :game/db to snapshot at tip. If fork-id is not nil and not found in :history/forks, returns db unchanged (no-op).
- `get-fork [db fork-id]` — Returns fork map from :history/forks, or nil if not found.
- `list-forks [db]` — Returns vector of all fork maps (vals of :history/forks).

#### Effective entries
- `effective-entries [db]` — Computes full entry list for current branch by walking parent chain:
  - If on main (current-branch nil): returns :history/main
  - If on a fork: recursively get parent's effective entries, take first (inc branch-point) entries, concat with fork's entries
  ```clojure
  (defn effective-entries-for-branch [db fork-id]
    (if (nil? fork-id)
      (:history/main db)
      (let [fork (get-fork db fork-id)
            parent-entries (effective-entries-for-branch db (:fork/parent fork))]
        (into (vec (take (inc (:fork/branch-point fork)) parent-entries))
              (:fork/entries fork)))))
  ```
- `current-entry [db]` — Returns entry at current position, or nil if position is -1 (no entries).
- `entry-count [db]` — Returns (count (effective-entries db)).

### 2. Create src/test/fizzle/history/core_test.cljs

Use mock game-db values (distinct keywords like :db-0, :db-1 etc.) to verify snapshot swapping without needing real Datascript.

#### Test cases (22 tests):

**Initialization:**
- init-history returns map with all four keys, empty main, empty forks, nil branch, position -1

**Entry creation and appending:**
- make-entry creates entry map with all four fields (:entry/snapshot, :entry/event-type, :entry/description, :entry/turn)
- append-entry on empty history (position -1) adds to main, sets position to 0
- append-entry at tip of main adds entry, increments position
- append-entry at tip of fork adds to fork's entries, increments position
- append-entry when NOT at tip returns db unchanged (precondition violation)

**Stepping:**
- step-to valid position updates :history/position and swaps :game/db to snapshot at that position
- step-to position 0 from later position (rewind to start)
- step-to out of bounds (negative) returns db unchanged
- step-to out of bounds (>= count) returns db unchanged
- can-step-back? returns false at position 0, true at position > 0
- can-step-forward? returns false at tip, true before tip
- at-tip? returns true when position equals last index, true when empty history

**Forking:**
- auto-fork at position 3 of 6-entry main creates fork with branch-point 3
- auto-fork preserves original main entries (all 6 still present)
- auto-fork sets current-branch to new fork-id
- auto-fork entry appears as last in effective entries
- auto-fork from a fork creates nested fork with correct parent

**Branch switching:**
- switch-branch to fork updates current-branch, position to fork tip, game-db to fork tip snapshot
- switch-branch to nil returns to main tip
- switch-branch to non-existent fork-id returns db unchanged

**Effective entries:**
- effective-entries on main returns :history/main
- effective-entries on fork returns parent[0..branch-point] ++ fork entries
- effective-entries on nested fork (3 levels: main -> fork A -> fork B) correctly walks full parent chain

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All 22+ tests pass: make test
- [ ] All operations are pure functions — no require of re-frame, no side effects, no atoms
- [ ] effective-entries on 3-level nested fork returns correct combined list
- [ ] step-to at any valid position swaps :game/db to that entry's snapshot
- [ ] auto-fork from rewound position preserves original timeline unchanged
- [ ] append-entry at non-tip returns db unchanged (does not corrupt history)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Empty History**
- Position -1 means no entries. current-entry returns nil. at-tip? returns true (nothing to step forward to). can-step-back? returns false. can-step-forward? returns false. append-entry works (transitions from -1 to 0).

**Edge Case: Fork at Position 0**
- Branch-point 0 means effective entries = [parent-entry-0] ++ fork-entries. This is valid — the fork diverges right after the initial state.

**Edge Case: Deeply Nested Forks (3+ levels)**
- effective-entries must recurse through parent chain correctly. Test with 3 levels: main -> fork-A -> fork-B. Each level takes a prefix of its parent and appends local entries.

**Edge Case: Switch to Non-existent Fork**
- Return db unchanged. Do NOT throw — callers should handle gracefully.

**Edge Case: Append When Not at Tip**
- Return db unchanged. The caller (interceptor in future task) must check at-tip? and call auto-fork instead. This separation keeps core operations simple.

**Branch-Point Semantics**
- Branch-point is INCLUSIVE (last parent index included). This is the most critical invariant. Off-by-one here corrupts all effective entry computations. Test thoroughly with concrete index assertions.

**Fork-ID Generation**
- Use (random-uuid) for fork IDs. Fork names use sequential counter: "Fork N" where N = (inc (count (:history/forks db))). Names are display-only; IDs are for lookup.

## Anti-Patterns (Task-Level)

- NO re-frame dependency in history/core.cljs (pure functions only — re-frame integration is a separate task)
- NO atoms or mutable state (all functions take and return immutable maps)
- NO throwing exceptions for invalid input (return db unchanged for out-of-bounds, non-existent forks, non-tip append)
- NO string-based fork IDs (use UUIDs for uniqueness)
- NO flattened fork storage (maintain parent chain for correct nested fork computation)
close_reason: All 27 tests pass (899 total). Pure functions only, no re-frame. 3-level nested fork effective-entries verified. make validate clean.

title: Task 2: Implement :add-restriction effect type
design:
## Goal
Add the :add-restriction effect type to effects.cljs that creates a :restriction grant on a target player. This enables spells like Orim's Chant to prevent players from casting spells.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Context
Completed fizzle-d3y: Player grants system now works. We can add grants to players and they expire correctly.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
Reference implementations:
- effects.cljs:106-108 - :add-mana effect (simple pattern)
- effects.cljs:111-132 - :mill effect (shows target resolution pattern)
- effects.cljs:144-171 - :lose-life effect (shows player-eid validation)
- grants.cljs:107-131 - add-player-grant function (just implemented)
- queries.cljs - q/get-game-state for current turn

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

Add tests to effects_test.cljs:
```clojure
;; Test 1: add-restriction effect creates grant on target player
(deftest add-restriction-creates-grant-test
  (testing "creates restriction grant on target player"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          ;; Create a spell object with target stored
          source-id (random-uuid)
          [source-id db] (add-spell-with-target db :player-1 source-id :opponent)
          effect {:effect/type :add-restriction
                  :restriction/type :cannot-cast-spells
                  :effect/target :targeted-player}
          db' (effects/execute-effect db :player-1 effect source-id)]
      (is (= 1 (count (grants/get-player-grants db' :opponent)))
          "Should create one grant on opponent")
      (is (= :restriction (:grant/type (first (grants/get-player-grants db' :opponent))))
          "Grant type should be :restriction")
      (is (= :cannot-cast-spells (get-in (first (grants/get-player-grants db' :opponent)) [:grant/data :restriction/type]))
          "Restriction type should be :cannot-cast-spells"))))

;; Test 2: restriction grant expires at current turn cleanup
(deftest add-restriction-expires-at-cleanup-test
  (testing "restriction grant expires at end of current turn"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          ;; Set game to turn 3
          game-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :where [?e :game/id _]] db)
          db (d/db-with db [[:db/add game-eid :game/turn 3]])
          [source-id db] (add-spell-with-target db :player-1 (random-uuid) :opponent)
          effect {:effect/type :add-restriction
                  :restriction/type :cannot-cast-spells
                  :effect/target :targeted-player}
          db' (effects/execute-effect db :player-1 effect source-id)
          grant (first (grants/get-player-grants db' :opponent))]
      (is (= 3 (get-in grant [:grant/expires :expires/turn]))
          "Should expire on turn 3")
      (is (= :cleanup (get-in grant [:grant/expires :expires/phase]))
          "Should expire at cleanup phase"))))

;; Test 3: cannot-attack restriction type works
(deftest add-restriction-cannot-attack-test
  (testing "can create cannot-attack restriction"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          [source-id db] (add-spell-with-target db :player-1 (random-uuid) :opponent)
          effect {:effect/type :add-restriction
                  :restriction/type :cannot-attack
                  :effect/target :targeted-player}
          db' (effects/execute-effect db :player-1 effect source-id)
          grant (first (grants/get-player-grants db' :opponent))]
      (is (= :cannot-attack (get-in grant [:grant/data :restriction/type]))
          "Restriction type should be :cannot-attack"))))

;; Test 4: effect targets self when :effect/target is :self
(deftest add-restriction-targets-self-test
  (testing "targets controller when effect specifies :self"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          source-id (random-uuid)
          effect {:effect/type :add-restriction
                  :restriction/type :cannot-cast-spells
                  :effect/target :self}
          db' (effects/execute-effect db :player-1 effect source-id)]
      (is (= 1 (count (grants/get-player-grants db' :player-1)))
          "Should create grant on controller (self)"))))

;; Test 5: invalid target is no-op
(deftest add-restriction-invalid-target-noop-test
  (testing "returns db unchanged when target player doesn't exist"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          source-id (random-uuid)
          ;; Spell targets non-existent player
          effect {:effect/type :add-restriction
                  :restriction/type :cannot-cast-spells
                  :effect/target :nonexistent-player}
          db' (effects/execute-effect db :player-1 effect source-id)]
      (is (= db db')
          "Should be no-op for invalid target"))))
```

Helper function needed:
```clojure
(defn add-spell-with-target
  "Create a spell object with target stored in :object/targets.
   Returns [object-id updated-db]."
  [db controller-id object-id target-player]
  (let [player-eid (q/get-player-eid db controller-id)
        conn (d/conn-from-db db)]
    (d/transact! conn [{:object/id object-id
                        :object/zone :stack
                        :object/owner player-eid
                        :object/controller player-eid
                        :object/targets {:player target-player}}])
    [object-id @conn]))
```

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] effects.cljs - Require grants namespace (already required at line 9)
- [ ] effects.cljs - Add defmethod for :add-restriction effect type:
      ```clojure
      (defmethod execute-effect-impl :add-restriction
        ;; Add a restriction grant to a target player.
        ;;
        ;; Effect keys:
        ;;   :restriction/type - Type of restriction (:cannot-cast-spells, :cannot-attack)
        ;;   :effect/target - Target player resolution:
        ;;       :targeted-player - Look up :player in source object's :object/targets
        ;;       :self - Use player-id (effect controller)
        ;;       :opponent - Use opponent of player-id
        ;;       keyword - Direct player ID
        ;;
        ;; Grant expires at end of current turn (cleanup phase).
        ;;
        ;; Handles edge cases:
        ;;   - Invalid target: no-op (returns db unchanged)
        ;;   - Missing source object: falls back to player-id as target
        ;;   - Missing game turn: defaults to turn 1
        [db player-id effect source-object-id]
        (let [;; Resolve target player
              target-raw (:effect/target effect)
              source-obj (when source-object-id (q/get-object db source-object-id))
              target-player (case target-raw
                              :targeted-player (get-in source-obj [:object/targets :player] player-id)
                              :self player-id
                              :opponent (q/get-opponent-id db player-id)
                              ;; Default: use as-is or fall back to player-id
                              (or target-raw player-id))
              ;; Get current turn for expiration
              game (q/get-game-state db)
              current-turn (or (:game/turn game) 1)]
          ;; Guard: invalid player is no-op
          (if (q/get-player-eid db target-player)
            (let [grant {:grant/id (random-uuid)
                         :grant/type :restriction
                         :grant/source source-object-id
                         :grant/expires {:expires/turn current-turn :expires/phase :cleanup}
                         :grant/data {:restriction/type (:restriction/type effect)}}]
              (grants/add-player-grant db target-player grant))
            db)))
      ```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] :add-restriction effect type implemented in effects.cljs
- [ ] 5+ tests pass in effects_test.cljs for restriction effects
- [ ] Creates :restriction grant on target player with correct data
- [ ] Grant expires at current turn, cleanup phase
- [ ] Supports :cannot-cast-spells and :cannot-attack restriction types
- [ ] Handles :targeted-player, :self, :opponent target resolution
- [ ] Invalid target returns db unchanged (no-op)
- [ ] `make validate` passes (lint, fmt, tests)

## Key Considerations (Edge Cases)

**Edge Case: Target player doesn't exist**
- get-player-eid returns nil for invalid player IDs
- MUST check and return db unchanged (no-op)
- Follow pattern from :lose-life effect (line 163)

**Edge Case: Source object doesn't exist**
- source-object-id may be nil for effects run outside spell context
- When nil, can't look up :object/targets
- Fall back to player-id for :targeted-player resolution
- Document this behavior

**Edge Case: Game turn not set**
- During initialization, :game/turn may be nil
- Default to turn 1 for expiration
- Use (or (:game/turn game) 1) pattern

**Edge Case: Opponent resolution**
- q/get-opponent-id exists for resolving opponent
- Verify this function exists before using

**Reference: Existing Effect Pattern**
- :mill effect (line 111-132) shows target resolution
- :lose-life effect (line 144-171) shows player validation
- All effects follow: check guards first, then execute

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoding Orim's Chant behavior (generic effect type)
- ❌ NO custom expiration logic (reuse grants/expire-grants)
- ❌ NO modifying targeting system (use existing :object/targets)
- ❌ NO throwing exceptions for invalid targets (return db unchanged)
- ❌ NO skipping player validation (always check get-player-eid)
close_reason: Closed

title: Unify selection discriminator fields (type vs effect-type)
description: Selection type is determined by two different fields used inconsistently: :selection/type (scry, pile-choice, cast-time-targeting, graveyard-return, ability-targeting) and :selection/effect-type (tutor, discard, cleanup-discard, player-target, exile-cards-cost, x-mana-cost, peek-and-select). The modal router in views/modals.cljs checks both. Should unify to a single discriminator field. Discovered during fizzle-cxa brainstorming.
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 3: Implement :exile-cards cost type
design:
## Goal
Implement the :exile-cards cost type that allows paying additional costs by exiling cards matching criteria from a zone. This enables Flash of Insight flashback cost: {1}{U} + exile X blue cards from graveyard.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context
- Completed Task 1: X cost system (can-pay?, pay-mana handle :x in mana costs)
- Completed Task 2: :peek-and-select effect (look at top X, select for hand)
- Flash of Insight flashback requires: {1}{U} + exile X blue cards from graveyard
- X value from exile count must flow to :peek-and-select effect

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing additional costs
Reference files for pattern:
- src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs:111-125 - can-pay-additional-cost? case dispatch
- src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs:128-142 - pay-additional-cost case dispatch
- src/main/fizzle/cards/deep_analysis.cljs:26-30 - flashback with :pay-life additional cost

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

**Test file:** src/test/fizzle/engine/exile_cost_test.cljs

Specific tests (with bug each catches):

```clojure
;; === can-pay-exile-cost? tests ===

(deftest test-can-pay-exile-cost-sufficient-cards
  ;; Bug caught: returns false when cards exist
  (testing "can-pay returns true when graveyard has enough blue cards"
    ;; Setup: 3 blue cards in graveyard, cost requires 2
    ;; Expect: true))

(deftest test-can-pay-exile-cost-insufficient-cards
  ;; Bug caught: allows casting with insufficient resources
  (testing "can-pay returns false when not enough matching cards"
    ;; Setup: 1 blue card in graveyard, cost requires 3
    ;; Expect: false))

(deftest test-can-pay-exile-cost-zero-count
  ;; Bug caught: crashes on X=0
  (testing "can-pay returns true for count=0 (vacuously true)"
    ;; Setup: empty graveyard, cost requires 0
    ;; Expect: true (X=0 is valid, no cards needed)))

(deftest test-can-pay-exile-cost-respects-color-criteria
  ;; Bug caught: ignores color filter, exiles any card
  (testing "can-pay filters by color - blue cards only"
    ;; Setup: 5 black cards in graveyard, cost requires 2 blue
    ;; Expect: false (no blue cards)))

(deftest test-can-pay-exile-cost-empty-graveyard
  ;; Bug caught: nil pointer on empty zone
  (testing "can-pay returns false on empty graveyard when count > 0"
    ;; Setup: empty graveyard, cost requires 1
    ;; Expect: false))

;; === pay-additional-cost tests ===

(deftest test-pay-exile-cost-moves-to-exile
  ;; Bug caught: cards go to wrong zone
  (testing "pay-exile-cost moves cards from graveyard to exile"
    ;; Setup: 3 blue cards in graveyard, exile 2
    ;; Expect: 2 cards in exile, 1 remains in graveyard))

(deftest test-pay-exile-cost-player-selection
  ;; Bug caught: auto-selects instead of prompting player
  (testing "pay-exile-cost creates selection state for player choice"
    ;; Setup: 3 blue cards in graveyard, exile 2
    ;; Expect: pending selection with 3 candidates, select 2))

(deftest test-pay-exile-cost-binds-x-value
  ;; Bug caught: X not available to effects
  (testing "exiled card count binds X for spell effects"
    ;; Setup: exile 3 blue cards
    ;; Expect: :spell/x-value = 3 on spell object))

;; === integration test ===

(deftest test-flashback-with-exile-cost-full-flow
  ;; Bug caught: mana and exile not both paid
  (testing "flashback pays mana cost AND exile cost"
    ;; Setup: Flash of Insight in graveyard, 1U available, 3 blue cards
    ;; Cast with flashback exiling 3 blue cards
    ;; Expect: mana spent, cards exiled, X=3 for effect))
```

### 3. Implementation checklist

**File: src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs**

- [ ] Add :exile-cards case to can-pay-additional-cost? (around line 115):
  ```clojure
  :exile-cards (let [zone (:cost/zone cost)          ; :graveyard
                     criteria (:cost/criteria cost)   ; {:card/colors #{:blue}}
                     required (:cost/count cost)      ; integer or :x
                     player-id (:object/controller (q/get-object db object-id))
                     available (q/query-zone-by-criteria db player-id zone criteria)]
                 (if (= required :x)
                   (pos? (count available))  ; For :x, need at least 1
                   (>= (count available) required)))
  ```

- [ ] Add :exile-cards case to pay-additional-cost (around line 132):
  ```clojure
  :exile-cards (let [zone (:cost/zone cost)
                     criteria (:cost/criteria cost)
                     required (:cost/count cost)
                     player-id (:object/controller (q/get-object db object-id))]
                 ;; For player selection: return db with selection state
                 ;; Actual exile happens after player confirms selection
                 ;; Bind X value to spell for effects
                 db)  ; Placeholder - selection handled at app-db level
  ```

**File: src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs**

- [ ] Add query-zone-by-criteria function (if not exists):
  ```clojure
  (defn query-zone-by-criteria
    "Get cards in zone matching criteria (colors, types).
     Returns vector of object-ids."
    [db player-id zone criteria]
    ...)
  ```

**File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs**

- [ ] Add build-exile-selection function for player choice
- [ ] Add execute-exile-selection function for moving cards
- [ ] Store X value on spell object (:object/x-value)

### 4. X Value Binding Flow

1. Player initiates flashback cast
2. can-cast-mode? checks mana AND exile cost payable
3. cast-spell-mode pays mana, creates exile selection
4. Player selects cards to exile
5. On confirm: cards exiled, :object/x-value set on spell
6. Spell resolves, effect reads :object/x-value for :effect/count

## Success Criteria
- [ ] can-pay-additional-cost? returns true when graveyard has N+ cards matching criteria
- [ ] can-pay-additional-cost? returns false when insufficient matching cards
- [ ] can-pay-additional-cost? handles :x count (at least 1 card available)
- [ ] can-pay-additional-cost? handles count=0 (vacuously true)
- [ ] pay-additional-cost creates selection state for player card choice
- [ ] execute-exile-selection moves selected cards to exile zone
- [ ] X value (exiled count) stored on spell object as :object/x-value
- [ ] 9 new tests pass: make test (verify test-exile output)
- [ ] All 702+ existing tests pass: make test
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Empty graveyard**
- can-pay-exile-cost? must return false when count > 0 and zone empty
- Prevent nil pointer on empty zone query
- Test: test-can-pay-exile-cost-empty-graveyard

**Edge Case: X=0 (exile 0 cards)**
- X=0 is valid (vacuously true, no cards needed)
- can-pay should return true
- No selection UI needed (skip to spell resolution)
- Test: test-can-pay-exile-cost-zero-count

**Edge Case: Exactly N cards available**
- If required=3 and exactly 3 blue cards exist, must work
- Player has no choice (all must be exiled)
- Still show selection UI (consistency with anti-pattern)

**X Value Flow:**
- Exile cost determines X (count of exiled cards)
- X must be stored on spell object before resolution
- :peek-and-select effect reads X from :object/x-value
- Alternative: pass X through effect chain

**Player Selection for Exile:**
- Player must choose which cards to exile (not random)
- Follow build-tutor-selection pattern
- Selection state: candidates = matching cards, select exactly N
- For :x count: player chooses how many (min 1)

**Mana AND Exile:**
- Flashback pays BOTH mana cost ({1}{U}) AND exile cost
- Anti-pattern: must not skip mana cost
- Test: test-flashback-with-exile-cost-full-flow

**Reference Implementation:**
- Follow :pay-life pattern in rules.cljs:116-122 and 133-140
- Follow build-tutor-selection in game.cljs for selection state

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO card-specific logic (generic :exile-cards only)
- ❌ NO hardcoding blue color (use :cost/criteria from card definition)
- ❌ NO skipping mana cost in flashback (exile is ADDITIONAL cost)
- ❌ NO auto-selecting cards to exile (player must choose)
- ❌ NO random exile selection (player agency required)
- ❌ NO unwrap/expect in cost checking (use if-let guards)
close_reason: Closed

title: Epic: Standardize selection state keys
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- All selection builders use :selection/select-count exclusively (no :selection/count)
- All selection consumers read :selection/select-count exclusively
- The legacy dual-set pattern (setting both keys) is removed
- A canonical schema docstring documents all 10 selection types and their key sets at the top of selection.cljs
- All existing selection flows (tutor, discard, scry, pile-choice, graveyard-return, exile-cards, x-mana, player-target, cast-time-targeting, cleanup-discard) continue to work identically

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Zero occurrences of :selection/count in src/ (only :selection/select-count)
- [ ] Zero occurrences of :selection/count in test/ (only :selection/select-count)
- [ ] selection.cljs has top-level docstring documenting canonical selection state shapes
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + all tests)
- [ ] No regressions in selection flows

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO backwards-compatibility shims reading both keys (clean rename, not deprecation layer)
- NO changes to :selection/type or :selection/effect-type discriminators (separate ticket)
- NO boolean key renames like :allow-fail-to-find to :allow-fail-to-find? (separate ticket)
- NO structural changes to selection flow (pure key rename + docs)
- NO spec/schema validation additions (out of scope - future Datascript migration)

## Approach
Direct mechanical rename of :selection/count to :selection/select-count across all builders, consumers, views, subscriptions, and tests. Remove the dual-set pattern in tutor builder (selection.cljs lines 130-131). Add canonical schema docstring to selection.cljs namespace.

## Architecture
Files affected:
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection.cljs - Builders and confirmers (~40 locations)
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs - build-cleanup-discard-selection, confirm-selection
- src/main/fizzle/subs/game.cljs - Subscriptions reading selection state
- src/main/fizzle/views/modals.cljs - Modal components reading count
- src/test/fizzle/events/*_test.cljs - Test assertions and setup

## Design Rationale
### Problem
:selection/count and :selection/select-count represent the same concept (how many items to select) but are used inconsistently. The tutor builder sets BOTH for backwards compatibility (lines 130-131 in selection.cljs). This creates confusion and bug risk, and blocks the P1 Datascript migration (fizzle-4jc) which needs a clean selection schema.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- selection.cljs:130-131 - Explicit comment showing dual-set for backwards compat
- :selection/count used by: discard, graveyard-return, cleanup-discard builders
- :selection/select-count used by: tutor multi-select, peek-and-select, exile-cards-cost builders
- 35 unique :selection/* keys across 10 selection types
- Views in modals.cljs route via :selection/type OR :selection/effect-type (dual discriminator - separate issue)

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Direct rename (CHOSEN)
Rename :selection/count to :selection/select-count everywhere. Remove dual-set pattern.
Chosen because: Mechanical, verifiable, no legacy code left behind.

#### 2. Deprecation layer (REJECTED)
Add reader helper checking both keys, gradually migrate.
REJECTED BECAUSE: Adds indirection for a purely internal key. More code, not less.
DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS: Selection keys become part of a public API.

#### 3. Spec-driven migration (REJECTED)
Define clojure.spec for selection maps, use spec to enforce.
REJECTED BECAUSE: Overkill for a key rename. Spec adds dependency for no proportional benefit.
DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS: Adding spec validation as a general pattern.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- :selection/count to :selection/select-count rename in all files
- Remove dual-set pattern in tutor builder
- Add canonical schema docstring to selection.cljs
- Update all tests

**Out of scope (separate tickets):**
- Boolean naming: :allow-fail-to-find to :allow-fail-to-find?
- Discriminator unification: :selection/type vs :selection/effect-type
- Datascript migration of selection state (fizzle-4jc)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Which count key? | :selection/select-count | More descriptive, used by newer code |
| Scope? | Keys only, ticket others | Keeps blast radius small |
| Documentation? | Yes, docstring in selection.cljs | Documents canonical key sets |
| Approach? | Direct rename | No legacy code, no deprecation layer |

### Research Deep-Dives
- :selection/count users: discard, graveyard-return, cleanup-discard builders, views/modals
- :selection/select-count users: tutor multi-select, peek-and-select, exile-cards-cost
- Both set simultaneously in tutor builder (selection.cljs:130-131) for backwards compat
- 10 selection types, 9 builders in selection.cljs + 1 in game.cljs (cleanup-discard)

### Dead-End Paths
- :selection/shuffle vs :selection/shuffle? conflict from original ticket - DOES NOT EXIST. Only the ? variant exists.

### Open Concerns
- Dual discriminator (:selection/type vs :selection/effect-type) needs its own ticket
- Boolean naming inconsistency needs its own ticket
close_reason: All success criteria met: zero :selection/count occurrences, dual-set pattern removed, schema docstring added, all 831 tests pass. Unblocks fizzle-4jc (Datascript migration).

title: Card Batch 2: Tutor, Triggers, Flashback Infrastructure
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

1. **Tutor System**: Implement :tutor effect type with library search, criteria filtering, selection modal, and fail-to-find option
2. **Event System Migration**: Migrate City of Brass :becomes-tapped trigger to event system, remove old scanning code (fire-matching-triggers, fire-and-resolve-triggers)
3. **Land-Entered Trigger**: Add :land-entered event type for City of Traitors sacrifice trigger
4. **Flashback System**: Implement graveyard casting with alternate cost and exile-on-resolution
5. **Cards**: Merchant Scroll, Polluted Delta, City of Traitors, Deep Analysis

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] Merchant Scroll tutors for blue instant with selection modal and fail-to-find
- [ ] Polluted Delta fetches Island/Swamp subtype to battlefield, sacrifices self
- [ ] City of Traitors produces {C}{C}, sacrifices when another land enters
- [ ] Deep Analysis castable from graveyard with flashback cost, exiles on resolution
- [ ] City of Brass trigger uses event system (no scanning)
- [ ] fire-matching-triggers and fire-and-resolve-triggers removed from codebase
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO scanning-based triggers (tech debt: event system is the pattern, scanning doesn't work for Gaea's Blessing-style cards)
- ❌ NO auto-select for tutors (rules: fail-to-find must always be available, hidden information matters)
- ❌ NO new trigger code using check-trigger multimethod (consistency: use trigger-registry + dispatch-event)
- ❌ NO flashback cards going to graveyard on resolution (rules: flashback exiles)
- ❌ NO leaving dead code (cleanup: if it's not used, delete it)

## Approach

**Phase 1: Event System Cleanup**
- Migrate City of Brass :becomes-tapped to event system
- Add :permanent-tapped event dispatch to activate-mana-ability
- Register CoB trigger on ETB, unregister on leave
- Delete fire-matching-triggers, fire-and-resolve-triggers, check-trigger :becomes-tapped
- Update/delete affected tests

**Phase 2: Tutor Infrastructure**
- Add :tutor effect type to effects.cljs
- Add library query function with criteria matching (types, colors, subtypes)
- Add tutor selection modal (reuse Careful Study pattern)
- Include fail-to-find option in all tutor selections
- Implement Merchant Scroll as validation

**Phase 3: Land-Entered Trigger**
- Add land-entered-event to events.cljs
- Dispatch event in play-land
- Add :exclude-self filter support to matches-filter?
- Implement City of Traitors

**Phase 4: Fetchland**
- Implement Polluted Delta using tutor + sacrifice ability
- Add :pay-life cost type if not present
- Tutor to battlefield (not hand)

**Phase 5: Flashback**
- Add :card/flashback-cost property
- Extend can-cast? to check graveyard zone
- Track cast-via-flashback for exile-on-resolution
- Implement Deep Analysis

## Architecture

**Event System (existing, to be used consistently):**
- events.cljs: Event creation helpers
- trigger_registry.cljs: Register/unregister triggers
- trigger_dispatch.cljs: dispatch-event matching and execution

**New Components:**
- effects.cljs: Add :tutor effect type
- queries.cljs: Add query-library-by-criteria
- rules.cljs: Extend can-cast? for flashback
- views/selection.cljs: Extend modal for tutor selection

**Card Definitions (iggy_pop.cljs):**
- merchant-scroll, polluted-delta, city-of-traitors, deep-analysis

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Iggy Pop deck needs tutor effects (Merchant Scroll, fetchlands), land sacrifice triggers (City of Traitors), and flashback (Deep Analysis). Current codebase has partial trigger system migration leaving dead code and inconsistent patterns.

### Research Findings

**Codebase:**
- trigger_registry.cljs - Event-based trigger registration exists
- trigger_dispatch.cljs - dispatch-event with filter matching exists
- triggers.cljs:220-310 - Old fire-matching-triggers still used by City of Brass
- game.cljs:529 - activate-mana-ability calls old scanning system
- events.cljs - Has permanent-tapped-event, zone-change-event helpers

**Patterns:**
- Selection modal exists (Careful Study discard)
- Sacrifice-self cost exists (Lotus Petal, LED)
- Pay-life may need implementation for fetchlands

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Event-Based Triggers for All Cards ✓

**What it is:** Use trigger_registry + dispatch-event for all card triggers. Migrate City of Brass, implement new cards with same pattern.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed trigger_dispatch.cljs - clean dispatch with filter matching
- Reviewed trigger_registry.cljs - register/unregister pattern
- Confirmed turn-based actions already use this system

**Pros:**
- Consistent architecture
- Works for zone-change triggers (Gaea's Blessing)
- Clean event-driven model

**Cons:**
- Requires migrating City of Brass
- Must delete old code

**Chosen because:** Scanning won't work for cards that trigger on zone changes (mill triggers). Event system is the correct long-term architecture.

#### 2. Keep Dual Systems ❌

**What it is:** Leave City of Brass on old system, use new system for new cards.

**Why we looked at this:** Less scope, faster delivery.

**Investigation:**
- Would leave dead code
- Inconsistent patterns for similar triggers
- Tech debt compounds

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Tech debt, inconsistency, user explicitly wants cleanup.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Never - this is a cleanup requirement.

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- 4 cards: Merchant Scroll, Polluted Delta, City of Traitors, Deep Analysis
- Tutor effect with selection modal
- Land-entered trigger type
- Flashback mechanic
- City of Brass migration
- Dead code removal

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Intuition (pile selection UI - more complex)
- Ill-Gotten Gains (multi-player selection)
- Cunning Wish (sideboard zone)
- Other fetchlands (same pattern as Polluted Delta)

### Open Questions
- Should tutor shuffle happen before or after selection confirmed? (After - selection might fail)
- Should flashback cost show in graveyard view? (Yes - display alternate cost)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Card priority | All 4 cards | Full batch scope |
| Tutor UI | Modal with filtered list | Reuse Careful Study pattern |
| City of Traitors trigger | When you play another land | :land-entered event + exclude-self filter |
| Flashback UI | Full implementation | Graveyard casting, exile on resolution |
| Fetch targets | Match subtypes | Query by :card/subtypes |
| Auto-select tutors | No - fail to find required | Always show modal with Find Nothing option |
| Migrate City of Brass | Yes | Clean up tech debt, remove old code |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Trigger System Architecture
**Question explored:** Which trigger system to use?
**Sources consulted:**
- trigger_registry.cljs - event registration pattern
- trigger_dispatch.cljs - event dispatch with filtering
- triggers.cljs - old scanning approach
- game.cljs:529 - where old system is called

**Findings:**
- Old system scans battlefield after state change
- New system dispatches events, registered triggers match
- New system required for zone-change triggers (Gaea's Blessing)
- City of Brass is only card using old system

**Conclusion:** Migrate to event system, delete old code

### Dead-End Paths

#### Scanning-Based Triggers
**Why explored:** Already implemented, seemed to work
**Investigation:**
- User pointed out Gaea's Blessing won't work with scanning
- Scanning checks state diff, doesn't know what caused change
- Zone-change triggers need event context

**Why abandoned:** Doesn't support zone-change triggers, event system already exists

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'Code for scan based system still exists' → Full cleanup, remove dead code
- 'Gaea's Blessing compatibility' → Event system handles zone-change triggers correctly
notes:
## Epic Closure Summary

### Delivered (6/7 tasks)
- ✅ **Event System Migration** (fizzle-h4l): City of Brass trigger migrated to event system, old scanning code removed
- ✅ **Tutor System** (fizzle-pk2): Implemented :tutor effect type with selection modal, fail-to-find, shuffle - Merchant Scroll working
- ✅ **Land-Entered Trigger** (fizzle-3al): Added :land-entered event type with exclude-self filter - City of Traitors working  
- ✅ **Fetchland** (fizzle-7ef): Implemented Polluted Delta with fetch-to-battlefield
- ✅ **Pay-Life Cost** (fizzle-pxz): Added :pay-life cost type for Polluted Delta
- ✅ **Activated Abilities on Stack** (fizzle-6gi): Polluted Delta (and all activated abilities) now use the stack

### Deferred to Future Epic
- ⏸️ **Flashback System** (fizzle-2q8): Well-documented implementation plan for Deep Analysis and generic flashback mechanic

### Why Scope Reduced
Original epic scope was ambitious. The activated abilities stack work (fizzle-6gi) was emergent complexity discovered during fetchland implementation. Flashback requires significant rules engine changes (graveyard casting, life costs, exile-on-resolution) that deserve their own focused epic.

### Success Criteria Status
- ✅ Merchant Scroll tutors for blue instant with selection modal and fail-to-find
- ✅ Polluted Delta fetches Island/Swamp subtype to battlefield, sacrifices self  
- ✅ City of Traitors produces {C}{C}, sacrifices when another land enters
- ⏸️ Deep Analysis - deferred (flashback system not implemented)
- ✅ City of Brass trigger uses event system (no scanning)
- ✅ fire-matching-triggers and fire-and-resolve-triggers removed from codebase
- ✅ All tests passing
- ✅ make validate passes
close_reason: Epic delivered 6/7 planned features: tutor system, land-entered triggers, fetchland, pay-life costs, activated abilities stack integration, and event system migration. Flashback system (fizzle-2q8) deferred to future epic - well-documented for pickup.

title: Implement mill effect and Brain Freeze card
design:
## Goal

Implement the mill effect type and Brain Freeze card definition to enable storm-mill combos.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Context

Completed tasks:
- fizzle-21w: Trigger infrastructure (create-trigger, resolve-trigger, stack integration)
- fizzle-1fm: Storm keyword (copies created on cast, resolve before original)

Now need: Mill effect + Brain Freeze card to test storm-mill combo.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code

Reference implementations:
- src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:31-33 - execute-effect :add-mana pattern (follow this)
- src/main/fizzle/engine/zones.cljs:12-33 - move-to-zone function (use for moving cards)
- src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Dark Ritual card definition pattern
- src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs:79-90 - get-objects-in-zone pattern
- src/main/fizzle/db/schema.cljs:38 - :object/position for library ordering

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

Test file: src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs (add to existing file)

Test cases with bug-catching rationale:
- [ ] test-mill-effect-moves-cards: Mill 3 moves top 3 from library to graveyard (catches: effect not working at all)
- [ ] test-mill-effect-respects-position: Mill takes cards with LOWEST :object/position (catches: wrong order - bottom instead of top)
- [ ] test-mill-effect-partial-library: Mill 5 with 2 cards in library moves 2 (catches: crash on partial library)
- [ ] test-mill-effect-empty-library: Mill on empty library is no-op, returns unchanged db (catches: crash on empty library)
- [ ] test-mill-effect-preserves-order: First card milled (lowest position) goes to graveyard first (catches: flashback order issues later)
- [ ] test-mill-effect-targets-opponent: Mill with :effect/target :opponent mills opponent, not caster (catches: targeting wrong player)

Note: Brain Freeze card definition tests are deferred to integration testing - card definitions are pure data and testing their structure is tautological. Instead, we test the behavior when used.

### 3. Implementation checklist

Add query to src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs:
- [ ] get-top-n-library [db player-id n]
  - Query objects where :object/owner = player and :object/zone = :library
  - Sort by :object/position ascending (lowest = top of library)
  - Take first n (or all if fewer than n)
  - Return vector of object-ids (just the :object/id values, not full objects)
  - Handle empty library: return []
  - Handle nil player: return nil

Example implementation:
```clojure
(defn get-top-n-library
  "Get the top N cards from a player's library by position.
   Returns vector of object-ids (lowest position = top).
   Returns [] if library has fewer than N cards.
   Returns nil if player doesn't exist."
  [db player-id n]
  (let [player-eid (get-player-eid db player-id)]
    (when player-eid
      (->> (d/q '[:find ?oid ?pos
                  :in $ ?owner
                  :where [?obj :object/owner ?owner]
                         [?obj :object/zone :library]
                         [?obj :object/id ?oid]
                         [?obj :object/position ?pos]]
                db player-eid)
           (sort-by second)  ; sort by position ascending
           (take n)
           (mapv first)))))  ; extract just object-ids
```

Add to src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:
- [ ] Add require for zones: [fizzle.engine.zones :as zones]
- [ ] Add require for queries: [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
- [ ] defmethod execute-effect :mill [db player-id effect]
  - Get target-player: (get effect :effect/target player-id) - defaults to caster if not specified
  - If target is :opponent, resolve to actual opponent player-id (query game state)
  - Get count from (:effect/amount effect)
  - Get top N object-ids via (q/get-top-n-library db target-player count)
  - Reduce over object-ids, calling (zones/move-to-zone db oid :graveyard) for each
  - Return final db

Example implementation:
```clojure
(defmethod execute-effect :mill
  [db player-id effect]
  (let [target (get effect :effect/target player-id)
        target-player (if (= target :opponent)
                        (q/get-opponent-id db player-id)
                        target)
        amount (:effect/amount effect)
        cards-to-mill (q/get-top-n-library db target-player amount)]
    (reduce (fn [db' oid]
              (zones/move-to-zone db' oid :graveyard))
            db
            cards-to-mill)))
```

- [ ] Add helper query if needed: get-opponent-id [db player-id]
  - Find player where :player/is-opponent is opposite of given player
  - Or simpler: find player where :player/id != player-id

Add to src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs:
- [ ] def brain-freeze
```clojure
(def brain-freeze
  {:card/id :brain-freeze
   :card/name "Brain Freeze"
   :card/cmc 2
   :card/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :blue 1}
   :card/colors #{:blue}
   :card/types #{:instant}
   :card/keywords #{:storm}
   :card/text "Target player mills 3. Storm."
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :mill
                   :effect/amount 3
                   :effect/target :opponent}]})
```
- [ ] Update all-cards to include brain-freeze: (def all-cards [dark-ritual brain-freeze])

## Success Criteria

- [ ] get-top-n-library query returns cards sorted by :object/position (lowest first)
- [ ] get-top-n-library handles empty library (returns [])
- [ ] get-top-n-library handles partial library (returns all available)
- [ ] Mill effect moves cards from library to graveyard zone
- [ ] Mill effect targets correct player (:opponent resolves to opponent)
- [ ] Brain Freeze card defined with :storm keyword and :mill effect
- [ ] 6 mill effect tests pass (see test list above)
- [ ] All existing tests still pass: make test (60 tests)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass: make validate

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Empty Library**
- get-top-n-library returns [] for empty library
- Mill effect reduce over empty list is no-op (returns db unchanged)
- Test: test-mill-effect-empty-library verifies this

**Edge Case: Partial Library**
- get-top-n-library uses (take n) which handles n > available
- Mill 5 with 2 cards in library moves 2, not crash
- Test: test-mill-effect-partial-library verifies this

**Edge Case: Library Ordering**
- :object/position determines top of library (lowest = top)
- MUST sort ascending, not descending
- Test: test-mill-effect-respects-position verifies correct order

**Target Resolution**
- :effect/target :opponent is a symbolic reference
- Must resolve to actual player-id at execution time
- If target doesn't exist, reduce over [] is no-op (safe)

**Graveyard Ordering**
- Cards milled go to graveyard in order (first milled = "bottom" of graveyard)
- Important for flashback, dredge, etc. later
- Test: test-mill-effect-preserves-order verifies this

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow execute-effect :add-mana pattern (effects.cljs:31-33)
- Follow move-to-zone pattern (zones.cljs:12-33)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO crashing on empty/partial library (handle gracefully with take/reduce)
- ❌ NO hardcoded Brain Freeze logic in engine (card is data, engine interprets)
- ❌ NO milling more cards than exist in library (use take n, not nth)
- ❌ NO assuming library order without :object/position (always sort by position)
- ❌ NO milling wrong player (must resolve :opponent to actual player-id)
- ❌ NO ignoring graveyard order (matters for flashback/dredge later)
close_reason: Implemented mill effect and Brain Freeze card with tests

title: Task 2: Implement pile choice selection phase
design:
## Goal
Implement the pile choice selection phase that triggers after multi-select tutor confirmation when :selection/pile-choice is present. Player chooses which card(s) go to hand, rest go to graveyard.

## Context
Completed fizzle-kd6: Multi-select tutor selection now works. Selection state includes :selection/pile-choice from the tutor effect. Need to chain pile choice after tutor confirmation.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study current tutor confirmation flow
- events/game.cljs ::confirm-tutor-selection - currently executes tutor then clears selection
- CRITICAL CHANGE: When pile-choice present, skip execute-tutor-selection (don't move cards yet)
- Instead: Build pile-choice selection with the selected card IDs
- Cards remain in library until pile choice confirms

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Create tests in src/test/fizzle/events/pile_choice_test.cljs:

```clojure
;; Test: build-pile-choice-selection creates correct state
;; Setup: 3 card IDs, pile-choice config {:hand 1 :graveyard :rest}
;; Action: (build-pile-choice-selection card-ids pile-choice player-id spell-id)
;; Assert:
;;   - (:selection/type result) = :pile-choice
;;   - (:selection/candidates result) = set of 3 card IDs
;;   - (:selection/hand-count result) = 1
;;   - (:selection/player-id result) = player-id
;;   - (:selection/allow-random result) = true
;;   - (:selection/selected result) = #{}

;; Test: execute-pile-choice moves 1 to hand, 2 to graveyard
;; Setup: db with 3 cards in library, selection with hand-selected = #{card-1}
;; Action: (execute-pile-choice db selection)
;; Assert:
;;   - card-1 zone = :hand
;;   - card-2 zone = :graveyard
;;   - card-3 zone = :graveyard
;;   - library count unchanged (cards came from library)
;;   - NO shuffle (verify library order preserved)

;; Test: confirm-tutor-selection chains to pile-choice when present
;; Setup: app-db with pending tutor selection, 3 cards selected, pile-choice in selection
;; Action: dispatch ::confirm-tutor-selection
;; Assert:
;;   - :game/pending-selection now has :selection/type :pile-choice
;;   - Cards still in library (not moved to hand yet)
;;   - Spell NOT moved to graveyard yet (still on stack)

;; Test: confirm-tutor-selection without pile-choice works normally
;; Setup: app-db with pending tutor selection, 1 card selected, NO pile-choice
;; Action: dispatch ::confirm-tutor-selection
;; Assert:
;;   - Card moved to hand
;;   - Library shuffled
;;   - :game/pending-selection cleared
;;   - Spell moved to graveyard

;; Test: select-random picks exactly hand-count cards
;; Setup: pile-choice selection with 3 candidates, hand-count 1
;; Action: dispatch ::select-random-pile-choice
;; Assert:
;;   - (:selection/selected) has exactly 1 element
;;   - Selected element is in candidates
;;   - Selection NOT auto-confirmed (can still change)

;; Test: single-card pile-choice auto-selects
;; Setup: 1 card ID, pile-choice {:hand 1 :graveyard :rest}
;; Action: build-pile-choice-selection
;; Assert:
;;   - (:selection/selected result) = #{single-card-id}
;;   - Still requires confirm (UI shows pre-selected state)
```

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] events/game.cljs - Add build-pile-choice-selection function:
      ```clojure
      (defn build-pile-choice-selection
        [card-ids pile-choice player-id spell-id remaining-effects]
        (let [hand-count (:hand pile-choice)
              candidates (set card-ids)
              ;; Auto-select if only 1 card (per epic open question)
              auto-selected (if (= 1 (count candidates))
                              candidates
                              #{})]
          {:selection/type :pile-choice
           :selection/candidates candidates
           :selection/hand-count hand-count
           :selection/selected auto-selected
           :selection/player-id player-id
           :selection/spell-id spell-id
           :selection/remaining-effects remaining-effects
           :selection/allow-random true}))
      ```
- [ ] events/game.cljs - Add execute-pile-choice function:
      ```clojure
      (defn execute-pile-choice
        [db selection]
        (let [hand-selected (:selection/selected selection)
              all-candidates (:selection/candidates selection)
              graveyard-cards (clojure.set/difference all-candidates hand-selected)]
          ;; Move hand cards from library to hand
          (let [db-after-hand (reduce #(zones/move-to-zone %1 %2 :hand)
                                      db hand-selected)
                ;; Move remaining to graveyard
                db-after-graveyard (reduce #(zones/move-to-zone %1 %2 :graveyard)
                                           db-after-hand graveyard-cards)]
            db-after-graveyard)))
      ```
- [ ] events/game.cljs - Modify ::confirm-tutor-selection:
      - Check if :selection/pile-choice present AND cards were selected
      - If pile-choice: build-pile-choice-selection, DON'T execute-tutor-selection
      - Keep spell on stack (don't move to graveyard yet)
      - Store new selection in :game/pending-selection
      - If no pile-choice: existing behavior (execute-tutor, shuffle, clear)
- [ ] events/game.cljs - Add ::confirm-pile-choice-selection event:
      ```clojure
      (rf/reg-event-db
        ::confirm-pile-choice-selection
        (fn [db _]
          (let [selection (:game/pending-selection db)
                hand-count (:selection/hand-count selection)
                selected (:selection/selected selection)
                candidates (:selection/candidates selection)
                player-id (:selection/player-id selection)
                spell-id (:selection/spell-id selection)
                remaining-effects (:selection/remaining-effects selection)
                game-db (:game/db db)]
            ;; Validate: correct number selected and all are candidates
            (if (and (= hand-count (count selected))
                     (every? #(contains? candidates %) selected))
              (let [db-after-pile (execute-pile-choice game-db selection)
                    ;; Shuffle library AFTER pile choice (tutor requirement)
                    db-after-shuffle (zones/shuffle-library db-after-pile player-id)
                    ;; Execute remaining effects
                    db-after-effects (reduce (fn [d e] (effects/execute-effect d player-id e))
                                             db-after-shuffle
                                             (or remaining-effects []))
                    ;; Move spell to graveyard (or exile for flashback)
                    spell-obj (queries/get-object db-after-effects spell-id)
                    cast-mode (:object/cast-mode spell-obj)
                    destination (or (:mode/on-resolve cast-mode) :graveyard)
                    db-final (zones/move-to-zone db-after-effects spell-id destination)]
                (-> db
                    (assoc :game/db db-final)
                    (dissoc :game/pending-selection)))
              ;; Invalid - do nothing
              db))))
      ```
- [ ] events/game.cljs - Add ::select-random-pile-choice event:
      ```clojure
      (rf/reg-event-db
        ::select-random-pile-choice
        (fn [db _]
          (let [selection (:game/pending-selection db)
                candidates (vec (:selection/candidates selection))
                hand-count (:selection/hand-count selection)
                random-selected (set (take hand-count (shuffle candidates)))]
            (assoc-in db [:game/pending-selection :selection/selected] random-selected))))
      ```

### 4. Verification
- Run: make test (all tests pass)
- Run: make validate (lint, format, tests)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] build-pile-choice-selection returns map with :selection/type :pile-choice
- [ ] :selection/candidates contains exactly the tutored card IDs
- [ ] :selection/hand-count matches pile-choice config (:hand value)
- [ ] :selection/allow-random = true for Random button
- [ ] Single-card selection auto-populates :selection/selected
- [ ] execute-pile-choice moves hand-selected to :hand zone
- [ ] execute-pile-choice moves remaining to :graveyard zone
- [ ] ::confirm-tutor-selection chains to pile-choice when :selection/pile-choice present
- [ ] ::confirm-tutor-selection preserves backwards compat (no pile-choice = normal flow)
- [ ] ::confirm-pile-choice-selection validates count and shuffles library
- [ ] ::select-random-pile-choice selects exactly hand-count cards randomly
- [ ] 6+ unit tests pass with specific assertions
- [ ] make validate passes (lint, format, tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**CRITICAL: Card location during pile choice**
- Cards stay in library during tutor selection phase
- Cards stay in library during pile choice selection phase
- Only on pile-choice confirm do cards move to hand/graveyard
- Shuffle happens AFTER pile choice confirm, not during tutor confirm

**Edge Case: Empty selection after tutor (fail-to-find)**
- If fail-to-find (0 cards selected), no pile choice phase
- ::confirm-tutor-selection should use existing flow (just shuffle)

**Edge Case: Single card selected**
- If only 1 card from tutor, pile choice triggers with auto-selected card
- User sees confirmation UI but choice is predetermined
- Still requires explicit confirm click

**Edge Case: hand-count > selected cards**
- If pile-choice {:hand 2} but only 1 card selected
- All cards go to hand, none to graveyard
- Should work but log warning (unexpected state)

**Edge Case: Spell lifecycle**
- Spell stays on stack until pile-choice confirms
- Only after confirm: spell moves to graveyard/exile
- This is correct MTG rules (spell resolves after all effects)

**Integration: Shuffle timing**
- Tutor confirm does NOT shuffle when pile-choice present
- Pile-choice confirm shuffles library
- This maintains single shuffle per Intuition resolution

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO shuffling during pile-choice (shuffle only on final confirm)
- ❌ NO skipping pile-choice UI when 1 card (show UI with auto-selected)
- ❌ NO auto-confirming random selection (let user review before confirm)
- ❌ NO moving cards to hand during tutor confirm when pile-choice present
- ❌ NO moving spell to graveyard until pile-choice complete
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 3: Targeting System Foundation
description:
## Goal
Create the targeting system infrastructure for cast-time target declaration and validation.

## Context
Grant System (Phase 1-2) is complete. Now we need targeting for:
- Recoup targeting sorceries in graveyard
- Deep Analysis targeting players
- Cephalid Coliseum ability targeting players

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Update schema for target storage
- Change :object/targets from ref collection to map
- Map format: target-ref keyword → target-id (UUID or player-id)

### Step 2: Create engine/targeting.cljs with core functions
- get-targeting-requirements: Extract targeting from card/ability
- find-valid-targets: Find objects/players matching criteria
- has-valid-targets?: Check if any valid target exists
- target-still-legal?: Check if chosen target is still valid
- all-targets-legal?: Check all targets on resolution

### Step 3: Write tests for each function (TDD)
- Test target finding in different zones
- Test player targeting
- Test legality checking when target moves zones

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Schema updated for map-based target storage
- [ ] All 5 targeting functions implemented
- [ ] Tests written and passing for each function
- [ ] Functions handle both object and player targets

## Anti-Patterns
- Do NOT modify casting flow yet (Phase 4 will do that)
- Do NOT implement fizzle logic yet (Phase 4 will do that)
design:
## Goal
Create the targeting system infrastructure for cast-time target declaration and validation.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Context
Grant System (Phase 1-2) is complete. Now we need targeting for:
- Recoup targeting sorceries in graveyard
- Deep Analysis targeting players
- Cephalid Coliseum ability targeting players

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Update schema for target storage (TDD)
**File:** src/main/fizzle/db/schema.cljs

Change :object/targets from:
\`\`\`clojure
:object/targets {:db/cardinality :db.cardinality/many
                 :db/valueType :db.type/ref}
\`\`\`

To:
\`\`\`clojure
:object/targets {}  ; Map of target-ref keyword → target-id
\`\`\`

**Example stored value:**
\`\`\`clojure
{:graveyard-sorcery #uuid "abc-123"
 :player :player-1}
\`\`\`

### Step 2: Create engine/targeting.cljs with functions (TDD)
**File:** src/main/fizzle/engine/targeting.cljs
**Test file:** src/test/fizzle/engine/targeting_test.cljs

**Function 1: get-targeting-requirements**
\`\`\`clojure
(defn get-targeting-requirements
  "Extract targeting requirements from card or ability.
   Returns vector of target requirement maps, or empty vector if no targeting."
  [card-or-ability]
  ;; Check :card/targeting or :ability/targeting
  ;; Return vector of {:target/id :target/type :target/zone ...} maps
  )
\`\`\`

**Function 2: find-valid-targets**
\`\`\`clojure
(defn find-valid-targets
  "Find all objects/players that are valid targets for a requirement.
   Returns vector of valid target IDs (object UUIDs or player keywords)."
  [db player-id target-requirement]
  ;; For :target/type :object - query zone for matching objects
  ;; For :target/type :player - return valid player options
  )
\`\`\`

**Function 3: has-valid-targets?**
\`\`\`clojure
(defn has-valid-targets?
  "Check if at least one valid target exists for EACH required target.
   Returns true only if ALL required targets have valid options."
  [db player-id card-or-ability]
  ;; Get requirements, check each has at least one valid target
  )
\`\`\`

**Function 4: target-still-legal?**
\`\`\`clojure
(defn target-still-legal?
  "Check if a chosen target is still legal (zone, attributes match).
   Used on resolution to check if target moved/changed."
  [db target-id target-requirement]
  ;; Check target still in correct zone
  ;; Check target still matches criteria
  )
\`\`\`

**Function 5: all-targets-legal?**
\`\`\`clojure
(defn all-targets-legal?
  "Check if ALL chosen targets on an object are still legal.
   Used on resolution to determine if spell fizzles."
  [db object-id]
  ;; Get object's stored targets and targeting requirements
  ;; Check each target against its requirement
  )
\`\`\`

### Step 3: Target requirement structure
Define the target requirement map format:
\`\`\`clojure
{:target/id       :graveyard-sorcery  ; Identifier for this target slot
 :target/type     :object             ; :object or :player
 :target/zone     :graveyard          ; For objects: required zone
 :target/controller :self             ; :self, :opponent, :any
 :target/criteria {:card/types #{:sorcery}}  ; Card attributes to match
 :target/required true}               ; Must have valid target to cast
\`\`\`

For player targeting:
\`\`\`clojure
{:target/id       :player
 :target/type     :player
 :target/options  [:self :opponent :any-player]
 :target/required true}
\`\`\`

## Success Criteria
